task: Examine car roofs for wear and damage, and repair defective sections, using roofing material, cement, nails, and waterproof paint.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of car roofs and hands-on repair work using tools and materials like cement, nails, and paint.

task: Paint car exteriors, interiors, and fixtures.
rationale: Painting involves physical manipulation of tools and materials, direct application to tangible surfaces requiring hands-on execution.

task: Repair car upholstery.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of upholstery materials, tools like needles, thread, and potentially sewing machines or adhesives to repair car interior.

task: Repair window sash frames, attach weather stripping and channels to frames, and replace window glass, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of window components, hand tools, and installation work on tangible objects.

task: Examine or test operation of parts or systems to ensure completeness of repairs.
rationale: Examining and testing parts/systems requires hands-on physical inspection and operation of equipment to verify repairs are complete.

task: Repair plumbing or propane gas lines, using caulking compounds and plastic or copper pipe.
rationale: Involves physical manipulation of pipes, tools, and materials to repair plumbing/gas systems.

task: Inspect recreational vehicles to diagnose problems and perform necessary adjustment, repair, or overhaul.
rationale: Physical inspection, diagnosis, adjustment, repair, and overhaul of recreational vehicles requires hands-on manipulation and operation of tools.

task: Locate and repair frayed wiring, broken connections, or incorrect wiring, using ohmmeters, soldering irons, tape, or hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of wiring and hands-on use of tools (soldering irons, ohmmeters, tape, hand tools) to repair electrical connections.

task: Confer with customers, read work orders, or examine vehicles needing repair to determine the nature and extent of damage.
rationale: Examining vehicles requires physical inspection—touching, looking at damage firsthand. Not desk review.

task: Connect electrical systems to outside power sources, and activate switches to test the operation of appliances or light fixtures.
rationale: Task involves physical operation of electrical systems, connecting cables, and manipulating switches—hands-on technical work requiring physical presence and interaction.

task: Connect water hoses to inlet pipes of plumbing systems, and test operation of toilets or sinks.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of hoses and pipes, hands-on testing of plumbing fixtures; direct tangible object handling.

task: Remove damaged exterior panels, and repair and replace structural frame members.
rationale: Physical manipulation of panels and structural frame members; removal, repair, and replacement require hands-on work with tools.

task: Open and close doors, windows, or drawers to test their operation, trimming edges to fit, as necessary.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of doors, windows, and drawers; involves hands-on testing and trimming with tools to achieve proper fit and operation.

task: Repair leaks with caulking compound or replace pipes, using pipe wrenches.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of tools (pipe wrenches) and materials (caulking compound, pipes) to repair water system infrastructure.

task: Refinish wood surfaces on cabinets, doors, moldings, or floors, using power sanders, putty, spray equipment, brushes, paints, or varnishes.
rationale: Physical manipulation of tools (sanders, spray equipment, brushes) and materials to refinish surfaces. Requires hands-on execution with tangible objects and equipment operation.

task: Reset hardware, using chisels, mallets, and screwdrivers.
rationale: Resetting hardware requires physical manipulation of tools (chisels, mallets, screwdrivers) and hands-on work with tangible objects.

task: Seal open sides of modular units to prepare them for shipment, using polyethylene sheets, nails, and hammers.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials (polyethylene sheets, nails) and operation of tools (hammers) to seal modular units.

task: Inspect or test wiring installations, assemblies, or circuits for resistance factors or for operation, and record results.
rationale: Inspecting and testing wiring requires hands-on physical interaction with electrical installations, use of testing tools/meters, and direct examination of circuits.

task: Assemble electrical or electronic systems or support structures and install components, units, subassemblies, wiring, or assembly casings, using rivets, bolts, soldering or micro-welding equipment.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of components, operation of soldering/welding equipment, and hands-on assembly work.

task: Adjust, repair, or replace electrical or electronic components to correct defects and to ensure conformance to specifications.
rationale: Repairing and replacing electrical/electronic components requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and tools to correct defects.

task: Clean parts, using cleaning solutions, air hoses, and cloths.
rationale: Physical manipulation of parts using tools (air hoses, cloths) and cleaning solutions; hands-on cleaning work.

task: Mark and tag components so that stock inventory can be tracked and identified.
rationale: Marking and tagging components requires physical manipulation of objects and labels to identify and track inventory.

task: Position, align, or adjust workpieces or electrical parts to facilitate wiring or assembly.
rationale: Positioning, aligning, and adjusting workpieces requires direct physical manipulation of tangible objects and parts.

task: Pack finished assemblies for shipment, and transport them to storage areas, using hoists or handtrucks.
rationale: Task involves physical packing, lifting, and operating equipment (hoists/handtrucks) to move tangible finished products.

task: Measure and adjust voltages to specified values to determine operational accuracy of instruments.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of test equipment and instruments to measure and adjust voltages; hands-on technical work.

task: Fabricate or form parts, coils, or structures according to specifications, using drills, calipers, cutters, or saws.
rationale: Fabricating and forming parts requires operating physical tools (drills, cutters, saws) and manipulating tangible objects according to specifications.

task: Drill or tap holes in specified equipment locations to mount control units or to provide openings for elements, wiring, or instruments.

task: Distribute materials, supplies, or subassemblies to work areas.
rationale: Task requires physically handling and moving materials, supplies, or subassemblies to designated work areas.

task: Position, align, fit, and weld parts to form complete units or subunits, following blueprints and layout specifications, and using jigs, welding torches, and hand tools.

task: Verify conformance of workpieces to specifications, using squares, rulers, and measuring tapes.

task: Tack-weld fitted parts together.

task: Lay out and examine metal stock or workpieces to be processed to ensure that specifications are met.

task: Align and fit parts according to specifications, using jacks, turnbuckles, wedges, drift pins, pry bars, and hammers.

task: Locate and mark workpiece bending and cutting lines, allowing for stock thickness, machine and welding shrinkage, and other component specifications.

task: Position or tighten braces, jacks, clamps, ropes, or bolt straps, or bolt parts in position for welding or riveting.

task: Move parts into position, manually or with hoists or cranes.
rationale: Moving parts into position requires physical manipulation of objects and operation of machinery (hoists/cranes) to execute the task.

task: Set up and operate fabricating machines, such as brakes, rolls, shears, flame cutters, grinders, and drill presses, to bend, cut, form, punch, drill, or otherwise form and assemble metal components.

task: Hammer, chip, and grind workpieces to cut, bend, and straighten metal.

task: Smooth workpiece edges and fix taps, tubes, and valves.

task: Design and construct templates and fixtures, using hand tools.
rationale: Designing and constructing templates and fixtures requires hands-on physical manipulation of objects and operation of hand tools, which is manual work.

task: Straighten warped or bent parts, using sledges, hand torches, straightening presses, or bulldozers.
rationale: Requires physical operation of tools (sledges, torches, presses, bulldozers) to manipulate tangible warped/bent parts into correct shape.

task: Mark reference points onto floors or face blocks and transpose them to workpieces, using measuring devices, squares, chalk, and soapstone.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of measuring devices, chalk, soapstone, and direct hands-on marking of workpieces.

task: Set up face blocks, jigs, and fixtures.
rationale: Setting up face blocks, jigs, and fixtures requires physical manipulation and arrangement of tangible tools and equipment.

task: Remove high spots and cut bevels, using hand files, portable grinders, and cutting torches.
rationale: Requires operation of physical tools (hand files, grinders, torches) and direct manipulation of objects to remove material.

task: Lift or move materials and finished products, using large cranes.
rationale: Operating large cranes to lift and move materials requires physical operation of machinery and direct control of load movement.

task: Heat-treat parts, using acetylene torches.
rationale: Operating acetylene torches to heat-treat parts requires hands-on operation of physical tools and direct manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Preheat workpieces to make them malleable, using hand torches or furnaces.
rationale: Preheating workpieces requires operating hand torches or furnaces (physical tools) to heat materials for malleability.

task: Install boilers, containers, and other structures.

task: Erect ladders and scaffolding to fit together large assemblies.
rationale: Erecting ladders and scaffolding requires physical manipulation and assembly of tangible structures to support work.

task: Observe, feel, taste, or otherwise examine products during and after processing to ensure conformance to standards.

task: Observe temperature, humidity, pressure gauges, and product samples and adjust controls, such as thermostats and valves, to maintain prescribed operating conditions for specific stages.

task: Operate or tend equipment that roasts, bakes, dries, or cures food items such as cocoa and coffee beans, grains, nuts, and bakery products.

task: Set temperature and time controls, light ovens, burners, driers, or roasters, and start equipment, such as conveyors, cylinders, blowers, driers, or pumps.

task: Observe flow of materials and listen for machine malfunctions, such as jamming or spillage, and notify supervisors if corrective actions fail.

task: Weigh or measure products, using scale hoppers or scale conveyors.
rationale: Operating physical machinery (scales, hoppers, conveyors) and handling products for measurement requires hands-on physical operation.

task: Take product samples during or after processing for laboratory analyses.

task: Fill or remove product from trays, carts, hoppers, or equipment, using scoops, peels, or shovels, or by hand.

task: Open valves, gates, or chutes or use shovels to load or remove products from ovens or other equipment.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of equipment controls (valves, gates, chutes) and use of tools (shovels) to load/remove products from ovens.

task: Clean equipment with steam, hot water, and hoses.
rationale: Cleaning equipment with steam, hot water, and hoses requires physical operation of tools and equipment to manipulate tangible objects.

task: Clear or dislodge blockages in bins, screens, or other equipment, using poles, brushes, or mallets.

task: Push racks or carts to transfer products to storage, cooling stations, or the next stage of processing.

task: Smooth out products in bins, pans, trays, or conveyors, using rakes or shovels.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (rakes/shovels) and direct handling of products in bins/conveyors.

task: Install equipment, such as spray units, cutting blades, or screens, using hand tools.
rationale: Installing equipment using hand tools requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and tools to assemble and secure equipment components.

task: Test products for moisture content, using moisture meters.
rationale: Requires physical operation of moisture meters on tangible products; hands-on measurement and inspection.

task: Measure and inspect machined parts to ensure conformance to product specifications.
rationale: Physical inspection requiring hands-on measurement of tangible parts using gauges or tools to verify dimensions and specifications.

task: Start machines to produce sample workpieces, and observe operations to detect machine malfunctions and to verify that machine setups conform to specifications.
rationale: Starting machines and observing physical operations to verify setup and detect malfunctions requires hands-on operation and on-site physical inspection.

task: Remove dies from machines when production runs are finished.
rationale: Removing dies from machines requires physical manipulation of equipment and tangible objects at a work site.

task: Turn handles or knobs to set pressures and depths of ram strokes and to synchronize machine operations.
rationale: Physically turning handles or knobs requires manual operation and direct physical manipulation of machine controls.

task: Repair, maintain, and replace parts on dies.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and repair of die equipment parts; hands-on maintenance and replacement work.

task: Set up, operate, or tend presses and forging machines to perform hot or cold forging by flattening, straightening, bending, cutting, piercing, or other operations to taper, shape, or form metal.

task: Position and move metal wires or workpieces through a series of dies that compress and shape stock to form die impressions.
rationale: Positioning and moving metal wires/workpieces through dies requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and operation of die-forming machinery.

task: Select, align, and bolt positioning fixtures, stops, and specified dies to rams and anvils, forging rolls, or presses and hammers.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and assembly of fixtures, dies, and equipment onto machinery using tools like wrenches for bolting.

task: Trim and compress finished forgings to specified tolerances.
rationale: Trimming and compressing forgings requires physical manipulation of metal objects and operation of machinery to achieve specified tolerances.

task: Sharpen cutting tools and drill bits, using bench grinders.

task: Adjust and correct machine set-ups to reduce thicknesses, reshape products, and eliminate product defects.
rationale: Requires hands-on physical manipulation and adjustment of machinery, tools, and direct interaction with products to shape and refine them.

task: Examine, inspect, and measure raw materials and finished products to verify conformance to specifications.
rationale: Requires physical inspection and measurement of tangible objects to verify specifications; hands-on examination of materials and products.

task: Manipulate controls and observe dial indicators to monitor, adjust, and regulate speeds of machine mechanisms.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of machine controls and hands-on observation of dial indicators to regulate speeds—direct physical execution.

task: Start operation of rolling and milling machines to flatten, temper, form, and reduce sheet metal sections and to produce steel strips.
rationale: Starting and operating physical machinery to process sheet metal requires hands-on operation of industrial equipment and machines.

task: Set distance points between rolls, guides, meters, and stops, according to specifications.
rationale: Setting distance points involves physical manipulation and positioning of mechanical components (rolls, guides, meters, stops) according to specifications, requiring hands-on mechanical adjustment.

task: Thread or feed sheets or rods through rolling mechanisms, or start and control mechanisms that automatically feed steel into rollers.
rationale: Threading/feeding physical materials through machinery and operating control mechanisms requires hands-on physical manipulation and operation of equipment.

task: Position, align, and secure arbors, spindles, coils, mandrels, dies, and slitting knives.
rationale: Positioning, aligning, and securing physical components (arbors, spindles, coils, mandrels, dies, knives) requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects and tools.

task: Fill oil cups, adjust valves, and observe gauges to control flow of metal coolants and lubricants onto workpieces.

task: Install equipment such as guides, guards, gears, cooling equipment, and rolls, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of equipment and hand tools to install guides, guards, gears, and rolls—direct manual work.

task: Signal and assist other workers to remove and position equipment, fill hoppers, and feed materials into machines.
rationale: Task requires physical positioning of equipment, filling hoppers, and material feeding—hands-on manipulation and bodily movement at worksite.

task: Activate shears and grinders to trim workpieces.
rationale: Operating physical tools (shears and grinders) to manipulate and trim workpieces is hands-on manual work.

task: Remove scratches and polish roll surfaces, using polishing stones and electric buffers.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tools (polishing stones, electric buffers) and hands-on work on tangible objects (roll surfaces).

task: Disassemble sizing mills removed from rolling lines, and sort and store parts.

task: Measure completed workpieces to verify conformance to specifications, using micrometers, gauges, calipers, templates, or rulers.

task: Examine completed workpieces for defects, such as chipped edges or marred surfaces and sort defective pieces according to types of flaws.

task: Load workpieces, plastic material, or chemical solutions into machines.

task: Test and adjust machine speeds or actions, according to product specifications, using gauges and hand tools.

task: Clean and lubricate machines.

task: Position, align, and secure workpieces against fixtures or stops on machine beds or on dies.

task: Adjust ram strokes of presses to specified lengths, using hand tools.

task: Place workpieces on cutting tables, manually or using hoists, cranes, or sledges.

task: Position guides, stops, holding blocks, or other fixtures to secure and direct workpieces, using hand tools and measuring devices.

task: Replace defective blades or wheels, using hand tools.

task: Mark identifying data on workpieces.

task: Turn controls to set cutting speeds, feed rates, or table angles for specified operations.
rationale: Turning controls requires physical manipulation of machinery controls to adjust operational parameters during cutting operations.

task: Grind out burrs or sharp edges, using portable grinders, speed lathes, or polishing jacks.

task: Sharpen dulled blades, using bench grinders, abrasive wheels, or lathes.
rationale: Involves operating physical tools (bench grinders, abrasive wheels, lathes) to manually manipulate and sharpen blades.

task: Remove housings, feed tubes, tool holders, or other accessories to replace worn or broken parts, such as springs or bushings.

task: Hone cutters with oilstones to remove nicks.

task: Select, clean, and install spacers, rubber sleeves, or cutters on arbors.

task: Preheat workpieces, using heating furnaces or hand torches.
rationale: Operating heating furnaces or hand torches to preheat workpieces involves direct physical operation of equipment and tools.

task: Inspect or measure finished workpieces to determine conformance to specifications, using measuring instruments, such as gauges or micrometers.

task: Set and adjust machine controls according to product specifications, using knowledge of machine operation.

task: Measure workpieces and lay out work, using precision measuring devices.

task: Mount and position tools in machine chucks, spindles, or other tool holding devices, using hand tools.

task: Activate machine start-up switches to grind, lap, hone, debar, shear, or cut workpieces, according to specifications.

task: Move machine controls to index workpieces, and to adjust machines for pre-selected operational settings.

task: Brush or spray lubricating compounds on workpieces, or turn valve handles and direct flow of coolant against tools and workpieces.

task: Slide spacers between buffs on spindles to set spacing.

task: Thread and hand-feed materials through machine cutters or abraders.

task: Adjust air cylinders and setting stops to set traverse lengths and feed arm strokes.

task: Repair or replace machine parts, using hand tools, or notify engineering personnel when corrective action is required.

task: Draw smelted metal samples from furnaces or kettles for analysis, and calculate types and amounts of materials needed to ensure that materials meet specifications.

task: Drain, transfer, or remove molten metal from furnaces, and place it into molds, using hoists, pumps, or ladles.

task: Operate controls to move or discharge metal workpieces from furnaces.

task: Weigh materials to be charged into furnaces, using scales.

task: Inspect furnaces and equipment to locate defects and wear.

task: Observe air and temperature gauges or metal color and fluidity, and turn fuel valves or adjust controls to maintain required temperatures.

task: Measure and visually inspect products for surface and dimension defects to ensure conformance to specifications, using precision measuring instruments.

task: Set up, operate, or tend metal or plastic molding, casting, or coremaking machines to mold or cast metal or thermoplastic parts or products.

task: Position and secure workpieces on machines, and start feeding mechanisms.

task: Turn valves and dials of machines to regulate pressure, temperature, and speed and feed rates, and to set cycle times.

task: Trim excess material from parts, using knives, and grind scrap plastic into powder for reuse.

task: Cool products after processing to prevent distortion.

task: Install dies onto machines or presses and coat dies with parting agents, according to work order specifications.

task: Mix pigments to match patients' skin coloring, according to formulas, and apply mixtures to orthotic or prosthetic devices.
