task: Adjust lenses and frames to correct alignment.
rationale: Adjusting lenses and frames requires physical manipulation of eyewear components and tools to correct alignment.

task: Mount, secure, and align finished lenses in frames or optical assemblies, using precision hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of optical components using precision hand tools to mount and align lenses—clear hands-on manual work.

task: Mount and secure lens blanks or optical lenses in holding tools or chucks of cutting, polishing, grinding, or coating machines.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of optical lenses and securing them into machine chucks/holding tools—hands-on setup work.

task: Shape lenses appropriately so that they can be inserted into frames.
rationale: Shaping lenses requires physical manipulation and cutting of tangible optical materials using specialized tools and equipment.

task: Assemble eyeglass frames and attach shields, nose pads, and temple pieces, using pliers, screwdrivers, and drills.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of eyeglass components and operation of tools (pliers, screwdrivers, drills).

task: Inspect lens blanks to detect flaws, verify smoothness of surface, and ensure thickness of coating on lenses.
rationale: Requires physical inspection—touching/examining lens blanks to detect flaws, verify surface smoothness, and inspect coating thickness. Not a desk review.

task: Clean finished lenses and eyeglasses, using cloths and solvents.
rationale: Physical manipulation of eyeglasses and lenses using cloths and solvents requires hands-on execution.

task: Select lens blanks, molds, tools, and polishing or grinding wheels, according to production specifications.
rationale: Selecting physical materials (lens blanks, molds, tools, wheels) requires direct handling and manipulation of tangible objects per production specifications.

task: Set dials and start machines to polish lenses or hold lenses against rotating wheels to polish them manually.
rationale: Task involves operating physical machinery (rotating wheels) and manually holding lenses against them for polishing—direct physical manipulation of objects and equipment operation.

task: Set up machines to polish, bevel, edge, or grind lenses, flats, blanks, or other precision optical elements.
rationale: Setting up machines requires physical operation and adjustment of equipment to process optical elements.

task: Repair broken parts, using precision hand tools and soldering irons.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (hand tools, soldering irons) and hands-on repair work on tangible components.

task: Position and adjust cutting tools to specified curvature, dimensions, and depth of cut.
rationale: Positioning and adjusting cutting tools requires hands-on physical manipulation and operation of tools to achieve precise specifications.

task: Inspect, weigh, and measure mounted or unmounted lenses after completion to verify alignment and conformance to specifications, using precision instruments.
rationale: Physical inspection using precision instruments to examine, weigh, and measure tangible optical components requires hands-on manipulation and physical presence.

task: Remove lenses from molds and separate lenses in containers for further processing or storage.
rationale: Removing lenses from molds and handling/separating them requires physical manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Lay out lenses and trace lens outlines on glass, using templates.
rationale: Laying out and tracing lens outlines requires physical handling of lenses, templates, and glass; marking/tracing is manual execution.

task: Immerse eyeglass frames in solutions to harden, soften, or dye frames.
rationale: Physical manipulation of eyeglass frames in chemical solutions requires hands-on handling and chemical processing of tangible objects.

task: Control equipment that coats lenses to alter their reflective qualities.
rationale: Operating physical lens-coating equipment requires hands-on control of machinery to execute the coating process.

task: Start engines, operate controls, and pilot airplanes to transport passengers, mail, or freight, adhering to flight plans, regulations, and procedures.
rationale: Piloting airplanes requires operating physical controls and machinery to transport passengers/cargo, a primary hands-on execution task.

task: Inspect aircraft for defects and malfunctions, according to pre-flight checklists.
rationale: Physical inspection of aircraft requires hands-on examination, touching/testing components, and being present at the aircraft to detect defects.

task: Conduct in-flight tests and evaluations at specified altitudes and in all types of weather to determine the receptivity and other characteristics of equipment and systems.
rationale: Requires hands-on operation and physical manipulation of aircraft systems and equipment at various altitudes and weather conditions during flight operations.

task: Check aircraft prior to flights to ensure that the engines, controls, instruments, and other systems are functioning properly.
rationale: Physical inspection of aircraft engines, controls, and systems requires hands-on examination and testing of tangible equipment at the work site.

task: Start engines, operate controls, and pilot airplanes to transport passengers, mail, or freight according to flight plans, regulations, and procedures.
rationale: Operating controls and piloting an airplane requires hands-on physical manipulation of aircraft systems and active navigation/control of a vehicle.

task: Use instrumentation to pilot aircraft when visibility is poor.
rationale: Piloting aircraft requires operating physical controls and manipulating the aircraft's machinery and systems directly.

task: Check baggage or cargo to ensure that it has been loaded correctly.
rationale: Checking baggage/cargo requires physical inspection and interaction with tangible objects to verify correct loading.

task: Conduct in-flight tests and evaluations at specified altitudes and in all types of weather to determine the receptivity and other characteristics of equipment and systems.
rationale: In-flight testing requires physical operation of aircraft and hands-on inspection/evaluation of equipment systems at various altitudes and weather conditions.

task: Rescue and evacuate injured persons.
rationale: Physically moving, carrying, and handling injured persons requires direct bodily contact and physical presence at the emergency site.

task: Perform minor aircraft maintenance and repair work, or arrange for major maintenance.
rationale: Minor maintenance and repair work requires physical manipulation of aircraft components and tools. Major maintenance arrangement is coordinating but involves hands-on work.

task: Fly with other pilots or pilot-license applicants to evaluate their proficiency.
rationale: Flying an aircraft requires direct physical operation of controls and manipulation of complex machinery in real-time.

task: Post visual display boards and status boards.
rationale: Posting boards requires physical placement and installation of tangible objects on walls or designated areas.

task: Follow appropriate safety procedures for transporting dangerous goods.
rationale: Transporting dangerous goods requires physical operation of vehicles and hands-on handling of hazardous materials at work sites.

task: Check vehicles to ensure that mechanical, safety, and emergency equipment is in good working order.
rationale: Physical inspection of vehicles requires hands-on examination of mechanical parts, safety systems, and equipment to verify working order.

task: Maneuver trucks into loading or unloading positions, following signals from loading crew and checking that vehicle and loading equipment are properly positioned.
rationale: Operating and maneuvering a truck requires physical control of machinery and equipment at a work site.

task: Drive trucks with capacities greater than 13 tons, including tractor-trailer combinations, to transport and deliver products, livestock, or other materials.
rationale: Operating a truck or tractor-trailer is physical operation of a vehicle requiring hands-on control and bodily presence at the work site.

task: Secure cargo for transport, using ropes, blocks, chain, binders, or covers.
rationale: Securing cargo involves physically handling ropes, chains, binders, and covers—direct manipulation of tangible objects and tools to prepare cargo for transport.

task: Couple or uncouple trailers by changing trailer jack positions, connecting or disconnecting air or electrical lines, or manipulating fifth-wheel locks.
rationale: Physical manipulation of equipment: positioning jacks, connecting/disconnecting lines, operating fifth-wheel locks.

task: Check conditions of trailers after contents have been unloaded to ensure that there has been no damage.
rationale: Inspecting trailers requires physical presence at the work site to examine equipment for damage.

task: Crank trailer landing gear up or down to safely secure vehicles.
rationale: Operating landing gear requires physical manipulation of the crank mechanism and trailer equipment to secure vehicles.

task: Perform basic vehicle maintenance tasks, such as adding oil, fuel, or radiator fluid, performing minor repairs, or washing trucks.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of vehicle parts, tools, and fluids; hands-on maintenance and repair work.

task: Inventory and inspect goods to be moved to determine quantities and conditions.
rationale: Physical inspection of goods requires hands-on examination and being present at the work site to assess conditions and quantities.

task: Remove debris from loaded trailers.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation—removing debris involves hands-on cleaning and object handling of tangible materials from trailers.

task: Follow special cargo-related procedures, such as checking refrigeration systems for frozen foods or providing food or water for livestock.
rationale: Checking refrigeration systems and providing food/water require hands-on physical inspection and manipulation of equipment and materials.

task: Install or remove special equipment, such as tire chains, grader blades, plow blades, or sanders.
rationale: Installing or removing equipment requires physical manipulation and attachment/detachment of tangible objects to machinery.

task: Perform emergency roadside repairs, such as changing tires or installing light bulbs, tire chains, or spark plugs.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of vehicle parts and tools—changing tires, installing chains, bulbs, and spark plugs are hands-on mechanical tasks.

task: Drive engines within railroad yards or other establishments to couple, uncouple, or switch railroad cars.
rationale: Operating locomotive engines to maneuver and couple/uncouple railroad cars requires direct physical operation of heavy machinery and vehicle control.

task: Inspect engines before and after use to ensure proper operation.
rationale: Inspecting engines requires physical examination and hands-on interaction with equipment to check operation and condition.

task: Apply and release hand brakes.
rationale: Physically manipulating hand brakes requires direct physical interaction with a mechanical control device.

task: Inspect the condition of stationary trains, rolling stock, and equipment.
rationale: Physical inspection requires being present to examine trains, rolling stock, and equipment directly; involves touching/observing tangible objects.

task: Observe water levels and oil, air, and steam pressure gauges to ensure proper operation of equipment.
rationale: Requires physical presence at equipment to observe gauges and monitor conditions in real-time on-site.

task: Spot cars for loading and unloading at customer locations.
rationale: Spotting cars requires physical presence at loading areas, positioning vehicles, and hands-on logistics coordination at work sites.

task: Inspect track for defects such as broken rails and switch malfunctions.
rationale: Physical inspection of rails and switches requires being on-site, examining and touching track infrastructure for visible and tactile defects.

task: Ride on moving cars by holding onto grab irons and standing on ladder steps.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and bodily movement: holding onto grab irons and standing on ladder steps while riding moving cars.

task: Operate track switches, derails, automatic switches, and retarders to change routing of train or cars.
rationale: Operating track switches, derails, and retarders requires physical manipulation and operation of mechanical equipment to control train routing.

task: Couple and uncouple air hoses and electrical connections between cars.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of hoses and connections; hands-on attachment/detachment of equipment between rail cars.

task: Pull knuckles to open them for coupling.
rationale: Pulling knuckles involves physical manipulation of coupling components to open them.

task: Provide assistance in aligning drawbars, using available equipment to lift, pull, or push on the drawbars.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and operation of equipment to lift, pull, and push drawbars into alignment.

task: Drive locomotives to and from various stations in roundhouses to have locomotives cleaned, serviced, repaired, or supplied.
rationale: Operating locomotives is physical operation of machinery requiring hands-on control and movement at a work site.

task: Perform routine repair and maintenance duties.
rationale: Repair and maintenance involve hands-on physical manipulation of equipment, tools, and machinery to restore or maintain functionality.

task: Operate flatcars equipped with derricks or railcars to transport personnel or equipment.
rationale: Operating flatcars with derricks involves controlling physical machinery and equipment to transport cargo—a hands-on operation requiring active control of machinery.

task: Provide assistance in the installation or repair of rails and ties.
rationale: Installation or repair of rails and ties requires physical manipulation of heavy materials, use of tools and equipment, and hands-on work at a site.

task: Instruct workers to set warning signals in front and at rear of trains during emergency stops.
rationale: Requires physical placement of warning signals on tracks—tangible object manipulation in a work environment.

task: Inspect each car periodically during runs.
rationale: Inspecting cars requires physical presence, hands-on observation, and touching/examining vehicle components.

task: Inspect freight cars for compliance with sealing procedures, and record car numbers and seal numbers.
rationale: Physical inspection of freight cars requires being present onsite, visually examining seals and physically recording car/seal numbers on location.

task: Activate fuel pumps and fill fuel tanks of vehicles with gasoline or diesel fuel to specified levels.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of fuel pumps and tanks; involves handling tangible objects (fuel nozzles, tanks) and operating equipment.

task: Clean parking areas, offices, restrooms, or equipment, and remove trash.
rationale: Cleaning requires physical manipulation of cleaning tools, equipment, and direct physical contact with surfaces, restrooms, and trash removal.

task: Perform minor repairs, such as adjusting brakes, replacing spark plugs, or changing engine oil or filters.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical manipulation and operation of vehicle parts and tools to adjust, replace, and change components.

task: Order stock, and price and shelve incoming goods.
rationale: Shelving goods requires physical manipulation and placement of objects. Pricing involves inspection and physical labeling of items.

task: Rotate, test, and repair or replace tires.
rationale: Rotating, testing, and repairing/replacing tires requires hands-on physical manipulation of vehicle parts and tools.

task: Grease and lubricate vehicles or specified units, such as springs, universal joints, or steering knuckles, using grease guns or spray lubricants.
rationale: Physical manipulation of tools (grease guns, spray lubricants) and direct application to vehicle components requires hands-on mechanical work.

task: Sell and install accessories, such as batteries, windshield wiper blades, fan belts, bulbs, or headlamps.
rationale: Installing accessories requires physical manipulation of vehicle parts (batteries, wiper blades, belts, bulbs, headlamps). Work involves hands-on assembly and fastening.

task: Test and charge batteries.
rationale: Physical manipulation and operation of batteries and charging equipment required.

task: Start aircraft and observe gauges, meters, and other instruments to detect evidence of malfunctions.
rationale: Operating an aircraft engine (starting) is physical machinery operation. Observing instruments requires being present at the aircraft to monitor gauges directly.

task: Examine aircraft access plates and doors for security.
rationale: Requires physical inspection, touching/visually examining aircraft access plates and doors on-site.

task: Examine landing gear, tires, and exteriors of fuselage, wings, and engines for evidence of damage or corrosion and the need for repairs.
rationale: Physical inspection requiring hands-on examination and visual/tactile assessment of aircraft components for damage and corrosion.

task: Inspect new, repaired, or modified aircraft to identify damage or defects and to assess airworthiness and conformance to standards, using checklists, hand tools, and test instruments.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of aircraft, hands-on use of test instruments and tools, and visual/tactile examination to identify defects.

task: Conduct flight test programs to test equipment, instruments, and systems under a variety of conditions, using both manual and automatic controls.
rationale: Flight testing involves operating aircraft controls (manual and automatic) and physically piloting equipment through various conditions to evaluate systems.

task: Position deflector bars, gates, chutes, or spouts to divert flow of materials from one conveyor onto another conveyor.
rationale: Positioning physical equipment (deflector bars, gates, chutes, spouts) requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects to control material flow.

task: Weigh or measure materials and products, using scales or other measuring instruments, or read scales on conveyors that continually weigh products, to verify specified tonnages and prevent overloads.
rationale: Requires physical operation of scales and measuring instruments, and reading conveyor scales on-site to verify product weights.

task: Manipulate controls, levers, and valves to start pumps, auxiliary equipment, or conveyors, and to adjust equipment positions, speeds, timing, and material flows.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of controls, levers, and valves to operate pumps and conveyors—direct hands-on equipment operation.

task: Clean, sterilize, and maintain equipment, machinery, and work stations, using hand tools, shovels, brooms, chemicals, hoses, and lubricants.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tools, chemicals, and machinery; direct hands-on cleaning, sterilization, and maintenance work.

task: Repair or replace equipment components or parts such as blades, rolls, and pumps.
rationale: Repairing or replacing equipment components requires physical manipulation of tangible parts (blades, rolls, pumps) and hands-on mechanical work.

task: Stop equipment or machinery and clear jams, using poles, bars, and hand tools, or remove damaged materials from conveyors.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (poles, bars, hand tools) and hands-on clearing of jams and removal of materials from machinery.

task: Collect samples of materials or products, checking them to ensure conformance to specifications or sending them to laboratories for analysis.
rationale: Task involves physical collection of material samples and hands-on inspection to verify conformance, or preparation for lab analysis.

task: Load, unload, or adjust materials or products on conveyors by hand, by using lifts, hoists, and scoops, or by opening gates, chutes, or hoppers.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of objects, operation of mechanical equipment (lifts, hoists), and manual labor (loading, unloading, adjusting materials).

task: Affix identifying information to materials or products, using hand tools.
rationale: Affixing identifying information requires physical manipulation using hand tools on tangible materials or products.

task: Distribute materials, supplies, and equipment to work stations, using lifts and trucks.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical manipulation of equipment (lifts, trucks) and distributing tangible materials/supplies/equipment to work stations.

task: Move, assemble, and connect hoses or nozzles to material hoppers, storage tanks, conveyor sections or chutes, and pumps.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of hoses, nozzles, and equipment connections; involves bodily movement to assemble and connect components at various work locations.

task: Press console buttons to deflect packages to predetermined accumulators or reject lines.
rationale: Pressing console buttons requires direct physical manipulation of equipment controls to operate the package sorting system.

task: Join sections of conveyor frames at temporary working areas, and connect power units.
rationale: Joining conveyor frames and connecting power units requires physical manipulation of equipment and assembly work at job sites.

task: Thread strapping through strapping tools and secure battens with strapping to form protective pallets around extrusions.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of strapping materials and tools, plus securing battens—hands-on physical work requiring direct object handling.

task: Move levers, depress foot pedals, or turn dials to operate cranes, cherry pickers, electromagnets, or other moving equipment for lifting, moving, or placing loads.
rationale: Operating physical machinery (cranes, cherry pickers, electromagnets) requires direct manipulation of controls and physical operation of equipment.

task: Inspect cables or grappling devices for wear and install or replace cables, as needed.
rationale: Physical inspection of cables/devices and installation/replacement of cables requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Test cooked food by tasting and smelling it to ensure palatability and flavor conformity.
rationale: Task requires physical tasting and smelling—direct sensory interaction with food objects to evaluate quality.

task: Clean, lubricate, and maintain mechanisms such as cables, pulleys, or grappling devices, making repairs, as necessary.
rationale: Requires hands-on physical manipulation of equipment components and operation of maintenance tools to clean, lubricate, and repair mechanical devices.

task: Inspect and adjust crane mechanisms or lifting accessories to prevent malfunctions or damage.
rationale: Requires physical inspection, manipulation, and adjustment of crane mechanisms and lifting equipment to identify and prevent defects.

task: Load or unload bundles from trucks, or move containers to storage bins, using moving equipment.
rationale: Loading/unloading cargo and moving containers involves physical manipulation of objects and operation of moving equipment.

task: Weigh bundles, using floor scales, and record weights for company records.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of bundles and operation of floor scales, with bodily movement to interact with physical objects.

task: Direct truck drivers backing vehicles into loading bays and cover, uncover, or secure loads for delivery.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation (covering/securing loads) and bodily presence at work site to direct vehicles and handle cargo.

task: Inspect bundle packaging for conformance to regulations or customer requirements, and remove and batch packaging tickets.
rationale: Task requires physical inspection of packaging, handling bundles, and manually removing/batching tickets—tangible object manipulation.

task: Attach identifying tags to containers or mark them with identifying information.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation—attaching tags or marking containers involves hands-on work with tangible objects.

task: Move freight, stock, or other materials to and from storage or production areas, loading docks, delivery vehicles, ships, or containers, by hand or using trucks, tractors, or other equipment.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials and operation of equipment (trucks, tractors) to move freight and stock.

task: Assemble product containers or crates, using hand tools and precut lumber.
rationale: Assembling containers/crates requires physical manipulation of materials, operation of hand tools, and direct physical work with tangible objects (lumber).

task: Pack containers and re-pack damaged containers.
rationale: Packing and repacking containers requires physical manipulation of objects, hands-on work with tangible items.

task: Carry needed tools or supplies from storage or trucks and return them after use.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and carrying of tangible objects (tools/supplies) between locations.

task: Install protective devices, such as bracing, padding, or strapping, to prevent shifting or damage to items being transported.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of protective materials (bracing, padding, strapping) and tangible objects to secure cargo for transport.

task: Maintain equipment storage areas to ensure that inventory is protected.
rationale: Maintaining storage areas requires physical work: organizing, arranging, cleaning, and inspecting tangible inventory items on-site.

task: Schedule and receive food and beverage deliveries, checking delivery contents to verify product quality and quantity.
rationale: Receiving deliveries requires physical inspection of items (checking quality/quantity) and handling of tangible products (food and beverages).

task: Attach slings, hooks, or other devices to lift cargo and guide loads.
rationale: Physical manipulation of cargo and lifting devices (slings, hooks) to guide loads during hoisting operations.

task: Adjust controls to guide, position, or move equipment, such as cranes, booms, or cameras.
rationale: Adjusting controls requires operating physical machinery/equipment to move or position objects in space.

task: Connect electrical equipment to power sources so that it can be tested before use.
rationale: Connecting electrical equipment to power sources requires physical manipulation of equipment and cables, a hands-on task.

task: Monitor food preparation methods, portion sizes, and garnishing and presentation of food to ensure that food is prepared and presented in an acceptable manner.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of food preparation, observing techniques, examining portions, and assessing presentation by being present at the work site.

task: Inspect waste pretreatment, treatment, and disposal facilities and systems for conformance to federal, state, or local regulations.
rationale: Inspecting facilities requires physical presence on-site, examining equipment, systems, and conditions directly. Inspectors must observe and interact with physical infrastructure firsthand.

task: Determine sampling locations and methods, and collect water or wastewater samples for analysis, preserving samples with appropriate containers and preservation methods.
rationale: Collecting water/wastewater samples requires physically handling containers, equipment, and samples on-site; determining locations involves physical presence at work site.

task: Observe and record field conditions, gathering, interpreting, and reporting data such as flow meter readings and chemical levels.
rationale: Requires physical presence at work site to observe conditions, read meters, and collect physical samples or data onsite.

task: Perform laboratory tests on samples collected, such as analyzing the content of contaminated wastewater.
rationale: Lab testing involves physical manipulation of samples, operating lab equipment and instruments, and hands-on analysis of tangible materials.

task: Maintain and repair materials, work sites, and equipment.
rationale: Maintenance and repair require physical manipulation of tools, equipment, and materials at work sites.

task: Inspect, diagnose, maintain, and operate test setups and equipment to detect malfunctions.
rationale: Inspecting, diagnosing, and operating test setups and equipment requires physical interaction with tangible machinery and hands-on troubleshooting of malfunctions.

task: Adjust, repair, or replace faulty components of test setups and equipment.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of equipment components, hands-on repair work, and replacement of faulty parts.

task: Construct and maintain test facilities for aircraft parts and systems, according to specifications.
rationale: Constructing and maintaining test facilities involves physical setup, assembly, installation of equipment, and hands-on maintenance work at the facility site.

task: Test aircraft systems under simulated operational conditions, performing systems readiness tests and pre- and post-operational checkouts, to establish design or fabrication parameters.
rationale: Testing aircraft systems requires hands-on operation of equipment, physical inspection, and direct manipulation of systems in a test environment.

task: Fabricate and install parts and systems to be tested in test equipment, using hand tools, power tools, and test instruments.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of parts and systems using hand tools, power tools, and direct installation work.

task: Perform some food preparation or service tasks, such as cooking, clearing tables, and serving food and drinks when necessary.
rationale: Cooking, clearing tables, and serving food/drinks involve physical manipulation of objects and food preparation.

task: Finish vehicle instrumentation and deinstrumentation.
rationale: Installing and removing instruments from vehicles requires physical manipulation of equipment and tangible objects.

task: Test performance of electromechanical assemblies, using test instruments such as oscilloscopes, electronic voltmeters, or bridges.
rationale: Requires physical operation of test instruments and hands-on testing of electromechanical assemblies.

task: Install electrical or electronic parts and hardware in housings or assemblies, using soldering equipment and hand tools.
rationale: Installing parts using soldering equipment and hand tools requires physical manipulation and operation of equipment.

task: Align, fit, or assemble component parts, using hand or power tools, fixtures, templates, or microscopes.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of component parts using hand/power tools, fixtures, and templates to assemble items.

task: Inspect parts for surface defects.
rationale: Inspection requires hands-on physical examination and touching/viewing parts to detect surface defects.

task: Verify part dimensions or clearances to ensure conformance to specifications, using precision measuring instruments.
rationale: Requires physical use of precision measuring instruments to inspect tangible objects and verify conformance to specifications at work site.

task: Operate metalworking machines to fabricate housings, jigs, fittings, or fixtures.
rationale: Operating metalworking machines requires physical manipulation of equipment and tools to fabricate tangible metal products.

task: Repair, rework, or calibrate hydraulic or pneumatic assemblies or systems to meet operational specifications or tolerances.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of hydraulic/pneumatic equipment, operation of tools, and hands-on assembly work to meet specifications.

task: Observe the structure and properties of matter, and the transformation and propagation of energy, using equipment such as masers, lasers, and telescopes, to explore and identify the basic principles governing these phenomena.
rationale: Operating specialized equipment (masers, lasers, telescopes) and conducting physical observations requires hands-on equipment manipulation and operation.

task: Visit people in homes, hospitals, or prisons to provide them with comfort and support.
rationale: Requires physical presence at work sites and hands-on emotional/spiritual caregiving interaction with people in person.

task: Copy and distribute classroom materials.
rationale: Copying and distributing materials require physical manipulation of tangible objects and operation of copying equipment.

task: Demonstrate use of laboratory equipment and enforce laboratory rules.
rationale: Demonstrating equipment use requires hands-on physical manipulation and operation. Enforcing rules involves physical presence and oversight at the work site.

task: Complete laboratory projects prior to assigning them to students so that any needed modifications can be made.
rationale: Completing laboratory projects requires hands-on physical execution of experiments, handling lab equipment, materials, and recording observations.

task: Advise farmers and demonstrate techniques in areas such as feeding and health maintenance of livestock, growing and harvesting practices, and financial planning.
rationale: Demonstrating feeding, health, and harvesting techniques requires hands-on physical execution and practical livestock/crop handling.

task: Prepare and distribute leaflets, pamphlets, and visual aids for educational and informational purposes.
rationale: Preparing and distributing leaflets requires physical handling, assembly, and transportation of tangible materials to distribute them.

task: Greet guests, escort them to their seats, and present them with menus and wine lists.
rationale: Greeting and escorting guests requires physical presence and movement at the work site; presenting menus involves handling and delivering tangible objects.

task: Conduct field demonstrations of new products, techniques, or services.
rationale: Field demonstrations involve physically showing and operating products/equipment on-site to audiences.

task: Provide direct assistance to farmers by performing activities such as purchasing or selling products and supplies, supervising properties, and collecting soil and herbage samples for testing.
rationale: Collecting soil and herbage samples requires physical presence, handling materials, and hands-on inspection at work sites.

task: Use materials such as pens and ink, watercolors, charcoal, oil, or computer software to create artwork.
rationale: Creating artwork requires physical manipulation of materials (pens, brushes, charcoal) and/or operation of computer software to produce a tangible work product.

task: Create finished art work as decoration, or to elucidate or substitute for spoken or written messages.
rationale: Creating finished artwork requires physical manipulation of materials (paints, brushes, clay, etc.) and hands-on artistic execution.

task: Cut, bend, laminate, arrange, and fasten individual or mixed raw and manufactured materials and products to form works of art.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials (cutting, bending, laminating, arranging, fastening) to create art.

task: Render drawings, illustrations, and sketches of buildings, manufactured products, or models, working from sketches, blueprints, memory, models, or reference materials.
rationale: Creating drawings requires physical manipulation of tools (pencils, brushes, pens) and hand-eye coordination to produce visual work product.

task: Create sketches, profiles, or likenesses of posed subjects or photographs, using any combination of freehand drawing, mechanical assembly kits, and computer imaging.
rationale: Requires physical drawing, handling mechanical kits, and hands-on artistic execution of visual work.

task: Create sculptures, statues, and other three-dimensional artwork by using abrasives and tools to shape, carve, and fabricate materials such as clay, stone, wood, or metal.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools and abrasives to shape and carve tangible materials like clay, stone, wood, and metal into artwork.

task: Shade and fill in sketch outlines and backgrounds, using a variety of media such as water colors, markers, and transparent washes, labeling designated colors when necessary.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of art materials (watercolors, markers, washes) and hands-on application to sketches.

task: Collaborate with engineers, mechanics, and other technical experts as necessary to build and install creations.
rationale: Building and installing creations involves hands-on physical work manipulating objects, assembling components, and operating tools/machinery.

task: Create and prepare sketches and model drawings of cartoon characters, providing details from memory, live models, manufactured products, or reference materials.
rationale: Creating sketches and model drawings requires physical manipulation of art materials (pencils, paper, drawing tools) and hands-on execution of artistic work.

task: Brush or spray protective or decorative finishes on completed background panels, informational legends, exhibit accessories, or finished paintings.
rationale: Brushing or spraying finishes requires hands-on physical manipulation of tools (brush/spray) and materials on tangible objects.

task: Trace drawings onto clear acetate for painting or coloring, or trace them with ink to make final copies.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical manipulation of materials (acetate, drawings, ink) and hand-based tracing work.

task: Apply solvents and cleaning agents to clean surfaces of paintings, and to remove accretions, discolorations, and deteriorated varnish.
rationale: Applying solvents and agents involves hands-on physical manipulation of paintings and tangible materials to clean and restore surfaces.

task: Model substances such as clay or wax, using fingers and small hand tools to form objects.
rationale: Physical manipulation of clay/wax with fingers and hand tools to form objects requires direct tactile execution.

task: Provide entertainment at special events by performing activities such as drawing cartoons.
rationale: Drawing cartoons requires hands-on physical manipulation of tools (pen, paper) and bodily presence to perform entertainment.

task: Direct rehearsals to instruct dancers in dance steps and in techniques to achieve desired effects.
rationale: Directing rehearsals involves physically demonstrating dance steps and techniques to dancers, requiring hands-on physical demonstration of movement.

task: Train, exercise, and attend dance classes to maintain high levels of technical proficiency, physical ability, and physical fitness.
rationale: Training and exercise involve physical movement and bodily exertion to develop fitness and technical proficiency through hands-on practice.

task: Teach students, dancers, and other performers about rhythm and interpretive movement.
rationale: Teaching through physical demonstration of movement and rhythm requires hands-on bodily movement and physical execution, not just verbal instruction.

task: Experiment with different types of dancers, steps, dances, and placements, testing ideas informally to get feedback from dancers.
rationale: Choreographer physically demonstrates dance steps and movements with dancers, requiring bodily movement and hands-on creative direction of physical performance.

task: Restage traditional dances and works in dance companies' repertoires, developing new interpretations.
rationale: Restaging dances requires physical movement, choreography execution, and demonstration of dance techniques with the body as the primary tool.

task: Investigate the adequacy of ventilation, exhaust equipment, lighting, or other conditions that could affect employee health, comfort, or performance.
rationale: Task requires physical inspection on-site of ventilation, exhaust, lighting, and workplace conditions—necessitating presence at work location to observe and assess tangible environmental factors.

task: Conduct safety training or education programs and demonstrate the use of safety equipment.
rationale: Demonstrating safety equipment requires physical handling and showing actual use, making it a hands-on manual task.

task: Collect samples of dust, gases, vapors, or other potentially toxic materials for analysis.
rationale: Collecting samples requires physical handling of sampling equipment, containers, and navigating to work sites to gather tangible materials for analysis.

task: Investigate health-related complaints and inspect facilities to ensure that they comply with public health legislation and regulations.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of facilities and on-site investigation to verify compliance with health regulations.

task: Inspect specified areas to ensure the presence of fire prevention equipment, safety equipment, or first-aid supplies.
rationale: Requires physical presence at work site to visually inspect and verify equipment locations and conditions.

task: Conduct audits at hazardous waste sites or industrial sites or participate in hazardous waste site investigations.
rationale: Conducting audits and investigations at hazardous waste sites requires physical presence on-site, hands-on sampling, inspection of facilities, and direct interaction with the physical environment.

task: Collect samples of hazardous materials or arrange for sample collection.
rationale: Collecting hazardous material samples requires physical handling, proper containment, and hands-on execution at the site. Arranging collection also involves coordinating physical work.

task: Prepare hazardous, radioactive, or mixed waste samples for transportation or storage by treating, compacting, packaging, and labeling them.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of hazardous materials, compacting, packaging, and labeling samples—all hands-on physical execution.

task: Perform laboratory analyses or physical inspections of samples to detect disease or to assess purity or cleanliness.
rationale: Physical inspection and manipulation of samples requires hands-on work with tangible objects to detect disease, assess purity, or cleanliness.

task: Supply, operate, or maintain personal protective equipment.
rationale: Supplying, operating, or maintaining PPE involves physical handling, fitting, testing, and inspection of tangible protective equipment items.

task: Prepare or calibrate equipment used to collect or analyze samples.
rationale: Preparing and calibrating equipment requires hands-on physical manipulation and operation of tangible instruments and machinery.

task: Test workplaces for environmental hazards, such as exposure to radiation, chemical or biological hazards, or excessive noise.
rationale: Testing workplaces for environmental hazards requires physical presence on-site, using equipment to measure/detect radiation, chemicals, noise, and other hazards. Hands-on inspection and measurement are required.

task: Estimate sizes and characteristics of fires, and report findings to base camps by radio or telephone.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical presence at fire scenes to observe, assess, and measure fire characteristics firsthand.

task: Extinguish smaller fires with portable extinguishers, shovels, and axes.
rationale: Requires physical operation of tools (extinguishers, shovels, axes) and hands-on fire suppression work at the scene.

task: Locate forest fires on area maps, using azimuth sighters and known landmarks.
rationale: Requires physical operation of azimuth sighters (instruments) and on-site physical inspection of area maps to locate fires.

task: Examine and inventory firefighting equipment, such as axes, fire hoses, shovels, pumps, buckets, and fire extinguishers, to determine amount and condition.
rationale: Examining and inventorying physical equipment requires hands-on inspection, visual assessment, and physical interaction with tangible objects like axes, hoses, and extinguishers.

task: Manage and maintain temporary or permanent lodging facilities.
rationale: Maintaining lodging facilities involves hands-on physical work: inspecting rooms, cleaning, repairing fixtures, managing grounds, performing preventive maintenance.

task: Direct maintenance and repair of firefighting equipment, or requisition new equipment.
rationale: Maintaining and repairing firefighting equipment requires hands-on physical work with tools and equipment; requisitioning is a secondary administrative component.

task: Patrol assigned areas, looking for forest fires, hazardous conditions, and weather phenomena.
rationale: Patrolling assigned areas requires physical presence at work site to observe and inspect environment for fires and hazards.

task: Inspect camp sites to ensure that campers are in compliance with forest use regulations.
rationale: Requires physical presence at campsites to observe conditions, check equipment, and verify compliance with regulations on-site.

task: Inspect forest tracts and logging areas for fire hazards such as accumulated wastes or mishandling of combustibles, and recommend appropriate fire prevention measures.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of forest tracts and logging areas on-site, examining tangible hazards like waste accumulation and combustibles.

task: Monitor traffic flow to locate safe gaps through which pedestrians can cross streets.
rationale: Monitoring traffic flow requires physical presence at a street location to observe and assess real-time traffic patterns and identify safe crossing gaps for pedestrians.

task: Direct traffic movement or warn of hazards, using signs, flags, lanterns, and hand signals.
rationale: Uses physical tools (signs, flags, lanterns) and hand signals; requires physical presence at work site to direct traffic and warn of hazards.

task: Distribute traffic control signs and markers at designated points.
rationale: Distributing signs and markers requires physical manipulation and placement of tangible objects at work sites.

task: Check the quality of raw or cooked food products to ensure that standards are met.
rationale: Checking food quality requires physical inspection—touching, looking at, possibly tasting or smelling products to assess standards.

task: Check the quantity and quality of received products.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of tangible products to verify quantity and assess quality by sight and touch.

task: Determine how food should be presented and create decorative food displays.
rationale: Creating decorative food displays requires physical manipulation of food and objects to arrange them aesthetically.

task: Prepare and cook foods of all types, either on a regular basis or for special guests or functions.
rationale: Cooking involves physical manipulation of food, operation of kitchen equipment and appliances, and hands-on food preparation.

task: Demonstrate new cooking techniques or equipment to staff.
rationale: Demonstrating cooking techniques requires hands-on physical manipulation of cooking equipment and food preparation activities.

task: Monitor food distribution, ensuring that meals are delivered to the correct recipients and that guidelines, such as those for special diets, are followed.
rationale: Requires physical presence to observe meal delivery, verify recipients, and inspect special diet compliance. Involves hands-on monitoring at the point of distribution.

task: Clean or sterilize dishes, kitchen utensils, equipment, or facilities.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and contact with tangible objects—dishes, utensils, equipment—to clean and sterilize them.

task: Examine trays to ensure that they contain required items.
rationale: Examining trays requires physical inspection—being present to visually and tactilely verify item contents.

task: Place food servings on plates or trays according to orders or instructions.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of food and tableware to portion and arrange items on plates or trays.

task: Load trays with accessories, such as eating utensils, napkins, or condiments.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of objects (utensils, napkins, condiments) and arranging them on trays.

task: Stock service stations with items, such as ice, napkins, or straws.
rationale: Requires physical handling and placement of tangible items (ice, napkins, straws) into service station storage/dispensers.

task: Remove trays and stack dishes for return to kitchen after meals are finished.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of trays and dishes—removing, handling, and stacking tangible objects for transport.

task: Prepare food items, such as sandwiches, salads, soups, or beverages.
rationale: Preparing food requires physical manipulation of ingredients, using kitchen tools, and hands-on food preparation work.

task: Carry food, silverware, or linen on trays or use carts to carry trays.
rationale: Physical manipulation and carrying of tangible objects (food, silverware, linen, trays) is hands-on manual work.

task: Inspect guest rooms, public areas, and grounds for cleanliness and appearance.
rationale: Requires physical presence and visual/tactile inspection of spaces to assess cleanliness and appearance conditions.

task: Determine where patients or patrons would like to eat their meals and help them get situated.
rationale: Requires physical presence to interact with patients/patrons, help them get situated involves bodily movement and direct personal care assistance.

task: Wipe tables or seats with dampened cloths or replace dirty tablecloths.
rationale: Physical manipulation of objects (cloths, tablecloths) and tangible cleaning of tables and seats.

task: Set tables with clean linens, condiments, or other supplies.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of linens, condiments, and supplies to arrange tables for service.

task: Scrape and stack dirty dishes and carry dishes and other tableware to kitchens for cleaning.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical manipulation of dishes and tableware, carrying objects to kitchens—tangible physical work.

task: Clean up spilled food or drink or broken dishes and remove empty bottles and trash.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of objects (cleaning spills, removing dishes, collecting trash) and bodily movement to clean a work area.

task: Perform serving, cleaning, or stocking duties in establishments, such as cafeterias or dining rooms, to facilitate customer service.
rationale: Serving, cleaning, and stocking involve physical manipulation of objects (dishes, food, supplies) and bodily movement throughout establishments.

task: Maintain adequate supplies of items, such as clean linens, silverware, glassware, dishes, or trays.
rationale: Maintaining supplies involves physically handling, organizing, and restocking tangible items like linens, silverware, and dishes.

task: Serve ice water, coffee, rolls, or butter to patrons.
rationale: Serving food and beverages requires physically handling items, carrying them, and directly presenting them to patrons at tables.

task: Fill beverage or ice dispensers.
rationale: Filling dispensers requires physical manipulation of tangible objects (beverages, ice, containers) and direct manual operation.

task: Stock cabinets or serving areas with condiments and refill condiment containers.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of containers, stocking shelves, and refilling condiment dispensers at serving areas.

task: Carry food, dishes, trays, or silverware from kitchens or supply departments to serving counters.
rationale: Physical manipulation of tangible objects—carrying food, dishes, trays, and silverware—requiring bodily movement and handling.

task: Serve food to customers when waiters or waitresses need assistance.
rationale: Serving food involves physical manipulation and carrying of tangible objects (plates, drinks) to customers at tables.

task: Clean and polish counters, shelves, walls, furniture, or equipment in food service areas or other areas of restaurants and mop or vacuum floors.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of cleaning tools and surfaces—wiping, polishing, mopping, vacuuming counters, shelves, walls, and floors in restaurants.

task: Carry trays from food counters to tables for cafeteria patrons.
rationale: Carrying trays requires physical manipulation and transport of objects from one location to another.

task: Replenish supplies of food or equipment at steam tables or service bars.
rationale: Physically carrying, placing, and arranging food and equipment at service stations requires hands-on manual work.

task: Wash glasses or other serving equipment at bars.
rationale: Washing glasses and serving equipment requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and hands-on cleaning work.

task: Garnish foods and position them on tables to make them visible and accessible.
rationale: Garnishing food requires physical manipulation of food items and positioning requires hands-on placement at work location.

task: Carry linens to or from laundry areas.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and carrying of tangible objects (linens) to specified locations.

task: Stock refrigerating units with wines or bottled beer or replace empty beer kegs.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of bottles and kegs, lifting, and operating refrigeration units to stock merchandise.

task: Mix and prepare flavors for mixed drinks.
rationale: Mixing and preparing drinks requires physical manipulation of bottles, ingredients, and tools (shakers, stirrers) to combine and prepare beverages.

task: Slice and pit fruit used to garnish drinks.
rationale: Slicing and pitting fruit requires physical manipulation of objects and use of cutting tools.

task: Wash dishes, glassware, flatware, pots, or pans, using dishwashers or by hand.
rationale: Physical manipulation of dishes, glassware, and utensils using dishwashers or hand-washing requires direct physical handling and operation of equipment.

task: Place clean dishes, utensils, or cooking equipment in storage areas.
rationale: Placing dishes and equipment requires physical handling and manipulation of tangible objects into storage.

task: Maintain kitchen work areas, equipment, or utensils in clean and orderly condition.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation—cleaning, organizing, and handling kitchen equipment and utensils to maintain workspace.

task: Stock supplies, such as food or utensils, in serving stations, cupboards, refrigerators, or salad bars.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tangible objects (food, utensils) and bodily movement to stock shelves, cupboards, and serving stations.

task: Sweep or scrub floors.
rationale: Sweeping and scrubbing floors require physical manipulation of cleaning tools and direct physical engagement with surfaces.

task: Clean garbage cans with water or steam.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and operation of cleaning equipment (water hose or steam cleaner) on tangible objects.

task: Sort and remove trash, placing it in designated pickup areas.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of trash objects and movement to designated pickup areas on site.

task: Clean or prepare various foods for cooking or serving.
rationale: Physical manipulation of food items (washing, chopping, peeling, trimming) required for preparation.

task: Set up banquet tables.
rationale: Setting up banquet tables requires physically moving, arranging, and assembling furniture and table settings in a space.

task: Transfer supplies or equipment between storage and work areas, by hand or using hand trucks.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of objects and operation of hand trucks to move supplies between locations.

task: Receive and store supplies.
rationale: Receiving and storing supplies requires physical handling of items, movement, and manipulation of tangible goods.

task: Prepare and package individual place settings.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tableware, napkins, and utensils to assemble and package individual place settings.

task: Load or unload trucks that deliver or pick up food or supplies.
rationale: Physically lifting, carrying, and moving food or supplies onto/off trucks requires manual operation and bodily movement.

task: Plant or maintain vegetation through activities such as mulching, fertilizing, watering, mowing, or pruning.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of plants and use of tools; hands-on work with tangible objects and vegetation.

task: Inventory supplies of tools, equipment, or materials to ensure that sufficient supplies are available and items are in usable condition.
rationale: Requires physical inspection and handling of tools, equipment, and materials to verify condition and availability.

task: Direct or perform mixing or application of fertilizers, insecticides, herbicides, or fungicides.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation—mixing chemicals and applying products to plants/soil via spraying or spreading equipment.

task: Install or maintain landscaped areas, performing tasks such as removing snow, pouring cement curbs, or repairing sidewalks.
rationale: Tasks involve physical manipulation (removing snow, pouring cement, repairing) and operation of tools/equipment on-site.

task: Tour grounds, such as parks, botanical gardens, cemeteries, or golf courses, to inspect conditions of plants and soil.
rationale: Requires physical presence on-site, walking/patrolling grounds, and hands-on visual and tactile inspection of plants and soil conditions.

task: Fill sprayer tanks with water and chemicals, according to formulas.
rationale: Filling tanks requires physical manipulation of objects, pouring liquids, and handling equipment—core manual execution tasks.

task: Mix pesticides, herbicides, or fungicides for application to trees, shrubs, lawns, or botanical crops.
rationale: Mixing chemicals requires physical manipulation of containers, measuring, pouring, and combining substances—core manual work.

task: Cover areas to specified depths with pesticides, applying knowledge of weather conditions, droplet sizes, elevation-to-distance ratios, and obstructions.
rationale: Applying pesticides requires physical manipulation of spray equipment and physical presence in the field to operate machinery and interact with the land.

task: Lift, push, and swing nozzles, hoses, and tubes to direct spray over designated areas.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and movement of equipment (nozzles, hoses, tubes) to direct spray—hands-on physical work.

task: Start motors and engage machinery, such as sprayer agitators or pumps or portable spray equipment.
rationale: Starting motors and engaging machinery requires physical operation of equipment and controls to activate mechanical systems.

task: Connect hoses and nozzles selected according to terrain, distribution pattern requirements, types of infestations, and velocities.
rationale: Connecting hoses and nozzles requires physical manipulation of tangible equipment components and hands-on assembly work.

task: Clean or service machinery to ensure operating efficiency, using water, gasoline, lubricants, or hand tools.
rationale: Cleaning and servicing machinery requires hands-on physical manipulation using tools, lubricants, and materials to maintain equipment.

task: Plant grass with seed spreaders, and operate straw blowers to cover seeded areas with mixtures of asphalt and straw.
rationale: Primary execution involves operating physical machinery (seed spreaders, straw blowers) and physical manipulation of work site landscaping.

task: Provide assistance to staff members by inspecting rooms, setting tables, or doing laundry.
rationale: Physical tasks: inspecting rooms, setting tables, doing laundry all require hands-on manipulation and physical work.

task: Stock or distribute resources, such as samples or promotional or educational materials.
rationale: Stocking and distributing materials involves physical handling, organizing, and moving tangible objects to various locations.

task: Read electric, gas, water, or steam consumption meters and enter data in route books or hand-held computers.
rationale: Reading meters requires physical inspection and touching/viewing instruments on-site; data entry is secondary to the primary physical meter-reading work.

task: Walk or drive vehicles along established routes to take readings of meter dials.
rationale: Involves physical operation of vehicles (driving) and/or bodily movement (walking) to physically access and inspect meter dials on-site.

task: Inspect meters for unauthorized connections, defects, and damage, such as broken seals.
rationale: Requires physical inspection on-site: examining meters, checking seals, visually assessing defects and damage. Hands-on examination of tangible objects.

task: Connect and disconnect utility services at specific locations.
rationale: Connecting and disconnecting utilities requires physical manipulation of equipment, tools, and infrastructure at specific work sites.

task: Weigh letters and parcels, compute mailing costs based on type, weight, and destination, and affix correct postage.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation: weighing objects on a scale, affixing postage to tangible items.

task: Check mail to ensure correct postage and that packages and letters are in proper condition for mailing.
rationale: Requires physical inspection and handling of tangible mail items to verify postage, condition, and readiness for mailing.

task: Sort incoming and outgoing mail, according to type and destination, by hand or by operating electronic mail-sorting and scanning devices.
rationale: Task involves physical handling of mail by hand and/or operating mail-sorting machinery, requiring hands-on manipulation and physical presence.

task: Put undelivered parcels away, retrieve them when customers come to claim them, and complete any related documentation.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of parcels (putting away, retrieving) plus some paperwork documentation.

task: Receive letters and parcels, and place mail into bags.
rationale: Task requires physical handling of tangible objects (letters, parcels, bags) and manual sorting/placement.

task: Feed mail into postage canceling devices or hand stamp mail to cancel postage.
rationale: Feeding mail into machines and hand stamping require physical manipulation of mail objects and operation of equipment.

task: Transport mail from one work station to another.
rationale: Transporting mail requires physical movement and carrying objects from one location to another.

task: Deliver mail to residences and business establishments along specified routes by walking or driving, using a combination of satchels, carts, cars, and small trucks.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical manipulation (carrying mail in satchels), operation of vehicles (driving small trucks/cars), and bodily movement along routes to deliver mail.

task: Return to the post office with mail collected from homes, businesses, and public mailboxes.
rationale: Task involves physically collecting mail from multiple locations and transporting it back to the post office, requiring physical movement and handling of tangible objects.

task: Turn in money and receipts collected along mail routes.
rationale: Handling and turning in physical currency, receipts, and mail requires physical manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Bundle mail in preparation for delivery or transportation to relay boxes.
rationale: Bundling mail requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and preparation for transport.

task: Return incorrectly addressed mail to senders.
rationale: Handling and physically manipulating mail items, sorting by address, and processing returns requires direct physical handling of tangible objects.

task: Provide customers with change of address cards and other forms.
rationale: Physically handling and distributing physical forms and cards to customers requires manual interaction with tangible objects.

task: Report any unusual circumstances concerning mail delivery, including the condition of street letter boxes.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of letter boxes and street conditions; involves observing and assessing tangible objects on-site.

task: Travel to post offices to pick up the mail for routes or pick up mail from postal relay boxes.
rationale: Traveling to physical locations and physically picking up mail requires bodily movement and manual manipulation of objects.

task: Collect or prepare measurement, weight, or identification labels and attach them to products.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of labels and products; attaching labels is hands-on assembly work.

task: Weigh or measure materials, equipment, or products to maintain relevant records, using volume meters, scales, rules, or calipers.
rationale: Requires physical operation of measurement tools (scales, calipers, volume meters, rules) to weigh and measure tangible objects.

task: Collect product samples and prepare them for laboratory analysis or testing.
rationale: Collecting product samples requires physical manipulation and movement to obtain tangible objects. Preparing samples involves hands-on handling for lab testing.

task: Unload or unpack incoming shipments.
rationale: Unloading/unpacking requires physical manipulation of shipment contents and movement of tangible objects.

task: Fill orders for products and samples, following order tickets, and forward or mail items.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of items (picking, packing, mailing), reading order tickets, and handling tangible products and samples.

task: Sort products or materials into predetermined sequences or groupings for display, packing, shipping, or storage.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tangible objects—sorting, arranging, and handling materials for display, packing, shipping, or storage.

task: Store samples of finished products in labeled cartons and record their location.
rationale: Task involves physically handling and storing product samples in cartons, plus recording locations—primarily physical manipulation and organization.

task: Remove from stock products or loads not meeting quality standards, and notify supervisors or appropriate departments of discrepancies or shortages.
rationale: Physically inspecting and removing products from stock requires hands-on examination and manipulation of tangible items.

task: Maintain, monitor, and clean work areas, such as recycling collection sites, drop boxes, counters and windows, and areas around scale houses.
rationale: Task requires physical cleaning, maintenance, and monitoring of work areas—hands-on physical labor involving tangible objects and work sites.

task: Examine products or materials, parts, subassemblies, and packaging for damage, defects, or shortages, using specification sheets, gauges, and standards charts.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of tangible products, materials, and parts, using gauges and hands-on examination for damage and defects.

task: Transport materials, products, or samples to processing, shipping, or storage areas, manually or using conveyors, pumps, or hand trucks.
rationale: Involves physical manipulation and movement of tangible objects using manual effort or equipment like conveyors, pumps, and hand trucks.

task: Load machines with required input or output media, such as paper, cards, disks, tape, or Braille media.
rationale: Loading physical media (paper, cards, disks, tape, Braille) into machines requires hands-on physical manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Locate, measure, and mark site locations or placement of structures or equipment, using measuring and marking equipment.
rationale: Task requires physical presence on-site, using measuring equipment, and physically marking locations on the ground.

task: Provide assistance to workers engaged in construction or extraction activities, using hand tools or other equipment.
rationale: Assisting with construction/extraction using hand tools requires direct physical manipulation and operation of equipment at work sites.

task: Examine boilers, pressure vessels, tanks, or vats to locate defects, such as leaks, weak spots, or defective sections, so that they can be repaired.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of equipment on-site, examining for defects through hands-on assessment and direct observation.

task: Bolt or arc weld pressure vessel structures and parts together, using wrenches or welding equipment.
rationale: Task involves operating welding equipment and using wrenches to physically join vessel parts—primary execution is hands-on physical manipulation and tool operation.

task: Inspect assembled vessels or individual components, such as tubes, fittings, valves, controls, or auxiliary mechanisms, to locate any defects.
rationale: Physically examining vessels and components to locate defects requires hands-on inspection, manipulation, and visual/tactile assessment of tangible objects.

task: Repair or replace defective pressure vessel parts, such as safety valves or regulators, using torches, jacks, caulking hammers, power saws, threading dies, welding equipment, or metalworking machinery.
rationale: Task requires hands-on physical manipulation and operation of tools (torches, jacks, welding equipment) and machinery to repair/replace defective vessel parts.

task: Attach rigging and signal crane or hoist operators to lift heavy frame and plate sections or other parts into place.
rationale: Attaching rigging involves hands-on manipulation of physical objects and equipment to prepare heavy materials for lifting operations.

task: Bell, bead with power hammers, or weld pressure vessel tube ends to ensure leakproof joints.
rationale: Primary execution involves operating power hammers and welding equipment to physically manipulate and join tube ends—inherently hands-on manual work.

task: Lay out plate, sheet steel, or other heavy metal and locate and mark bending and cutting lines, using protractors, compasses, and drawing instruments or templates.
rationale: Laying out and marking heavy metal requires physical manipulation of materials, tools (protractors, compasses), and precise physical marking on tangible objects.

task: Install manholes, handholes, taps, tubes, valves, gauges, or feedwater connections in drums of water tube boilers, using hand tools.
rationale: Installing physical components (manholes, valves, gauges) on boiler drums using hand tools requires direct physical manipulation and operation of tools.

task: Straighten or reshape bent pressure vessel plates or structure parts, using hammers, jacks, or torches.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical manipulation using hammers, jacks, and torches to reshape bent metal parts through hands-on work.

task: Shape seams, joints, or irregular edges of pressure vessel sections or structural parts to attain specified fit of parts, using cutting torches, hammers, files, or metalworking machines.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of metalworking tools (cutting torches, hammers, files, machines) and direct hands-on shaping of tangible metal parts.

task: Position, align, and secure structural parts or related assemblies to boiler frames, tanks, or vats of pressure vessels, following blueprints.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of structural parts, positioning, aligning, and securing them to vessels—hands-on assembly work requiring bodily movement and tool operation.

task: Locate and mark reference points for columns or plates on boiler foundations, following blueprints and using straightedges, squares, transits, or measuring instruments.
rationale: Task requires physical use of measuring instruments (straightedges, squares, transits) and hands-on marking of reference points on boiler foundations on-site.

task: Shape or fabricate parts, such as stacks, uptakes, or chutes, to adapt pressure vessels, heat exchangers, or piping to premises, using heavy-metalworking machines such as brakes, rolls, or drill presses.
rationale: Task requires operating heavy-metalworking machines (brakes, rolls, drill presses) and physically shaping/fabricating metal parts—primary execution is hands-on machine operation and physical manipulation.

task: Clean pressure vessel equipment, using scrapers, wire brushes, and cleaning solvents.
rationale: Physical manipulation of tools (scrapers, brushes) and solvents on tangible equipment requires hands-on manual labor and bodily movement at the work site.

task: Install refractory bricks or other heat-resistant materials in fireboxes of pressure vessels.
rationale: Installing refractory bricks requires physical manipulation of materials, handling tools, and hands-on assembly work in fireboxes of pressure vessels.

task: Assemble large vessels in an on-site fabrication shop prior to installation to ensure proper fit.
rationale: Assembling large vessels requires physical manipulation and operation of tools/equipment in a fabrication shop setting.

task: Clean or prepare construction sites to eliminate possible hazards.
rationale: Requires physical labor to remove debris, organize materials, and manipulate objects at the work site to create safe conditions.

task: Dig ditches or trenches, backfill excavations, or compact and level earth to grade specifications, using picks, shovels, pneumatic tampers, or rakes.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (picks, shovels, rakes, tampers) and direct operation of equipment to physically alter the landscape.

task: Position, join, align, or seal structural components, such as concrete wall sections or pipes.
rationale: Positioning, joining, aligning, and sealing structural components requires physical manipulation and hands-on work with tangible objects.

task: Measure, mark, or record openings or distances to layout areas where construction work will be performed.
rationale: Task requires physical measurement, marking, and on-site inspection to layout construction areas—hands-on work with tools and physical presence.

task: Load, unload, or identify building materials, machinery, or tools, distributing them to the appropriate locations, according to project plans or specifications.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and movement of tangible objects (materials, machinery, tools) to specified locations on job sites.

task: Erect or dismantle scaffolding, shoring, braces, traffic barricades, ramps, or other temporary structures.
rationale: Erecting and dismantling structures requires physical manipulation of materials, tools, and assembly work at job sites.

task: Position or dismantle forms for pouring concrete, using saws, hammers, nails, or bolts.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (saws, hammers, nails, bolts) and forms to position or dismantle concrete forms—hands-on execution.

task: Lubricate, clean, or repair machinery, equipment, or tools.
rationale: Task requires hands-on physical manipulation and operation of tools to maintain, repair, or service machinery and equipment.

task: Operate jackhammers or drills to break up concrete or pavement.
rationale: Operating jackhammers/drills requires physical manipulation and operation of mechanical tools to break concrete/pavement.

task: Smooth or finish freshly poured cement or concrete, using floats, trowels, screeds, or powered cement finishing tools.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of tools (floats, trowels, screeds, powered tools) and manual manipulation of concrete surfaces.

task: Operate or maintain air monitoring or other sampling devices in confined or hazardous environments.
rationale: Requires physical operation and maintenance of sampling devices in hazardous environments, involving hands-on manipulation of equipment and physical presence at work sites.

task: Install sewer, water, or storm drain pipes, using pipe-laying machinery or laser guidance equipment.
rationale: Task requires operating pipe-laying machinery and physical installation of pipes at a work site.

task: Provide assistance to craft workers, such as carpenters, plasterers, or masons.
rationale: Assisting craft workers involves hands-on physical work like holding materials, handing tools, or helping manipulate objects during construction tasks.

task: Tend pumps, compressors, or generators to provide power for tools, machinery, or equipment or to heat or move materials, such as asphalt.
rationale: Operating and maintaining physical equipment (pumps, compressors, generators) requires hands-on operation of machinery and monitoring at the work site.

task: Mop, brush, or spread paints, cleaning solutions, or other compounds over surfaces to clean them or to provide protection.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (mop, brush) and direct application of liquids/compounds to surfaces.

task: Place, consolidate, or protect case-in-place concrete or masonry structures.
rationale: Placing, consolidating, or protecting concrete/masonry requires physical manipulation of materials, operation of tools, and hands-on work at job sites.

task: Mix, pour, or spread concrete, using portable cement mixers.
rationale: Requires operating portable cement mixers and physically manipulating concrete materials through mixing, pouring, and spreading.

task: Tend machines that pump concrete, grout, cement, sand, plaster, or stucco through spray guns for application to ceilings or walls.

task: Mix ingredients to create compounds for covering or cleaning surfaces.

task: Unload and position prefabricated steel units for hoisting, as needed.

task: Drive drift pins through rivet holes to align rivet holes in structural steel members with corresponding holes in previously placed members.

task: Dismantle structures or equipment.

task: Cut prefabricated sections of framework, rails, and other components to specified dimensions.

task: Place dough in pans, molds, or on sheets, and bake in production ovens or on grills.
rationale: Placing dough and operating ovens/grills requires physical manipulation of materials and operation of machinery.

task: Check the quality of raw materials to ensure that standards and specifications are met.
rationale: Physical inspection of materials requires hands-on examination, testing, and direct assessment of tangible objects against specifications.

task: Apply glazes, icings, or other toppings to baked goods, using spatulas or brushes.
rationale: Applying glazes and toppings with spatulas or brushes requires direct physical manipulation of tools and products on tangible objects.

task: Check equipment to ensure that it meets health and safety regulations, and perform maintenance or cleaning, as necessary.
rationale: Physical inspection, maintenance, and cleaning of equipment involves hands-on tangible work and hands-on physical inspection.

task: Decorate baked goods, such as cakes or pastries.
rationale: Decorating baked goods requires hands-on physical manipulation of icing, toppings, and pastry items to create designs and finishes.
