task: Set time and speed controls for mixing machines, blending machines, or steam kettles so that ingredients will be mixed or cooked according to instructions.
rationale: Setting controls on machinery requires hands-on operation of physical equipment (mixing/blending machines, steam kettles).

task: Operate slicing or wrapping machines.
rationale: Operating slicing/wrapping machines requires hands-on manipulation of equipment, feeding materials, adjusting settings, and controlling machinery.

task: Measure dimensions of finished workpieces to ensure conformance to specifications, using precision measuring instruments, templates, and fixtures.
rationale: Physical inspection of workpieces using precision measuring instruments requires hands-on manipulation and touching objects to verify specifications.

task: Remove and replace dull cutting tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools—removing and replacing dull cutting tools is hands-on equipment maintenance.

task: Mount, install, align, and secure tools, attachments, fixtures, and workpieces on machines, using hand tools and precision measuring instruments.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tools, attachments, and workpieces; using hand tools and precision instruments for mounting, alignment, and securing work.

task: Listen to machines during operation to detect sounds such as those made by dull cutting tools or excessive vibration, and adjust machines to compensate for problems.
rationale: Requires physical presence at work site, listening to and monitoring machines, then hands-on adjustment of physical machinery.

task: Adjust machine feed and speed, change cutting tools, or adjust machine controls when automatic programming is faulty or if machines malfunction.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of machine controls, changing cutting tools, and adjusting machinery—hands-on mechanical work.

task: Stop machines to remove finished workpieces or to change tooling, setup, or workpiece placement, according to required machining sequences.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of machines, tools, and workpieces during machining operations.

task: Lift workpieces to machines manually or with hoists or cranes.
rationale: Lifting workpieces requires physical manipulation of objects, either manually or via operating hoists/cranes—both are hands-on physical execution.

task: Insert control instructions into machine control units to start operation.
rationale: Inserting control instructions into physical machine control units requires hands-on physical manipulation and interaction with tangible equipment to initiate operations.

task: Check to ensure that workpieces are properly lubricated and cooled during machine operation.
rationale: Requires physical inspection and handling of workpieces and machine operations to check lubrication and cooling systems.

task: Set up and operate computer-controlled machines or robots to perform one or more machine functions on metal or plastic workpieces.
rationale: Requires physical operation of machinery/equipment and hands-on interaction with workpieces and controls.

task: Control coolant systems.
rationale: Controlling coolant systems requires operating physical machinery, adjusting valves, and monitoring equipment to manage fluid flow and temperature.

task: Maintain machines and remove and replace broken or worn machine tools, using hand tools.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of machines, hand tools, and direct replacement of parts—hands-on mechanical work.

task: Stack or load finished items, or place items on conveyor systems.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and placement of tangible objects (stacking, loading items onto conveyors).

task: Clean machines, tooling, or parts, using solvents or solutions and rags.
rationale: Cleaning machines and parts requires physical manipulation of objects, tools, rags, and solvents—direct hands-on manual labor.

task: Enter commands or load control media, such as tapes, cards, or disks, into machine controllers to retrieve programmed instructions.
rationale: Loading physical media (tapes, cards, disks) into machine controllers requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Examine electronic components for defects or completeness of laser-beam trimming, using microscopes.
rationale: Requires physical inspection using microscopes to examine tangible electronic components, involving hands-on inspection at a workstation.

task: Lay out and mark areas of parts to be shot peened and fill hoppers with shot.
rationale: Task involves physical marking of parts and manually filling hoppers with shot material—both require hands-on manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Enter coordinates of hole locations into program memories by depressing pedals or buttons of programmers.
rationale: Requires physical operation of pedals/buttons on programmer equipment to input coordinates.

task: Align and secure pattern film on reference tables of optical programmers, and observe enlarger scope views of printed circuit boards.
rationale: Aligning and securing physical pattern film requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects. Observing enlarger scopes involves physical inspection and operation of optical equipment at a workstation.

task: Verify conformance of machined work to specifications, using measuring instruments, such as calipers, micrometers, or fixed or telescoping gauges.
rationale: Physical inspection using measuring instruments requires hands-on handling of tangible objects and physical measurement work.

task: Install tools in spindles.
rationale: Installing tools in spindles requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and physical assembly work.

task: Change worn cutting tools, using wrenches.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tools (wrenches) and cutting tools; hands-on equipment maintenance.

task: Position and secure workpieces on tables, using bolts, jigs, clamps, shims, or other holding devices.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of workpieces and use of tools (bolts, clamps, jigs) to position and secure objects.

task: Move machine controls to lower tools to workpieces and to engage automatic feeds.
rationale: Operating machine controls involves physical manipulation of controls to lower tools and engage mechanical feeds on equipment.

task: Turn valves and direct flow of coolants or cutting oil over cutting areas.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of valves and directing fluid flow over work areas—direct manual operation of equipment and materials.

task: Operate single- or multiple-spindle drill presses to bore holes so that machining operations can be performed on metal or plastic workpieces.
rationale: Operating drill presses to bore holes requires hands-on physical operation of machinery and manipulation of tangible workpieces.

task: Establish zero reference points on workpieces, such as at the intersections of two edges or over hole locations.
rationale: Establishing reference points requires physical manipulation of workpieces and precision marking/measurement at specific locations.

task: Lift workpieces onto work tables either manually or with hoists or direct crane operators to lift and position workpieces.
rationale: Includes manual lifting and hoisting operations involving physical manipulation of workpieces, even when directing crane operators.

task: Lay out reference lines and machining locations on work, using layout tools, and applying knowledge of shop math and layout techniques.
rationale: Physically marks work with layout tools (scribes, rulers) requiring hands-on manipulation and precise physical placement.

task: Perform minor assembly, such as fastening parts with nuts, bolts, or screws, using power tools or hand tools.
rationale: Physical manipulation of parts with tools (fastening with nuts, bolts, screws using power or hand tools).

task: Verify that workpiece reference lines are parallel to the axis of table rotation, using dial indicators mounted in spindles.
rationale: Physical inspection and manipulation using dial indicators mounted in spindles to measure workpiece alignment. Requires hands-on interaction with precision measurement tools.

task: Operate tracing attachments to duplicate contours from templates or models.
rationale: Operating tracing attachments involves physical manipulation of tools and hands-on work to duplicate contours from physical templates or models.

task: Sharpen cutting tools, using bench grinders.
rationale: Operating a bench grinder to physically sharpen cutting tools requires hands-on machinery operation and manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Inspect sample workpieces to verify conformance with specifications, using instruments such as gauges, micrometers, and dial indicators.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and use of precision instruments (gauges, micrometers, dial indicators) to inspect tangible workpieces.

task: Adjust machine controls and change tool settings to keep dimensions within specified tolerances.
rationale: Requires physical operation of machine controls and tools to maintain manufacturing tolerances—hands-on manipulation of equipment.

task: Move controls to set cutting speeds and depths and feed rates, and to position tools in relation to workpieces.
rationale: Operating physical machine controls to manipulate cutting speeds, depths, feed rates, and tool positioning requires hands-on physical operation of machinery.

task: Start lathe or turning machines and observe operations to ensure that specifications are met.
rationale: Starting and operating lathe machines involves physical operation of machinery and observing physical work processes to verify specifications are met.

task: Crank machines through cycles, stopping to adjust tool positions and machine controls to ensure specified timing, clearances, and tolerances.
rationale: Requires physical operation of machinery controls and hands-on adjustment of tools and machine settings to achieve proper specifications.

task: Lift metal stock or workpieces manually or using hoists, and position and secure them in machines, using fasteners and hand tools.
rationale: Primary execution involves lifting, positioning, and securing physical objects using manual manipulation and hand tools.

task: Replace worn tools, and sharpen dull cutting tools and dies, using bench grinders or cutter-grinding machines.
rationale: Physically manipulating tools and operating bench grinders/cutter-grinding machines to replace and sharpen tools requires hands-on physical execution.

task: Position, secure, and align cutting tools in toolholders on machines, using hand tools, and verify their positions with measuring instruments.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of cutting tools, hand tools, and measuring instruments; hands-on positioning and securing of tangible objects.

task: Install holding fixtures, cams, gears, and stops to control stock and tool movement, using hand tools, power tools, and measuring instruments.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical assembly, using hand tools and power tools to install mechanical components. Direct physical manipulation of objects and tools.

task: Move toolholders manually or by turning handwheels, or engage automatic feeding mechanisms to feed tools to and along workpieces.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation: moving toolholders manually, turning handwheels, or engaging feeding mechanisms on machinery.

task: Turn valve handles to direct the flow of coolant onto work areas or to coat disks with spinning compounds.
rationale: Turning valve handles requires physical manipulation of tangible objects to direct coolant flow or apply compounds to work surfaces.

task: Mount attachments, such as relieving or tracing attachments, to perform operations, such as duplicating contours of templates or trimming workpieces.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of attachments and tools to duplicate contours and trim workpieces.

task: Collect samples, or signal workers to sample metal for analysis.
rationale: Collecting samples requires physical manipulation of materials; signaling workers involves physical presence and gestures at a work site.

task: Pour and regulate the flow of molten metal into molds and forms to produce ingots or other castings, using ladles or hand-controlled mechanisms.
rationale: Pouring molten metal requires physical manipulation of ladles/equipment and direct hands-on control of material into molds—core manual/physical work.

task: Read temperature gauges and observe color changes, adjusting furnace flames, torches, or electrical heating units as necessary to melt metal to specifications.
rationale: Requires physical operation of furnaces/torches, reading instruments, and direct manipulation of heating equipment to melt metal.

task: Examine molds to ensure they are clean, smooth, and properly coated.
rationale: Inspecting molds requires physical presence, direct observation, and hands-on tactile assessment of surface quality and condition.

task: Position equipment such as ladles, grinding wheels, pouring nozzles, or crucibles, or signal other workers to position equipment.
rationale: Task involves physically positioning equipment (ladles, grinding wheels, crucibles) or signaling workers—direct physical manipulation and workplace coordination.

task: Skim slag or remove excess metal from ingots or equipment, using hand tools, strainers, rakes, or burners, collecting scrap for recycling.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical manipulation of tools (hand tools, strainers, rakes, burners) to remove slag/metal and collect scrap materials.

task: Turn valves to circulate water through cores, or spray water on filled molds to cool and solidify metal.
rationale: Turning valves and operating cooling equipment require physical manipulation of controls and equipment at a work site.

task: Add metal to molds to compensate for shrinkage.
rationale: Physically adding metal to molds requires manual manipulation of molten or solid metal and direct physical interaction with molds.

task: Pull levers to lift ladle stoppers and to allow molten steel to flow into ingot molds to specified heights.
rationale: Pulling levers physically manipulates equipment to control molten steel flow into molds. This requires hands-on operation of physical machinery in a steel foundry.

task: Load specified amounts of metal and flux into furnaces or clay crucibles.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of materials (metal and flux) and operation of furnaces through direct manual loading.

task: Remove solidified steel or slag from pouring nozzles, using long bars or oxygen burners.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (long bars, oxygen burners) and handling tangible materials (steel, slag) at a work site.

task: Assemble and embed cores in casting frames, using hand tools and equipment.
rationale: Assembling and embedding cores requires hands-on physical manipulation of objects and operation of hand tools and equipment—direct physical work on tangible items.

task: Remove metal ingots or cores from molds, using hand tools, cranes, and chain hoists.
rationale: Physically removing ingots from molds using hand tools, cranes, and hoists requires direct physical manipulation and operation of equipment.

task: Transport metal ingots to storage areas, using forklifts.
rationale: Operating a forklift to transport physical objects (metal ingots) requires mechanical equipment operation and physical presence at the work site.

task: Stencil identifying information on ingots and pigs, using special hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of hand tools and marking objects (ingots/pigs), constituting manual work.

task: Repair and maintain metal forms and equipment, using hand tools, sledges, and bars.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation using hand tools, sledges, and bars to repair and maintain equipment.

task: Set up and operate machines, such as lathes, drill presses, punch presses, or bandsaws, to fabricate prototypes or models.
rationale: Task requires operating physical machinery (lathes, drill presses, punch presses, bandsaws) to fabricate prototypes—primary execution is hands-on machine operation.

task: Inspect and test products to verify conformance to specifications, using precision measuring instruments or circuit testers.
rationale: Physical inspection and hands-on use of precision measuring instruments and circuit testers on tangible products to verify specifications.

task: Cut, shape, and form metal parts, using lathes, power saws, snips, power brakes and shears, files, and mallets.
rationale: Task requires operating physical tools (lathes, saws, shears, files, mallets) and manipulating metal parts through cutting and shaping.

task: Lay out and mark reference points and dimensions on materials, using measuring instruments and drawing or scribing tools.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of measuring instruments and scribing tools on materials; hands-on marking and layout work.

task: Drill, countersink, and ream holes in parts and assemblies for bolts, screws, and other fasteners, using power tools.
rationale: Physical operation of power tools to manipulate parts—drilling, countersinking, reaming holes requires hands-on tool use and tangible object manipulation.

task: Grind, file, and sand parts to finished dimensions.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of grinding, filing, and sanding tools/machinery on tangible parts to achieve finished dimensions.

task: Rework or alter component model or parts as required to ensure that products meet standards.
rationale: Reworking or altering physical components and parts requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects to meet standards.

task: Align, fit, and join parts, using bolts and screws or by welding or gluing.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of parts and tools—bolting, screwing, welding, gluing—all hands-on assembly work.

task: Assemble mechanical, electrical, and electronic components into models or prototypes, using hand tools, power tools, and fabricating machines.
rationale: Assembling components uses hand tools, power tools, and machines—core physical execution with tangible objects.

task: Devise and construct tools, dies, molds, jigs, and fixtures, or modify existing tools and equipment.
rationale: Devising and constructing tools, dies, molds, jigs, and fixtures requires physical manipulation of materials, operation of machinery, and hands-on assembly work.

task: Wire and solder electrical and electronic connections and components.
rationale: Wiring and soldering are hands-on physical tasks requiring manipulation of components and operation of soldering tools.

task: Set up and operate machine tools, such as milling machines, lathes, drill presses, and grinders, to machine castings or patterns.
rationale: Operating machine tools requires hands-on manipulation and control of physical machinery to machine castings or patterns.

task: Verify conformance of patterns or template dimensions to specifications, using measuring instruments such as calipers, scales, and micrometers.
rationale: Requires physical use of measuring instruments (calipers, micrometers, scales) to inspect tangible objects against specifications.

task: Design and create templates, patterns, or coreboxes according to work orders, sample parts, or mockups.
rationale: Designing and creating physical templates, patterns, and coreboxes requires hands-on manipulation of materials and tools to fabricate tangible objects according to specifications.

task: Assemble pattern sections, using hand tools, bolts, screws, rivets, glue, or welding equipment.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of objects and operation of tools (hand tools, welding equipment) to assemble pattern sections.

task: Repair and rework templates and patterns.
rationale: Repairing and reworking templates/patterns requires physical manipulation and hands-on work with tangible materials.

task: Lay out and draw or scribe patterns onto material, using compasses, protractors, rulers, scribes, or other instruments.
rationale: Physical manipulation of tools (compass, protractor, scribe) and material to draw/mark patterns requires hands-on execution at the work surface.

task: Clean and finish patterns or templates, using emery cloths, files, scrapers, and power grinders.
rationale: Directly manipulates physical tools (emery cloths, files, scrapers, grinders) to finish tangible objects. Primary execution is hands-on physical work.

task: Construct platforms, fixtures, and jigs for holding and placing patterns.
rationale: Constructing platforms and fixtures requires physical manipulation of materials, building structures, and assembling components to create tangible objects.

task: Mark identification numbers or symbols onto patterns or templates.
rationale: Physically marking identification numbers/symbols onto patterns or templates requires manual manipulation and physical marking action.

task: Select pattern materials such as wood, resin, and fiberglass.
rationale: Selecting pattern materials involves physical handling and manipulation of tangible objects (wood, resin, fiberglass) to assess quality and suitability.

task: Apply plastic-impregnated fabrics or coats of sealing wax or lacquer to patterns used to produce plastic.
rationale: Applying fabrics, wax, or lacquer requires physical manipulation of materials and use of coating/application tools on tangible objects.

task: Paint or lacquer patterns.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of paint/lacquer and tools to apply patterns to surfaces.

task: Clean and smooth molds, cores, and core boxes, and repair surface imperfections.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and inspection of molds and cores to clean, smooth, and repair surface defects.

task: Move and position workpieces, such as mold sections, patterns, and bottom boards, using cranes, or signal others to move workpieces.
rationale: Involves physical operation of cranes or signaling workers to physically move and position heavy workpieces—direct physical manipulation via equipment or bodily gestures.

task: Sprinkle or spray parting agents onto patterns and mold sections to facilitate removal of patterns from molds.
rationale: Sprinkle and spray are physical actions requiring manual operation of tools (spray equipment) and direct handling of materials on patterns and molds.

task: Position patterns inside mold sections, and clamp sections together.
rationale: Positioning patterns and clamping mold sections requires physical manipulation and handling of tangible objects and tools.

task: Position cores into lower sections of molds, and reassemble molds for pouring.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of mold cores and molds—positioning, handling, and reassembling tangible objects for manufacturing processes.

task: Sift and pack sand into mold sections, core boxes, and pattern contours, using hand or pneumatic ramming tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of sand and operation of pneumatic tools to pack materials into molds and patterns.

task: Tend machines that bond cope and drag together to form completed shell molds.
rationale: Operating machines that physically bond shell mold components together requires hands-on machinery operation and physical oversight at the work site.

task: Cut spouts, runner holes, and sprue holes into molds.
rationale: Cutting spouts and holes into molds requires physical manipulation of tools and materials to shape tangible objects.

task: Lift upper mold sections from lower sections, and remove molded patterns.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of mold sections and molded patterns—lifting and removing tangible objects.

task: Form and assemble slab cores around patterns, and position wire in mold sections to reinforce molds, using hand tools and glue.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of materials (slab cores, wire, molds), use of hand tools and glue to assemble and position components. Requires hands-on execution.

task: Pour molten metal into molds, manually or with crane ladles.
rationale: Pouring molten metal into molds involves physical manipulation of hazardous materials and operation of crane ladles, requiring hands-on execution.

task: Rotate sweep boards around spindles to make symmetrical molds for convex impressions.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of boards and spindles to create molds through hands-on rotation and positioning.

task: Operate ovens or furnaces to bake cores or to melt, skim, and flux metal.
rationale: Operating ovens/furnaces involves hands-on physical operation of industrial equipment to control temperature and process materials.

task: Set up and operate or tend machines, such as furnaces, baths, flame-hardening machines, and electronic induction machines, that harden, anneal, and heat-treat metal.
rationale: Operating and tending metal heat-treating machines requires hands-on operation of physical machinery and monitoring equipment to harden and treat metals.

task: Remove parts from furnaces after specified times, and air dry or cool parts in water, oil brine, or other baths.
rationale: Task involves physical removal of parts from furnaces and immersion in cooling baths—direct manual manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Position stock in furnaces, using tongs, chain hoists, or pry bars.
rationale: Positioning stock in furnaces using tongs, chain hoists, and pry bars requires direct physical manipulation of tools and objects.

task: Adjust controls to maintain temperatures and heating times, using thermal instruments and charts, dials and gauges of furnaces, and color of stock in furnaces to make setting determinations.
rationale: Requires physical operation of furnace controls, thermal instruments, and direct observation of stock color—hands-on machinery operation and monitoring.

task: Load parts into containers and place containers on conveyors to be inserted into furnaces, or insert parts into furnaces.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of parts and containers, operating conveyors, and inserting items into furnaces.

task: Mount workpieces in fixtures, on arbors, or between centers of machines.
rationale: Mounting workpieces requires physical manipulation of objects and operation of machine fixtures/arbors.

task: Move controls to light gas burners and to adjust gas and water flow and flame temperature.
rationale: Physically manipulating controls and adjusting gas, water, and flame on equipment requires hands-on operation of machinery.

task: Test parts for hardness, using hardness testing equipment, or by examining and feeling samples.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of hardness testing equipment and hands-on examination/tactile assessment of samples.

task: Examine parts to ensure metal shades and colors conform to specifications, using knowledge of metal heat-treating.
rationale: Physical inspection of metal parts requires hands-on examination and visual assessment of shades, colors, and surface characteristics.

task: Repair, replace, and maintain furnace equipment as needed, using hand tools.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of furnace equipment using hand tools, repair and replacement work requiring hands-on mechanical execution.

task: Start conveyors and open furnace doors to load stock, or signal crane operators to uncover soaking pits and lower ingots into them.
rationale: Starting conveyors, opening furnace doors, physically loading stock, and signaling crane operations for loading ingots are hands-on physical operations in a steel mill environment.

task: Heat billets, bars, plates, rods, and other stock to specified temperatures preparatory to forging, rolling, or processing, using oil, gas, or electrical furnaces.
rationale: Operating furnaces and physically handling heated metal stock requires hands-on equipment operation and manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Clean oxides and scales from parts or fittings, using steam sprays or chemical and water baths.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of parts, operation of steam equipment, and handling of chemical baths—all hands-on physical work.

task: Stamp heat-treatment identification marks on parts, using hammers and punches.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tools (hammers and punches) to stamp marks on parts—direct hands-on manual execution.

task: Mount fixtures and industrial coils on machines, using hand tools.
rationale: Mounting fixtures and coils on machines requires physical manipulation of objects and hand tools.

task: Receive and mark articles for laundry or dry cleaning with identifying code numbers or names, using hand or machine markers.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of articles and operation of hand or machine markers to mark items—direct manual execution.

task: Start washers, dry cleaners, driers, or extractors, and turn valves or levers to regulate machine processes and the volume of soap, detergent, water, bleach, starch, and other additives.
rationale: Task involves operating physical machinery (washers, dryers, extractors) and manipulating controls (valves, levers) to regulate processes and add substances.

task: Sort and count articles removed from dryers, and fold, wrap, or hang them.
rationale: Task involves physically handling articles (removing, folding, wrapping, hanging) and manual counting.

task: Examine and sort into lots articles to be cleaned, according to color, fabric, dirt content, and cleaning technique required.
rationale: Requires physical inspection, touching, and sorting of tangible articles by examining color, fabric, dirt, and handling them into lots.

task: Load articles into washers or dry-cleaning machines, or direct other workers to perform loading.
rationale: Loading articles into machines requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and operation of machinery.

task: Mix and add detergents, dyes, bleaches, starches, and other solutions and chemicals to clean, color, dry, or stiffen articles.
rationale: Mixing and adding chemical solutions to articles is physical manipulation and handling of tangible substances and objects.

task: Clean machine filters, and lubricate equipment.
rationale: Cleaning and lubricating equipment requires direct physical manipulation of tangible machine parts and tools.

task: Remove items from washers or dry-cleaning machines, or direct other workers to do so.
rationale: Removing items from washers/dryers is physical manipulation of tangible objects. Directing others is supervisory communication, but the task emphasizes doing it yourself.

task: Operate extractors and driers, or direct their operation.
rationale: Operating equipment (extractors/driers) is hands-on machinery operation; directing operation supervises the physical work but the task includes direct operation.

task: Inspect soiled articles to determine sources of stains, to locate color imperfections, and to identify items requiring special treatment.
rationale: Physical inspection of articles requires examining, touching, and visually assessing items in person to identify stains and imperfections.

task: Spray steam, water, or air over spots to flush out chemicals, dry material, raise naps, or brighten colors.
rationale: Spraying steam, water, or air requires operating physical equipment (spray nozzles, machinery) and direct physical action to treat material surfaces.

task: Pre-soak, sterilize, scrub, spot-clean, and dry contaminated or stained articles, using neutralizer solutions and portable machines.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of articles, operation of portable cleaning machines, and hands-on scrubbing and drying.

task: Operate machines that comb, dry and polish furs, clean, sterilize and fluff feathers and blankets, or roll and package towels.
rationale: Primarily involves operating physical machinery and equipment (combing, drying, polishing, sterilizing machines) on tangible materials (furs, feathers, blankets, towels).

task: Spread soiled articles on work tables, and position stained portions over vacuum heads or on marble slabs.
rationale: Physical manipulation of soiled articles, positioning them on work surfaces. Hands-on execution with tangible objects.

task: Sprinkle chemical solvents over stains, and pat areas with brushes or sponges to remove stains.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of chemical solvents, brushes, and sponges, plus bodily movement to apply and pat stains.

task: Apply bleaching powders to spots and spray them with steam to remove stains from fabrics that do not respond to other cleaning solvents.
rationale: Task requires physical application of bleaching powder, handling spray equipment, and direct manipulation of fabrics with chemical treatments and steam.

task: Mix bleaching agents with hot water in vats, and soak material until it is bleached.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of bleaching agents and materials, operating vats, and hands-on chemical processing work.

task: Match sample colors, applying knowledge of bleaching agent and dye properties, and types, construction, conditions, and colors of articles.
rationale: Involves physical color matching of fabric samples using tangible bleaching agents and dyes. Requires hands-on manipulation and inspection of articles.

task: Identify articles' fabrics and original dyes by sight and touch, or by testing samples with fire or chemical reagents.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation (touching fabrics, testing samples with fire/chemicals), tactile inspection, and hands-on examination of tangible materials.

task: Iron or press articles, fabrics, and furs, using hand irons or pressing machines.
rationale: Requires physical operation of hand irons or pressing machines to manipulate fabrics and materials.

task: Hang curtains, drapes, blankets, pants, and other garments on stretch frames to dry.
rationale: Hanging garments on frames requires physical manipulation and arrangement of tangible items for drying.

task: Immerse articles in bleaching baths to strip colors.
rationale: Requires physical immersion of articles into bleaching baths—direct manipulation of tangible objects in liquid.

task: Monitor machine operation to detect problems such as defective stitching, breaks in thread, or machine malfunctions.
rationale: Requires physical presence and visual inspection of machine output and operation to detect defects, breaks, and malfunctions.

task: Position items under needles, using marks on machines, clamps, templates, or cloth as guides.
rationale: Positioning items under needles requires physical manipulation and precise hand placement using tools and guides to execute the task.

task: Place spools of thread, cord, or other materials on spindles, insert bobbins, and thread ends through machine guides and components.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of materials (spools, bobbins, thread) and operating machine components through hands-on setup.

task: Match cloth pieces in correct sequences prior to sewing them, and verify that dye lots and patterns match.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of cloth pieces, visual inspection, and hands-on matching before assembly.

task: Guide garments or garment parts under machine needles and presser feet to sew parts together.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical manipulation of garments under sewing machine needles and presser feet—hands-on operation of machinery and positioning of tangible objects.

task: Start and operate or tend machines, such as single or double needle serging and flat-bed felling machines, to automatically join, reinforce, or decorate material or articles.
rationale: Requires operation of physical sewing machines and handling of materials through hands-on machinery control.

task: Inspect garments, and examine repair tags and markings on garments to locate defects or damage, and mark errors as necessary.
rationale: Physical inspection of garments requires hands-on examination of textile items, handling them to examine defects, and making physical markings on them.

task: Mount attachments, such as needles, cutting blades, or pattern plates, and adjust machine guides according to specifications.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of machine attachments, installation of components, and hands-on adjustment of guides to specifications.

task: Cut excess material or thread from finished products.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and use of cutting tools on tangible objects (excess material/thread).

task: Inspect event facilities to ensure that they conform to customer requirements.
rationale: Inspecting facilities requires physical presence on-site to examine and touch/observe the venue to verify conformance with requirements.

task: Fold or stretch edges or lengths of items while sewing to facilitate forming specified sections.
rationale: Folding and stretching fabric edges while sewing requires physical manipulation of tangible materials and operation of sewing equipment.

task: Perform equipment maintenance tasks such as replacing needles, sanding rough areas of needles, or cleaning and oiling sewing machines.
rationale: Tasks involve physical manipulation of sewing equipment: replacing needles, sanding, cleaning, and oiling machines.

task: Examine and measure finished articles to verify conformance to standards, using rulers.
rationale: Physical inspection using rulers requires hands-on measurement of tangible objects to verify standards conformance.

task: Turn knobs, screws, and dials to adjust settings of machines, according to garment styles and equipment performance.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of machine controls (knobs, screws, dials) to adjust equipment settings.

task: Repair or alter items by adding replacement parts or missing stitches.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation—adding replacement parts and stitching—which is hands-on repair work.

task: Cut materials according to specifications, using blades, scissors, or electric knives.
rationale: Cutting materials requires physical manipulation of tools (blades, scissors, knives) to execute the task directly.

task: Remove holding devices and finished items from machines.
rationale: Physically removing items from machines requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects and mechanical components.

task: Position and mark patterns on materials to prepare for sewing.
rationale: Positioning and marking patterns requires physical manipulation of materials and marking tools on tangible fabric objects.

task: Position material or articles in clamps, templates, or hoop frames prior to automatic operation of machines.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation—positioning material into clamps, templates, and frames by hand before machine operation.

task: Attach tape, trim, appliques, or elastic to specified garments or garment parts, according to item specifications.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of garments and materials—attaching tape, trimming, applying appliques, or elastic to specified parts.

task: Perform specialized or automatic sewing machine functions, such as buttonhole making or tacking.
rationale: Operating sewing machinery and manipulating fabric for buttonhole making and tacking are physical actions requiring hands-on machine operation.

task: Attach buttons, hooks, zippers, fasteners, or other accessories to fabric, using feeding hoppers or clamp holders.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of fabric and accessories using equipment (hoppers, clamp holders) to attach fasteners—core manual/physical work.

task: Baste edges of material to align and temporarily secure parts for final assembly.
rationale: Basting involves physical manipulation and hand-sewing of material edges to temporarily secure parts, requiring hands-on execution at a work surface.

task: Tape or twist together thread or cord to repair breaks.
rationale: Physical manipulation of thread/cord using taping or twisting techniques to repair breaks requires hands-on manual work.

task: Draw markings or pin appliques on fabric to obtain variations in design.
rationale: Drawing markings and pinning appliques requires physical manipulation of fabric and tools (markers, pins) to create design variations.

task: Fit and study garments on customers to determine required alterations.
rationale: Fitting garments on customers requires physical handling of clothing, direct physical contact with the customer, and hands-on examination of fit and alterations needed.

task: Sew garments, using needles and thread or sewing machines.
rationale: Sewing garments requires physical manipulation of fabric, needles, thread, and operation of sewing machines.

task: Measure parts, such as sleeves or pant legs, and mark or pin-fold alteration lines.
rationale: Measuring garment parts and physically marking/pin-folding alteration lines requires hands-on manipulation of clothing and tools.

task: Take up or let down hems to shorten or lengthen garment parts, such as sleeves.
rationale: Hemming involves physical manipulation of fabric, threading needles, and hand-sewing or machine-sewing to alter garments. It requires hands-on work with tangible materials.

task: Let out or take in seams in suits and other garments to improve fit.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of garments and operation of sewing tools to alter seams.

task: Assemble garment parts and join parts with basting stitches, using needles and thread or sewing machines.
rationale: Physically handling garment parts, threading needles, and operating sewing machines requires manual manipulation and physical tool operation.

task: Remove stitches from garments to be altered, using rippers or razor blades.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of garments and operation of tools (rippers/razor blades) to remove stitches.

task: Record required alterations and instructions on tags, and attach them to garments.
rationale: Task involves physically handling garments, writing on tags, and attaching them—direct physical manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Examine tags on garments to determine alterations that are needed.
rationale: Examining tags requires physical inspection of garments—handling and inspecting tangible objects to assess alteration needs.

task: Fit, alter, repair, and make made-to-measure clothing, according to customers' and clothing manufacturers' specifications and fit, and applying principles of garment design, construction, and styling.
rationale: Physical manipulation of fabrics, garments, and tools (needles, shears, measuring tape) to fit, alter, repair, and construct custom clothing.

task: Maintain garment drape and proportions as alterations are performed.
rationale: Performing garment alterations requires physical manipulation of fabric and use of sewing tools.

task: Press garments, using hand irons or pressing machines.
rationale: Pressing garments requires operating hand irons or machines and physically manipulating clothing—direct manual work.

task: Trim excess material, using scissors.
rationale: Physical manipulation of tools (scissors) to cut material requires hands-on execution.

task: Make garment style changes, such as tapering pant legs, narrowing lapels, and adding or removing padding.
rationale: Tailoring tasks require hands-on physical manipulation of garments, cutting fabric, and sewing—direct manual execution with tools.

task: Measure customers, using tape measures, and record measurements.
rationale: Task involves physical use of tape measure to take customer measurements and hands-on body contact.

task: Repair or replace defective garment parts, such as pockets, zippers, snaps, buttons, and linings.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of garment parts—attaching, removing, and replacing components like zippers, buttons, and linings.

task: Position patterns of garment parts on fabric, and cut fabric along outlines, using scissors.
rationale: Positioning patterns and cutting fabric with scissors requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and operation of physical tools.

task: Sew buttonholes and attach buttons to finish garments.
rationale: Task requires hands-on manipulation of garments, sewing tools, and physical stitching of buttonholes and buttons.

task: Put in padding and shaping materials.
rationale: Installing padding and shaping materials requires physical manipulation of materials and hands-on placement/installation work.

task: Test patterns by making and fitting sample garments.
rationale: Making and fitting sample garments requires physical manipulation (cutting, sewing, assembling fabric) and hands-on fitting work.

task: Determine the best layout of pattern pieces to minimize waste of material, and mark fabric accordingly.
rationale: Requires physical layout of pattern pieces on fabric and marking/handling tangible materials.

task: Create a master pattern for each size within a range of garment sizes, using charts, drafting instruments, computers, or grading devices.
rationale: Task involves using drafting instruments, grading devices, and physical pattern creation—tangible object manipulation and tool operation.

task: Draw outlines of pattern parts by adapting or copying existing patterns, or by drafting new patterns.
rationale: Drawing outlines of pattern parts requires hands-on physical manipulation—using drafting tools, pencils, rulers, or working with physical materials to adapt or create patterns.

task: Position and cut out master or sample patterns, using scissors and knives, or print out copies of patterns, using computers.
rationale: Cutting patterns with scissors and knives is direct physical manipulation. Computer printing is secondary alternative method.

task: Mark samples and finished patterns with information, such as garment size, section, style, identification, and sewing instructions.
rationale: Physically labeling and marking tangible objects (samples and patterns) with written information requires hands-on manipulation and physical presence.

task: Trace outlines of paper onto cardboard patterns, and cut patterns into parts to make templates.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of paper and cardboard, tracing, and cutting—all hands-on activities using tools and objects.

task: Trace outlines of specified patterns onto material, and cut fabric, using scissors.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation—tracing patterns, handling scissors, and cutting fabric. Requires hands-on work with tangible materials.

task: Produce or assemble components of articles, such as store fixtures, office equipment, cabinets, or high-grade furniture.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical manipulation of components, assembly operations, and hands-on work with tangible objects.

task: Verify dimensions or check the quality or fit of pieces to ensure adherence to specifications.
rationale: Physical inspection requiring direct examination and measurement of tangible objects to verify fit and quality.

task: Set up or operate machines, including power saws, jointers, mortisers, tenoners, molders, or shapers, to cut, mold, or shape woodstock or wood substitutes.
rationale: Requires operation of physical machinery (power saws, jointers, mortisers, shapers) and physical manipulation of woodstock materials.

task: Measure and mark dimensions of parts on paper or lumber stock prior to cutting, following blueprints, to ensure a tight fit and quality product.
rationale: Physical marking and measuring of tangible materials (paper/lumber) using tools to prepare for fabrication.

task: Reinforce joints with nails or other fasteners to prepare articles for finishing.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of fasteners and tools to reinforce joints on tangible objects.

task: Attach parts or subassemblies together to form completed units, using glue, dowels, nails, screws, or clamps.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of parts, tools (glue, nails, screws, clamps), and assembly work—core manual operations.

task: Cut timber to the right size, and shape and trim parts of joints to ensure a snug fit, using hand tools, such as planes, chisels, or wood files.
rationale: Cutting timber, shaping parts, and trimming joints using hand tools (planes, chisels, files) requires direct physical manipulation and tool operation.

task: Match materials for color, grain, or texture, giving attention to knots or other features of the wood.
rationale: Requires physical inspection and manipulation of wood materials to examine and compare color, grain, texture, and features.

task: Trim, sand, or scrape surfaces or joints to prepare articles for finishing.
rationale: Trimming, sanding, and scraping are physical operations requiring hand tools and direct manipulation of surfaces.

task: Bore holes for insertion of screws or dowels, by hand or using boring machines.
rationale: Boring holes involves physically operating boring machines or hand tools to manipulate materials—core manual/physical work.

task: Perform final touch-ups with sandpaper or steel wool.
rationale: Using sandpaper or steel wool requires physical manipulation of tools and materials to prepare surfaces through hands-on finishing work.

task: Install hardware, such as hinges, handles, catches, or drawer pulls, using hand tools.
rationale: Installing hardware requires physical manipulation of objects using hand tools (hinges, handles, drawer pulls) and assembly work.

task: Repair or alter wooden furniture, cabinetry, fixtures, paneling, or other pieces.
rationale: Repairing and altering wooden furniture requires physical manipulation of wood, tools, and materials through hands-on work.

task: Apply Masonite, formica, or vinyl surfacing materials.
rationale: Applying surfacing materials requires physical manipulation, cutting, fitting, and installation of tangible materials onto surfaces.

task: Dip, brush, or spray assembled articles with protective or decorative finishes, such as stain, varnish, paint, or lacquer.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of articles and tools (brushes, sprayers) to apply finishes; hands-on execution with tangible objects.

task: Lay out patterns on wood stock and draw outlines of units, sectional patterns, or full-scale mock-ups of products, based on blueprint specifications and sketches, and using marking and measuring devices.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of wood, using marking/measuring devices, and drawing outlines—hands-on work with tangible materials.

task: Verify dimensions of completed patterns, using templates, straightedges, calipers, or protractors.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of templates, straightedges, calipers, and protractors to measure and inspect tangible patterns.

task: Set up, operate, and adjust a variety of woodworking machines such as bandsaws and lathes to cut and shape sections, parts, and patterns, according to specifications.
rationale: Requires operating and adjusting physical woodworking machinery (bandsaws, lathes) and physically manipulating wood to cut and shape materials.

task: Trim, smooth, and shape surfaces, and plane, shave, file, scrape, and sand models to attain specified shapes, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation using hand tools (planing, shaving, filing, scraping, sanding) to shape and smooth surfaces and models.

task: Fit, fasten, and assemble wood parts together to form patterns, models, or sections, using glue, nails, dowels, bolts, and screws.
rationale: Physical manipulation of wood parts, tools (nails, screws, bolts), and materials (glue) to assemble structures requires hands-on execution.

task: Correct patterns to compensate for defects in castings.
rationale: Correcting casting patterns involves physical manipulation of molds, patterns, and materials to adjust for defects—hands-on work.

task: Construct wooden models, templates, full scale mock-ups, jigs, or molds for shaping parts of products.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of wood, tools, and construction of tangible objects for manufacturing purposes.

task: Mark identifying information such as colors or codes on patterns, parts, and templates to indicate assembly methods.
rationale: Marking information on physical objects requires hands-on manipulation and physical marking of tangible items.

task: Finish completed products or models with shellac, lacquer, wax, or paint.
rationale: Applying shellac, lacquer, wax, or paint requires physical manipulation of finishing materials and tools on tangible products or models.

task: Glue fillets along interior angles of patterns.
rationale: Gluing fillets requires physical manipulation of materials and application of adhesive using hands and tools.

task: Select lumber to be used for patterns.
rationale: Selecting lumber involves physically examining and manipulating wood materials to choose appropriate pieces for use in patterns.

task: Repair broken or damaged patterns.
rationale: Repairing patterns requires physical manipulation of tangible objects (examining, adjusting, or reconstructing the patterns).

task: Divide patterns into sections according to shapes of castings to facilitate removal of patterns from molds.
rationale: Dividing patterns involves physical manipulation of tangible objects (patterns) and hands-on work at a work site to interact with molds and castings.

task: Collect and store patterns and lumber.
rationale: Collecting and storing lumber involves physical manipulation and handling of tangible materials and requires bodily movement at a work site.

task: Adjust saw blades, using wrenches and rulers, or by turning handwheels or pressing pedals, levers, or panel buttons.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (wrenches, rulers) and operating machinery controls (handwheels, pedals, levers, buttons) to adjust saw blades.

task: Inspect and measure workpieces to mark for cuts and to verify the accuracy of cuts, using rulers, squares, or caliper rules.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of workpieces and tools (rulers, squares, calipers) to inspect, measure, and mark them directly.

task: Set up, operate, or tend saws or machines that cut or trim wood to specified dimensions, such as circular saws, band saws, multiple-blade sawing machines, scroll saws, ripsaws, or crozer machines.
rationale: Setting up, operating, and tending saws requires direct operation of machinery and physical manipulation to cut/trim wood to specifications.

task: Inspect stock for imperfections or to estimate grades or qualities of stock or workpieces.
rationale: Physical inspection requires handling and examining tangible objects directly to identify imperfections and assess quality.

task: Operate panelboards of saw or conveyor systems to move stock through processes or to cut stock to specified dimensions.
rationale: Requires hands-on operation of machinery (panelboards, saws, conveyors) and physical control of stock movement and cutting processes.

task: Mount and bolt sawing blades or attachments to machine shafts.
rationale: Physically mounting and bolting blades to machine shafts requires hands-on manipulation of objects and tools.

task: Monitor sawing machines, adjusting speed and tension and clearing jams to ensure proper operation.
rationale: Adjusting machine speed/tension and clearing jams requires hands-on operation and physical manipulation of machinery.

task: Guide workpieces against saws, saw over workpieces by hand, or operate automatic feeding devices to guide cuts.
rationale: Guiding workpieces, hand-sawing, and operating feeding devices require direct physical manipulation of tools and objects to execute cuts.

task: Adjust bolts, clamps, stops, guides, or table angles or heights, using hand tools.
rationale: Physical manipulation of tangible objects (bolts, clamps, stops, guides) using hand tools requires direct manual execution.

task: Sharpen blades, or replace defective or worn blades or bands, using hand tools.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of blades/bands and hand tools; involves sharpening, replacing, and handling tangible objects.

task: Count, sort, or stack finished workpieces.
rationale: Counting, sorting, and stacking require physical manipulation and handling of tangible workpieces.

task: Lubricate or clean machines, using wrenches, grease guns, or solvents.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical manipulation of tools (wrenches, grease guns) and hands-on maintenance of tangible machinery.

task: Clear machine jams, using hand tools.
rationale: Clearing machine jams requires hands-on use of hand tools and physical manipulation to fix the machinery.

task: Dispose of waste material after completing work assignments.
rationale: Disposing of waste requires physical manipulation and handling of tangible materials, transporting them to disposal locations.

task: Measure and mark stock for cuts.
rationale: Requires physical measurement of tangible objects and marking them by hand for cutting.

task: Trim lumber to straighten rough edges or remove defects, using circular saws.
rationale: Requires operating a circular saw (physical tool) to physically manipulate lumber for trimming and shaping.

task: Position and clamp stock on tables, conveyors, or carriages, using hoists, guides, stops, dogs, wedges, or wrenches.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of stock using tools (hoists, wrenches, wedges) and direct physical positioning/clamping on equipment.

task: Cut grooves, bevels, or miters, saw curved or irregular designs, and sever or shape metals, according to specifications or work orders.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical manipulation of cutting tools and machinery to shape and cut metal materials according to specifications.

task: Unclamp and remove finished workpieces from tables.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation: unclamping devices and removing/carrying tangible workpieces from work surfaces.

task: Start machines, adjust controls, and make trial cuts to ensure that machinery is operating properly.
rationale: Requires operating physical machinery, adjusting controls, and making trial cuts—direct hands-on machine operation.

task: Feed stock through feed mechanisms or conveyors into planing, shaping, boring, mortising, or sanding machines to produce desired components.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials, feeding stock through machinery mechanisms—direct hands-on operation of equipment.

task: Adjust machine tables or cutting devices and set controls on machines to produce specified cuts or operations.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of machine controls, tables, and cutting devices to set up and execute manufacturing operations.

task: Monitor operation of machines and make adjustments to correct problems and ensure conformance to specifications.
rationale: Requires operating machinery controls and making physical adjustments to machines to maintain proper function and specifications.

task: Set up, program, operate, or tend computerized or manual woodworking machines, such as drill presses, lathes, shapers, routers, sanders, planers, or wood-nailing machines.
rationale: Operating machinery (drill presses, lathes, routers, sanders, etc.) requires physical operation and hands-on machine control.

task: Examine finished workpieces for smoothness, shape, angle, depth-of-cut, or conformity to specifications and verify dimensions, visually and using hands, rules, calipers, templates, or gauges.
rationale: Physical inspection using hands and tools (calipers, gauges, templates, rules) to examine tangible workpieces for quality and conformity.

task: Install and adjust blades, cutterheads, boring-bits, or sanding-belts, using hand tools and rules.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of tools and equipment parts (blades, bits, belts) using hand tools to install and adjust them.

task: Inspect and mark completed workpieces and stack them on pallets, in boxes, or on conveyors so that they can be moved to the next workstation.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of workpieces, manual marking, and handling/stacking them on pallets or conveyors.

task: Push or hold workpieces against, under, or through cutting, boring, or shaping mechanisms.
rationale: Involves physical manipulation of tangible workpieces against machinery (cutting, boring, shaping) requiring hands-on operation and bodily positioning.

task: Change alignment and adjustment of sanding, cutting, or boring machine guides to prevent defects in finished products, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of machine guides using hand tools to adjust alignment and prevent product defects.

task: Inspect pulleys, drive belts, guards, or fences on machines to ensure that machines will operate safely.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of tangible machine parts by being on-site, examining and touching pulleys, belts, guards, and fences.

task: Remove and replace worn parts, bits, belts, sandpaper, or shaping tools.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of tools and equipment—removing and replacing worn parts, bits, belts, sandpaper, and shaping tools.

task: Secure woodstock against a guide or in a holding device, place woodstock on a conveyor, or dump woodstock in a hopper to feed woodstock into machines.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of wood material—securing, placing on conveyor, and dumping into hopper to feed machines.

task: Clean or maintain products, machines, or work areas.
rationale: Cleaning and maintenance require physical manipulation of tools, equipment, and work areas to accomplish tangible improvements.

task: Attach and adjust guides, stops, clamps, chucks, or feed mechanisms, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and adjustment of tangible equipment components using hand tools.

task: Examine raw woodstock for defects and to ensure conformity to size and other specification standards.
rationale: Physical inspection of raw wood requires being present to examine, measure, and touch materials to assess defects and specifications.

task: Operate gluing machines to glue pieces of wood together, or to press and affix wood veneer to wood surfaces.
rationale: Operating gluing machines requires physical control of machinery to manipulate and glue wood pieces together.

task: Trim wood parts according to specifications, using planes, chisels, or wood files or sanders.
rationale: Trimming wood requires physical manipulation of tools (planes, chisels, files, sanders) and direct handling of tangible wood parts.

task: Unclamp workpieces and remove them from machines.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of workpieces and operation of machine clamping mechanisms.

task: Start machines and move levers to engage hydraulic lifts that press woodstocks into desired forms and disengage lifts after appropriate drying times.
rationale: Task involves starting machines, moving physical levers, and operating hydraulic lift equipment—all require hands-on physical operation of machinery.

task: Control hoists to remove parts or products from work stations.
rationale: Operating a hoist to remove parts requires physical control of machinery to move tangible objects from work stations.

task: Manipulate controls to adjust or activate power distribution equipment or machines.
rationale: Manipulating controls involves physical operation of equipment and machinery to adjust power distribution systems.

task: Inspect equipment to ensure that specifications are met or to detect any defects.
rationale: Physical inspection of equipment requires hands-on examination, touching, and seeing objects to detect defects and verify specifications.

task: Tend auxiliary equipment used in the power distribution process.
rationale: Tending equipment involves hands-on operation and maintenance of physical machinery in power distribution systems.

task: Start or stop generators, auxiliary pumping equipment, turbines, or other power plant equipment as necessary.
rationale: Operating power plant equipment (generators, turbines, pumps) requires physical manipulation of controls, switches, and machinery to execute the work.

task: Control or maintain auxiliary equipment, such as pumps, fans, compressors, condensers, feedwater heaters, filters, or chlorinators, to supply water, fuel, lubricants, air, or auxiliary power.
rationale: Controlling and maintaining physical equipment requires hands-on operation, adjustment, and servicing of machinery and tools.

task: Clean, lubricate, or maintain equipment, such as generators, turbines, pumps, or compressors, to prevent failure or deterioration.
rationale: Cleaning, lubricating, and maintaining equipment requires hands-on physical manipulation of machinery and tools.

task: Open and close valves and switches in sequence to start or shut down auxiliary units.
rationale: Operating physical valves and switches requires direct manipulation of tangible equipment objects in sequence to control auxiliary units.

task: Collect oil, water, or electrolyte samples for laboratory analysis.
rationale: Collecting samples requires physical manipulation—drawing, extracting, or handling tangible substances (oil, water, electrolytes) for lab testing.

task: Make adjustments or minor repairs, such as tightening leaking gland or pipe joints.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and repair of tangible objects (tightening joints, fixing leaks) using hands and tools.

task: Place standby emergency electrical generators on line in emergencies and monitor the temperature, output, and lubrication of the system.
rationale: Requires physical operation of equipment (placing generators on line) and hands-on monitoring of physical systems (checking gauges, inspecting temperature/lubrication).

task: Examine and test electrical power distribution machinery and equipment, using testing devices.
rationale: Requires physical operation of testing devices and hands-on inspection of electrical equipment and machinery.

task: Adjust controls to regulate temperature, pressure, feed, or flow of liquids or gases and times of prescribed reactions, according to knowledge of equipment and processes.
rationale: Operating physical controls on equipment to regulate industrial processes requires hands-on manipulation of machinery and direct physical interaction with equipment.

task: Control or operate equipment in which chemical changes or reactions take place during the processing of industrial or consumer products.
rationale: Requires operation of physical machinery and equipment where chemical reactions occur; hands-on control of industrial equipment.

task: Measure, weigh, and mix chemical ingredients, according to specifications.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of chemical materials, use of measurement tools, scales, and mixing equipment to prepare substances.

task: Inspect equipment or units to detect leaks or malfunctions, shutting equipment down, if necessary.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical inspection of equipment—checking for leaks, observing malfunctions, and manually shutting down equipment.

task: Patrol work areas to detect leaks or equipment malfunctions or to monitor operating conditions.
rationale: Physical presence at work site to inspect equipment and monitor operating conditions requires being there and observing tangible issues.

task: Test product samples for specific gravity, chemical characteristics, pH levels, concentrations, or viscosities, or send them to laboratories for testing.
rationale: Testing product samples requires hands-on manipulation of tangible materials, equipment operation, and physical measurement procedures. Sending samples to labs also involves physical handling of specimens.

task: Draw samples of products at specified stages so that analyses can be performed.
rationale: Drawing product samples at specified production stages involves physical manipulation and collection of tangible objects for testing purposes.

task: Add treating or neutralizing agents to products, and pump products through filters or centrifuges to remove impurities or to precipitate products.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of chemicals, operation of filters/centrifuges, and hands-on execution of manufacturing processes.

task: Open valves or start pumps, agitators, reactors, blowers, or automatic feed of materials.
rationale: Operating physical equipment (valves, pumps, reactors, etc.) requires hands-on manipulation of controls and machinery.

task: Drain equipment, and pump water or other solutions through to flush and clean tanks or equipment.
rationale: Task requires physically operating pumps and draining equipment—hands-on manipulation of tangible objects and equipment operation.

task: Make minor repairs, lubricate, and maintain equipment, using hand tools.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical manipulation with hand tools and direct equipment maintenance work.

task: Flush or clean equipment, using steam hoses or mechanical reamers.
rationale: Requires physical operation of steam hoses or mechanical reamers to manipulate and clean equipment—hands-on manual work.

task: Dump or scoop prescribed solid, granular, or powdered materials into equipment.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and scooping of materials into equipment—direct manual labor.

task: Set up or adjust machine controls to regulate conditions such as material flow, temperature, or pressure.
rationale: Physical manipulation of machine controls and gauges requires hands-on operation of equipment to regulate operational parameters.

task: Collect samples of materials or products for laboratory analysis.
rationale: Collecting samples requires physical manipulation and handling of tangible materials or products, and physical presence at the collection site.

task: Test samples to determine viscosity, acidity, specific gravity, or degree of concentration, using test equipment such as viscometers, pH meters, or hydrometers.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and operation of test equipment (viscometers, pH meters, hydrometers) to measure liquid properties.

task: Measure or weigh materials to be refined, mixed, transferred, stored, or otherwise processed.
rationale: Measuring or weighing materials requires physical manipulation of measurement tools and handling tangible materials at a work site.

task: Clean or sterilize tanks, screens, inflow pipes, production areas, or equipment, using hoses, brushes, scrapers, or chemical solutions.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (hoses, brushes, scrapers) and chemical solutions to clean/sterilize tangible equipment and work areas.

task: Inspect machines or equipment for hazards, operating efficiency, malfunctions, wear, or leaks.
rationale: Physical inspection requires hands-on examination of machines and equipment to identify hazards, leaks, wear, and malfunctions.

task: Dump, pour, or load specified amounts of refined or unrefined materials into equipment or containers for further processing or storage.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and loading of materials into equipment or containers; core execution is hands-on material handling.

task: Connect pipes between vats and processing equipment.
rationale: Connecting pipes requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and operation of hand tools to assemble equipment components.

task: Remove clogs, defects, or impurities from machines, tanks, conveyors, screens, or other processing equipment.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and hands-on operation to clean and clear equipment of blockages and impurities.

task: Install, maintain, or repair hoses, pumps, filters, or screens to maintain processing equipment, using hand tools.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical manipulation of equipment parts (hoses, pumps, filters, screens) and operation of hand tools to install, maintain, and repair.

task: Turn valves to pump sterilizing solutions or rinse water through pipes or equipment or to spray vats with atomizers.
rationale: Turning valves and operating atomizers requires physical manipulation of controls and equipment to manage fluid flow.

task: Remove full containers from discharge outlets and replace them with empty containers.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of containers—removing full ones and replacing with empty ones at discharge outlets.

task: Move controls to start, stop, or adjust machinery and equipment that crushes, grinds, polishes, or blends materials.
rationale: Operating physical machinery controls requires hands-on interaction with equipment to manipulate tangible materials through crushing, grinding, polishing, or blending processes.

task: Examine materials, ingredients, or products, visually or with hands, to ensure conformance to established standards.
rationale: Requires hands-on physical inspection and tactile examination of tangible objects to verify quality standards.

task: Weigh or measure materials, ingredients, or products at specified intervals to ensure conformance to requirements.
rationale: Weighing/measuring materials requires physical operation of scales/measurement tools and hands-on interaction with tangible objects.

task: Clean, adjust, and maintain equipment, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of equipment and operation of hand tools for maintenance work.

task: Set mill gauges to specified fineness of grind.
rationale: Requires physical adjustment and operation of mill machinery to set gauges to achieve specified grinding fineness.

task: Reject defective products and readjust equipment to eliminate problems.
rationale: Rejecting defective products and readjusting equipment involves physical manipulation and operation of machinery to identify and fix problems.

task: Clean work areas.
rationale: Cleaning requires physical manipulation and movement to remove dirt, organize spaces, and maintain work areas.

task: Transfer materials, supplies, and products between work areas, using moving equipment and hand tools.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation and operation of equipment to move materials, requiring hands-on work with moving equipment and hand tools.

task: Dislodge and clear jammed materials or other items from machinery and equipment, using hand tools.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of machinery and hand tools to dislodge and clear jammed materials.

task: Inspect chains, belts, or scrolls for signs of wear.
rationale: Inspecting machinery components requires physical presence to examine wear on chains, belts, and scrolls through direct observation and handling.

task: Tend accessory equipment, such as pumps and conveyors, to move materials or ingredients through production processes.
rationale: Operating pumps and conveyors requires hands-on management of physical equipment and machinery to move materials through production.

task: Test samples of materials or products to ensure compliance with specifications, using test equipment.
rationale: Task requires operating test equipment and physically handling material samples to conduct testing and inspection.

task: Collect samples of materials or products for laboratory testing.
rationale: Collecting samples requires physical handling and manipulation of tangible materials or products, often involving extraction, packaging, and transport.

task: Turn valves to regulate the moisture contents of materials.
rationale: Turning valves requires physical manipulation of tangible objects to control material moisture.

task: Load materials into machinery and equipment, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials and operation of hand tools to load equipment.

task: Add or mix chemicals and ingredients for processing, using hand tools or other devices.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials and hand tools/devices to mix and process chemicals and ingredients.

task: Weigh or measure materials, ingredients, or products to ensure conformance to requirements.
rationale: Weighing or measuring requires physical operation of scales/tools and handling tangible materials.

task: Test samples of materials or products to ensure compliance with specifications, using test equipment.
rationale: Requires operating test equipment and physically handling material samples for inspection and testing.

task: Operate or tend machines to mix or blend any of a wide variety of materials, such as spices, dough batter, tobacco, fruit juices, chemicals, livestock feed, food products, color pigments, or explosive ingredients.
rationale: Task requires operating physical machinery to mix/blend materials. Primary execution is hands-on machine operation and physical material handling.

task: Dump or pour specified amounts of materials into machinery or equipment.
rationale: Dumping or pouring materials requires physical manipulation of objects and operation of equipment to load them.

task: Stop mixing or blending machines when specified product qualities are obtained and open valves and start pumps to transfer mixtures.
rationale: Involves operating physical machinery (mixing/blending machines, valves, pumps) and monitoring for product quality to execute process steps.

task: Collect samples of materials or products for laboratory testing.
rationale: Collecting samples requires physical manipulation and handling of tangible materials or products to gather them for testing.

task: Add or mix chemicals or ingredients for processing, using hand tools or other devices.
rationale: Involves physical manipulation of chemicals/ingredients with hand tools and devices for processing work.

task: Examine materials, ingredients, or products visually or with hands to ensure conformance to established standards.
rationale: Physical inspection requires hands-on touching and visual examination of tangible materials to assess quality against standards.

task: Transfer materials, supplies, or products between work areas, using moving equipment or hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of objects and operation of moving equipment or hand tools to transfer materials between locations.

task: Tend accessory equipment, such as pumps or conveyors, to move materials or ingredients through production processes.
rationale: Tending equipment requires operating and monitoring physical machinery (pumps, conveyors) to process materials—hands-on operational work.

task: Compound or process ingredients or dyes, according to formulas.
rationale: Compounding or processing ingredients requires physical manipulation of materials, mixing, measuring, and handling tangible substances according to formulas.

task: Unload mixtures into containers or onto conveyors for further processing.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of materials into containers or onto conveyors; direct manual execution with tangible objects.

task: Clean and maintain equipment, using hand tools.
rationale: Cleaning and maintaining equipment with hand tools requires physical manipulation of objects and operation of tools.

task: Dislodge and clear jammed materials or other items from machinery or equipment, using hand tools.
