rationale: Primary execution requires physical manipulation of hand tools and tangible jammed materials to clear machinery—direct manual work.

task: Open valves to drain slurry from mixers into storage tanks.
rationale: Operating physical valves to drain material requires hands-on mechanical operation of equipment components.

task: Examine, measure, and weigh materials or products to verify conformance to specifications, using measuring devices, such as rulers, micrometers, or scales.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of measuring devices (rulers, micrometers, scales) and hands-on inspection of tangible materials/products.

task: Set up, operate, or tend machines that cut or slice materials, such as glass, stone, cork, rubber, tobacco, food, paper, or insulating material.
rationale: Task requires operating physical machinery to cut/slice materials. Primary execution involves hands-on machine operation and physical manipulation of equipment.

task: Stack and sort cut material for packaging, further processing, or shipping, according to types and sizes of material.
rationale: Stacking and sorting physical materials requires manual handling, manipulation, and organization of tangible objects.

task: Type instructions on computer keyboards, push buttons to activate computer programs, or manually set cutting guides, clamps, and knives.
rationale: Includes physical manipulation (setting guides/clamps/knives) and operation of machinery/equipment, not primarily cognitive or text-based work.

task: Remove defective or substandard materials from machines, and readjust machine components so that products meet standards.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of machine components and materials to remove and readjust parts.

task: Press buttons, pull levers, or depress pedals to start and operate cutting and slicing machines.
rationale: Operating cutting/slicing machines requires physical manipulation of controls (buttons, levers, pedals) and hands-on operation of equipment.

task: Adjust machine controls to alter position, alignment, speed, or pressure.
rationale: Adjusting machine controls requires physical manipulation of tangible control mechanisms and direct operation of machinery.

task: Monitor operation of cutting or slicing machines to detect malfunctions or to determine whether supplies need replenishment.
rationale: Requires physical presence and observation at equipment to detect problems and assess supplies—on-site machine monitoring.

task: Start machines to verify setups, and make any necessary adjustments.
rationale: Starting machines and making physical adjustments requires hands-on operation of equipment and tangible mechanical adjustments.

task: Remove completed materials or products from cutting or slicing machines, and stack or store them for additional processing.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials—removing items from machines, stacking, and storing them. Hands-on execution with tangible objects.

task: Select and install machine components, such as cutting blades, rollers, and templates, according to specifications, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of machine components and operation of hand tools to install parts per specifications.

task: Move stock or scrap to and from machines manually, or by using carts, handtrucks, or lift trucks.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and operation of material handling equipment like carts, handtrucks, and lift trucks to move stock or scrap.

task: Clean and lubricate cutting machines, conveyors, blades, saws, or knives, using steam hoses, scrapers, brushes, or oil cans.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (steam hoses, scrapers, brushes, oil cans) and hands-on maintenance of machinery equipment.

task: Feed stock into cutting machines, onto conveyors, or under cutting blades, by threading, guiding, pushing, or turning handwheels.
rationale: Involves physical manipulation of materials (feeding stock), operation of machinery (cutting machines, conveyors, handwheels), and hands-on execution.

task: Position stock along cutting lines, or against stops on beds of scoring or cutting machines.
rationale: Positioning stock requires physical manipulation of materials and interaction with machinery equipment at the work site.

task: Change or replace saw blades, cables, cutter heads, and grinding wheels, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of equipment parts (blades, cables, heads, wheels) using hand tools to replace/change them.

task: Mark cutting lines or identifying information on stock, using marking pencils, rulers, or scribes.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tools (pencils, rulers, scribes) and marking tangible objects (stock) by hand.

task: Turn cranks or press buttons to activate winches that move cars under sawing cables or saw frames.
rationale: Physical operation of machinery (cranks, buttons, winches) to move cars; requires hands-on control of equipment.

task: Position width gauge blocks between blades, and level blades and insert wedges into frames to secure blades to frames.
rationale: Task involves physically positioning gauge blocks, leveling blades, and inserting wedges—all tangible object manipulation and assembly work.

task: Tighten pulleys or add abrasives to maintain cutting speeds.
rationale: Tightening pulleys and adding abrasives require physical manipulation of equipment parts to maintain machinery.

task: Cut stock manually to prepare for machine cutting, using tools such as knives, cleavers, handsaws, or hammers and chisels.
rationale: Cutting stock manually involves direct physical manipulation using hand tools like knives, cleavers, handsaws, and chisels on tangible materials.

task: Sharpen cutting blades, knives, or saws, using files, bench grinders, or honing stones.
rationale: Physical manipulation of tools (files, grinders, stones) to sharpen blades requires hands-on execution and direct tool operation.

task: Adjust machine components to regulate speeds, pressures, and temperatures, and amounts, dimensions, and flow of materials or ingredients.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of machine components—adjusting controls, levers, gauges, and dials to regulate operational parameters.

task: Examine, measure, and weigh materials or products to verify conformance to standards, using measuring devices such as templates, micrometers, or scales.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of measurement devices (micrometers, scales, templates) and direct handling of materials to inspect and measure them.

task: Monitor machine operations and observe lights and gauges to detect malfunctions.
rationale: Requires physical presence at work site to visually inspect lights and gauges on machines to detect malfunctions.

task: Press control buttons to activate machinery and equipment.
rationale: Operating control buttons to activate machinery is direct physical operation of equipment.

task: Turn controls to adjust machine functions, such as regulating air pressure, creating vacuums, and adjusting coolant flow.
rationale: Turning controls requires physical manipulation of machine interfaces to operate machinery functions.

task: Select and install machine components, such as dies, molds, and cutters, according to specifications, using hand tools and measuring devices.
rationale: Requires physical installation of machine components using hand tools and measuring devices; direct manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Feed products into machines by hand or conveyor.
rationale: Feeding products into machines requires physical handling of tangible objects and operation of machinery.

task: Clear jams, and remove defective or substandard materials or products.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of equipment to clear jams and hands-on removal of defective materials/products from machinery or production lines.

task: Move materials, supplies, components, and finished products between storage and work areas, using work aids such as racks, hoists, and handtrucks.
rationale: Moving materials and products using work aids requires physical manipulation and operation of equipment like hoists and handtrucks.

task: Swab molds with solutions to prevent products from sticking.
rationale: Physically applying solutions to molds requires hands-on manipulation of swabs and tangible objects.

task: Complete work tickets, and place them with products.
rationale: Completing work tickets and placing them with products involves physical manipulation of tangible objects and physical presence to organize/place items.

task: Activate machines to shape or form products, such as candy bars, light bulbs, balloons, or insulation panels.
rationale: Operating physical machinery to shape/form tangible products requires hands-on operation of industrial equipment.

task: Remove molds, mold components, and feeder tubes from machinery after production is complete.
rationale: Removing molds and components from machinery requires physical manipulation and handling of tangible objects.

task: Remove materials or products from molds or from extruding, forming, pressing, or compacting machines, and stack or store them for additional processing.
rationale: Task requires physical removal of materials from machines and manual stacking/storing—primary execution is hands-on physical manipulation.

task: Measure, mix, cut, shape, soften, and join materials and ingredients, such as powder, cornmeal, or rubber to prepare them for machine processing.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials (measuring, mixing, cutting, shaping, joining) and handling tangible objects to prepare them for processing.

task: Send product samples to laboratories for analysis.
rationale: Requires physical handling and transport of product samples to laboratories—tangible object manipulation.

task: Clean dies, arbors, compression chambers, and molds, using swabs, sponges, or air hoses.
rationale: Physical manipulation of tools (swabs, sponges, air hoses) to clean tangible objects (dies, molds, chambers).

task: Disassemble equipment to repair it or to replace parts, such as nozzles, punches, and filters.
rationale: Disassembling equipment requires hands-on physical manipulation of tangible parts and tools to access and remove components.

task: Pour, scoop, or dump specified ingredients, metal assemblies, or mixtures into sections of machine prior to starting machines.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and pouring/scooping/dumping materials into machines before operation.

task: Install, align, and adjust neck rings, press plungers, and feeder tubes.
rationale: Installing, aligning, and adjusting physical equipment components requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects and mechanical assembly work.

task: Couple air and gas lines to machines to maintain plasticity of material and to regulate solidification of final products.
rationale: Coupling lines requires physical manipulation of equipment and tools to connect air and gas lines to machines.

task: Measure arbors and dies to verify sizes specified on work tickets.
rationale: Physical inspection and measurement of tangible objects (arbors and dies) using tools/instruments at the worksite.

task: Discard or reject products, materials, or equipment not meeting specifications.
rationale: Requires physical inspection and handling of products/materials to assess quality and manually discard/reject items not meeting specifications.

task: Inspect, test, or measure materials, products, installations, or work for conformance to specifications.
rationale: Inspecting, testing, and measuring materials/products requires physical presence, hands-on examination, and direct interaction with tangible objects or installations.

task: Measure dimensions of products to verify conformance to specifications, using measuring instruments, such as rulers, calipers, gauges, or micrometers.
rationale: Requires physical use of measuring instruments (rulers, calipers, gauges, micrometers) and hands-on inspection of products to verify specifications.

task: Collect or select samples for testing or for use as models.
rationale: Collecting or selecting samples requires physical handling and manipulation of tangible objects for testing or modeling purposes.

task: Compare colors, shapes, textures, or grades of products or materials with color charts, templates, or samples to verify conformance to standards.
rationale: Comparing colors, shapes, textures by viewing and touching physical samples requires hands-on visual and tactile inspection at the work site.

task: Read dials or meters to verify that equipment is functioning at specified levels.
rationale: Requires physical inspection and reading of instruments to monitor equipment status at the work site.

task: Remove defects, such as chips, burrs, or lap corroded or pitted surfaces.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of objects (removing chips, burrs, corrosion) using tools or hand techniques on tangible surfaces.

task: Adjust, clean, or repair products or processing equipment to correct defects found during inspections.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of equipment and products, hands-on adjustment, cleaning, and repair work.

task: Fabricate, install, position, or connect components, parts, finished products, or instruments for testing or operational purposes.
rationale: Fabricating, installing, positioning, and connecting physical components requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects and tools.

task: Grade, classify, or sort products according to sizes, weights, colors, or other specifications.
rationale: Grading and sorting products requires physical handling, inspection, and manipulation of tangible items based on visible specifications.

task: Make minor adjustments to equipment, such as turning setscrews to calibrate instruments to required tolerances.
rationale: Turning setscrews to calibrate instruments requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and tools to execute.

task: Observe machine gauges and equipment operation to detect defects or deviations from standards, and make adjustments as necessary.
rationale: Requires physical presence to observe gauges and equipment, and hands-on adjustments to correct deviations from standards.

task: Determine paint flow, viscosity, and coating quality by performing visual inspections, or by using viscometers.
rationale: Requires physical inspection, hands-on use of viscometers, and visual assessment of tangible paint samples.

task: Weigh or measure chemicals, coatings, or paints before adding them to machines.
rationale: Weighing/measuring chemicals involves physical manipulation of tangible materials and operation of measurement tools before adding to machines.

task: Turn dials, handwheels, valves, or switches to regulate conveyor speeds, machine temperature, air pressure and circulation, and the flow or spray of coatings or paints.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of dials, handwheels, valves, and switches to operate and control machinery equipment.

task: Start and stop operation of machines, using levers or buttons.
rationale: Operating machines via levers or buttons is direct physical manipulation of controls to execute equipment operation.

task: Operate auxiliary machines or equipment used in coating or painting processes.
rationale: Operating coating/painting equipment requires physical manipulation and control of machinery in production or work processes.

task: Fill hoppers, reservoirs, troughs, or pans with material used to coat, paint, or spray, using conveyors or pails.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of materials using conveyors or pails to fill containers. Requires hands-on material handling.

task: Thread or feed items or products through or around machine rollers and dryers.
rationale: Physical manipulation of materials being threaded through machinery rollers and dryers requires hands-on operation.

task: Attach hoses or nozzles to machines, using wrenches and pliers, and make adjustments to obtain the proper dispersion of spray.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of objects (hoses, nozzles, machines) using tools (wrenches, pliers) and hands-on adjustments.

task: Remove materials, parts, or workpieces from painting or coating machines, using hand tools.
rationale: Removing materials from machines using hand tools requires physical manipulation and operation of tools.

task: Examine, measure, weigh, or test sample products to ensure conformance to specifications.
rationale: Examining, measuring, weighing, and testing products requires physical handling of tangible objects and hands-on inspection to verify specifications.

task: Hold or position spray guns to direct spray onto articles.
rationale: Positioning and holding spray guns requires direct physical manipulation and operation of tools.

task: Apply coatings, such as paint, ink, or lacquer, to protect or decorate workpiece surfaces, using spray guns, pens, or brushes.
rationale: Applying coatings requires physical manipulation of tools (spray guns, pens, brushes) and direct contact with workpiece surfaces to paint, ink, or lacquer them.

task: Examine finished surfaces of workpieces to verify conformance to specifications and retouch any defective areas.
rationale: Requires physical inspection and hands-on touching of workpieces, plus manual retouching of defective areas.

task: Clean and maintain tools and equipment, using solvents, brushes, and rags.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tools, solvents, brushes, and rags to clean and maintain equipment.

task: Read job orders and inspect workpieces to determine work procedures and materials required.
rationale: Inspecting workpieces requires physical examination and hands-on assessment of tangible objects to determine procedures and materials.

task: Clean surfaces of workpieces in preparation for coating, using cleaning fluids, solvents, brushes, scrapers, steam, sandpaper, or cloth.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (brushes, scrapers, sandpaper) and direct handling of workpieces and cleaning materials to prepare surfaces.

task: Rinse, drain, or wipe coated workpieces to remove excess coating material or to facilitate setting of finish coats on workpieces.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of workpieces and use of tools (rinsing, draining, wiping) to remove coating material.

task: Place coated workpieces in ovens or dryers for specified times to dry or harden finishes.
rationale: Placing workpieces into ovens/dryers requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and operation of equipment.

task: Select and mix ingredients to prepare coating substances according to specifications, using paddles or mechanical mixers.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of ingredients and operation of mechanical mixers to prepare coating substances per specifications.

task: Conceal blemishes in workpieces, such as nicks and dents, using fillers such as putty.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of materials (applying putty/fillers to workpieces) and hands-on surface treatment.

task: Manipulate valves, switches, and buttons, or key commands into control panels to start semiconductor processing cycles.
rationale: Physical manipulation of valves, switches, and buttons on control panels requires direct manual operation of equipment controls.

task: Inspect materials, components, or products for surface defects and measure circuitry, using electronic test equipment, precision measuring instruments, microscope, and standard procedures.
rationale: Requires physical inspection using microscopes and precision instruments, plus hands-on operation of electronic test equipment to examine defects.

task: Clean semiconductor wafers using cleaning equipment, such as chemical baths, automatic wafer cleaners, or blow-off wands.
rationale: Requires physical operation of cleaning equipment and manipulation of wafers, chemical handling, and hands-on equipment operation.

task: Load and unload equipment chambers and transport finished product to storage or to area for further processing.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation (loading, unloading), handling equipment, and transporting products—all manual execution.

task: Clean and maintain equipment, including replacing etching and rinsing solutions and cleaning bath containers and work area.
rationale: Physical manipulation of equipment, cleaning supplies, and containers; handling and replacing solutions involves hands-on work with tangible objects.

task: Place semiconductor wafers in processing containers or equipment holders, using vacuum wand or tweezers.
rationale: Placing wafers requires physical manipulation of delicate objects using tools (vacuum wand or tweezers), a hands-on manual operation.

task: Set, adjust, and readjust computerized or mechanical equipment controls to regulate power level, temperature, vacuum, and rotation speed of furnace, according to crystal growing specifications.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of equipment controls and dials to regulate furnace settings according to specifications.

task: Etch, lap, polish, or grind wafers or ingots to form circuitry and change conductive properties, using etching, lapping, polishing, or grinding equipment.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of equipment (etching, grinding, polishing machinery) to manipulate wafers/ingots and change their properties through hands-on processing.

task: Load semiconductor material into furnace.
rationale: Loading material into a furnace requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and operation of equipment.

task: Count, sort, and weigh processed items.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation (weighing items, handling), inspection, and use of tools (scales).

task: Inspect equipment for leaks, diagnose malfunctions, and request repairs.
rationale: Inspecting equipment for leaks requires physical examination and hands-on interaction with tangible objects.

task: Align photo mask pattern on photoresist layer, expose pattern to ultraviolet light, and develop pattern, using specialized equipment.
rationale: Physical operation of specialized photolithography equipment requiring hands-on manipulation of materials and precise equipment control.

task: Stamp, etch, or scribe identifying information on finished component according to specifications.
rationale: Stamping, etching, or scribing requires physical manipulation of tools and components to mark finished goods per specifications.

task: Scribe or separate wafers into dice.
rationale: Scribing and separating wafers into dice requires physical manipulation of semiconductor wafers using tools and specialized equipment.

task: Connect reactor to computer, using hand tools and power tools.
rationale: Task requires hands-on operation of hand tools and power tools to physically connect reactor components—primary execution is manual/physical manipulation.

task: Measure and weigh amounts of crystal growing materials, mix and grind materials, load materials into container, and monitor processing procedures to help identify crystal growing problems.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation: measuring, weighing, mixing, grinding materials; loading into containers; hands-on monitoring of physical processes.

task: Examine and measure completed materials or products to verify conformance to specifications, using measuring devices such as tape measures, gauges, or calipers.
rationale: Physical inspection and measurement of tangible products using hands-on measuring devices requires direct physical presence and manipulation.

task: Adjust machine components according to specifications such as widths, lengths, and thickness of materials and amounts of glue, cement, or adhesive required.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of machine components, measuring materials, and applying adhesives—hands-on work with tangible objects.

task: Fill machines with glue, cement, or adhesives.
rationale: Filling machines requires physical manipulation of materials and operation of equipment, a hands-on tangible task.

task: Mount or load material such as paper, plastic, wood, or rubber in feeding mechanisms of cementing or gluing machines.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of materials and operation of machinery feeding mechanisms—clear hands-on work.

task: Start machines, and turn valves or move controls to feed, admit, apply, or transfer materials and adhesives, and to adjust temperature, pressure, and time settings.
rationale: Task involves starting machines and physically operating valves/controls to manipulate materials and adjust settings.

task: Perform test production runs and make adjustments as necessary to ensure that completed products meet standards and specifications.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of production machinery, hands-on testing of products, and making mechanical/physical adjustments to ensure quality standards.

task: Remove jammed materials from machines and readjust components as necessary to resume normal operations.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and adjustment of machine components to clear jams and restore operations.

task: Align and position materials being joined to ensure accurate application of adhesive or heat sealing.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and positioning of materials; hands-on work with tangible objects for adhesive or heat sealing application.

task: Observe gauges, meters, and control panels to obtain information about equipment temperatures and pressures, or the speed of feeders or conveyors.
rationale: Requires physical presence at work site to visually inspect gauges, meters, and control panels; direct observation of physical equipment.

task: Remove and stack completed materials or products, and restock materials to be joined.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation: removing, stacking products, and restocking materials—all hands-on physical actions.

task: Clean and maintain gluing and cementing machines, using solutions, lubricants, brushes, and scrapers.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tools (brushes, scrapers), applying solutions/lubricants, and hands-on equipment maintenance work.

task: Transport materials, supplies, and finished products between storage and work areas, using forklifts.
rationale: Operating a forklift to physically move materials and products is direct equipment operation involving tangible object manipulation.

task: Measure and mix ingredients to prepare glue.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of ingredients, measuring tools, and mixing—tangible objects and physical execution.

task: Carve designs or figures in full or bas relief on stone, employing knowledge of stone carving techniques and sense of artistry to produce carvings consistent with designers' plans.
rationale: Carving designs in stone requires physical manipulation of tools and materials, hands-on execution of specialized techniques on tangible stone objects.

task: Verify depths and dimensions of cuts or carvings to ensure adherence to specifications, blueprints, or models, using measuring instruments.
rationale: Requires physical measurement of cuts/carvings using measuring instruments, necessitating hands-on inspection and direct work site presence.

task: Lay out designs or dimensions from sketches or blueprints on stone surfaces, freehand or by transferring them from tracing paper, using scribes or chalk and measuring instruments.
rationale: Physical manipulation of stone surfaces, tools (scribes, chalk), and measuring instruments to transfer and mark designs.

task: Drill holes and cut or carve moldings and grooves in stone, according to diagrams and patterns.
rationale: Drilling holes and cutting/carving stone requires physical operation of tools and manipulation of tangible materials.

task: Shape, trim, or touch up roughed-out designs with appropriate tools to finish carvings.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (chisels, files, etc.) and direct hands-on work shaping and finishing carved objects.

task: Select chisels, pneumatic or surfacing tools, or sandblasting nozzles, and determine sequence of use.
rationale: Selecting and operating physical tools (chisels, pneumatic tools, sandblasting nozzles) and planning their use sequence involves hands-on tool manipulation.

task: Move fingers over surfaces of carvings to ensure smoothness of finish.
rationale: Physical tactile inspection using hands to feel carved surfaces and assess texture/smoothness.

task: Carve rough designs freehand or by chipping along marks on stone, using mallets and chisels or pneumatic tools.
rationale: Direct physical manipulation of stone with hand tools (mallets, chisels) and pneumatic equipment requires hands-on operation and bodily engagement.

task: Guide nozzles over stone, following stencil outlines, or chip along marks to create designs or to work surfaces down to specified finishes.
rationale: Guiding nozzles and chipping stone requires hands-on physical manipulation of tools and materials to shape stone surfaces.

task: Cut, shape, and finish rough blocks of building or monumental stone, according to diagrams or patterns.
rationale: Cutting, shaping, and finishing stone blocks requires hands-on physical manipulation and operation of tools to work with tangible materials.

task: Smooth surfaces of carvings, using rubbing stones.
rationale: Smoothing carved surfaces with rubbing stones requires hands-on physical manipulation of tools to process tangible objects.

task: Remove or add stencils during blasting to create differing cut depths, intricate designs, or rough, pitted finishes.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of stencils during active blasting operations to control surface effects on workpieces.

task: Copy drawings on rough clay or plaster models.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of clay/plaster models and hand-drawing on tangible objects.

task: Load sandblasting equipment with abrasives, attach nozzles to hoses, and turn valves to admit compressed air and activate jets.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of equipment (loading abrasives, attaching nozzles), operating machinery controls (valves), and setting up equipment for operation.

task: Dress stone surfaces, using bushhammers.
rationale: Dressing stone with bushhammers requires physical tool operation and manipulation of stone surfaces through direct hand-held tool use.

task: Inspect, weigh, and measure products to verify conformance to specifications, using instruments such as micrometers, calipers, magnifiers, or rulers.
rationale: Physical inspection requires hands-on use of instruments (micrometers, calipers, magnifiers, rulers) to directly examine and measure tangible products.

task: Heat glass to pliable stage, using gas flames or ovens and rotating glass to heat it uniformly.
rationale: Requires operating gas flames/ovens and physically rotating glass, which are manual equipment operation and physical manipulation tasks.

task: Blow tubing into specified shapes to prevent glass from collapsing, using compressed air or own breath, or blow and rotate gathers in molds or on boards to obtain final shapes.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of glass using breath/compressed air and direct manipulation to shape, requiring hands-on execution.

task: Shape, bend, or join sections of glass, using paddles, pressing and flattening hand tools, or cork.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of glass and operation of hand tools like paddles and pressing tools to shape and join sections.

task: Place rubber hoses on ends of tubing and charge tubing with gas.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of hoses and tubing, and operating gas charging equipment.

task: Superimpose bent tubing on asbestos patterns to ensure accuracy.
rationale: Physical manipulation and positioning of bent tubing onto asbestos patterns requires hands-on physical work and manual inspection.

task: Spray or swab molds with oil solutions to prevent adhesion of glass.
rationale: Physical manipulation of molds and spraying/swabbing with oil solutions requires hands-on work with tangible objects and physical tools.

task: Cut lengths of tubing to specified sizes, using files or cutting wheels.
rationale: Requires physical operation of cutting tools (files or cutting wheels) to manipulate tubing material.

task: Place glass into dies or molds of presses and control presses to form products, such as glassware components or optical blanks.
rationale: Placing glass into molds and operating presses requires physical manipulation of objects and machinery operation.

task: Design and create glass objects, using blowpipes and artisans' hand tools and equipment.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical manipulation of molten glass using blowpipes and hand tools, plus bodily presence at the work site.

task: Repair broken scrolls by replacing them with new sections of tubing.
rationale: Repairing broken scrolls requires physically manipulating components, removing damaged parts, and installing new tubing sections.

task: Operate and maintain finishing machines to grind, drill, sand, bevel, decorate, wash, or polish glass or glass products.
rationale: Requires operating physical machines and manually manipulating glass products through grinding, drilling, sanding, and polishing—direct physical equipment operation and execution.

task: Examine completed work to detect defects and verify conformance to work orders, and adjust machinery as necessary to correct production problems.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of tangible products and hands-on machinery adjustment to correct production issues.

task: Start machines and move controls to regulate tension on pressure rolls, to synchronize speed of machine components, and to adjust temperatures of glue or paraffin.
rationale: Operating physical machinery controls and adjusting tension, speed, and temperature settings requires hands-on operation of tangible machine components and controls.

task: Adjust guide assemblies, forming bars, and folding mechanisms according to specifications, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of assemblies and mechanisms using hand tools to adjust and form components according to specifications.

task: Install attachments to machines for gluing, folding, printing, or cutting.
rationale: Installing attachments to machines requires physical manipulation of tools and objects, directly manipulating tangible machine components.

task: Measure, space, and set saw blades, cutters, and perforators, according to product specifications.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of machinery components (saw blades, cutters, perforators) and precise hands-on adjustment per specifications.

task: Observe operation of various machines to detect and correct machine malfunctions such as improper forming, glue flow, or pasteboard tension.
rationale: Requires physical presence at machines to observe equipment operation and detect malfunctions through visual inspection and hands-on correction work.

task: Stamp products with information such as dates, using hand stamps or automatic stamping devices.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of stamping devices and products to imprint information; involves operation of tools and equipment.

task: Place rolls of paper or cardboard on machine feed tracks, and thread paper through gluing, coating, and slitting rollers.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of paper rolls, threading material through machinery, and hands-on operation of equipment components.

task: Fill glue and paraffin reservoirs, and position rollers to dispense glue onto paperboard.
rationale: Filling reservoirs and positioning rollers requires hands-on manipulation of equipment and materials to prepare machinery for glue dispensing.

task: Cut products to specified dimensions, using hand or power cutters.
rationale: Requires physical operation of cutting tools (hand or power cutters) to manipulate and cut products.

task: Monitor finished cartons as they drop from forming machines into rotating hoppers and into gravity feed chutes to prevent jamming.
rationale: Requires physical presence at work site to visually inspect and monitor carton movement through machinery, intervening to prevent jamming.

task: Disassemble machines to maintain, repair, or replace broken or worn parts, using hand or power tools.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of machines using hand/power tools to disassemble, repair, and replace parts.

task: Remove finished cores, and stack or place them on conveyors for transfer to other work areas.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of objects (removing, stacking, placing cores) and operation of conveyor equipment.

task: Lift tote boxes of finished cartons, and dump cartons into feed hoppers.
rationale: Task involves physically lifting and manipulating tote boxes and dumping cartons into hoppers—direct physical handling of tangible objects.

task: Operate machinery used in the production process, or assist machine operators.
rationale: Operating or assisting with production machinery requires physical interaction with equipment and tools.

task: Examine products to verify conformance to quality standards.
rationale: Examining products requires physical inspection, handling, and direct observation of tangible objects to assess quality standards.

task: Lift raw materials, finished products, and packed items, manually or using hoists.
rationale: Lifting raw materials and products involves physical manipulation of tangible objects and operation of equipment like hoists.

task: Count finished products to determine if product orders are complete.
rationale: Counting finished products requires physical inspection and handling of tangible objects to verify order completion.

task: Mark or tag identification on parts.
rationale: Marking or tagging parts requires physical manipulation of objects and marking tools to apply identification.

task: Load and unload items from machines, conveyors, and conveyances.
rationale: Physical manipulation of objects being loaded/unloaded from machines, conveyors, and vehicles requires hands-on handling.

task: Help production workers by performing duties of lesser skill, such as supplying or holding materials or tools, or cleaning work areas and equipment.
rationale: Task involves physical material handling, tool supply, and equipment cleaning—all hands-on manual work.

task: Clean and lubricate equipment.
rationale: Cleaning and lubricating equipment requires hands-on physical manipulation and operation of tools and machines.

task: Start machines or equipment to begin production processes.
rationale: Operating machinery and equipment to initiate production is physical equipment operation requiring hands-on interaction.

task: Separate products according to weight, grade, size, or composition of materials used to produce them.
rationale: Separating products requires physical manipulation and inspection of tangible objects by weight, grade, size, or material composition.

task: Turn valves to regulate flow of liquids or air, to reverse machines, to start pumps, or to regulate equipment.
rationale: Turning valves requires physical manipulation of tangible equipment to control flow and operate machinery.

task: Place products in equipment or on work surfaces for further processing, inspecting, or wrapping.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and placement of tangible products onto equipment or work surfaces—a hands-on action.

task: Remove products, machine attachments, or waste material from machines.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of objects (products, attachments, waste) and operation of machines to remove materials.

task: Pack and store materials and products.
rationale: Packing and storing materials requires physical manipulation of objects, handling, and organizing tangible items in storage locations.

task: Tie products in bundles for further processing or shipment, following prescribed procedures.
rationale: Tying products in bundles requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and manual handling to follow bundling procedures.

task: Transfer finished products, raw materials, tools, or equipment between storage and work areas of plants and warehouses, by hand or using hand trucks or powered lift trucks.
rationale: Task involves physical movement and manipulation of objects by hand or operating powered equipment like lift trucks in warehouse environments.

task: Prepare raw materials for processing.
rationale: Preparing raw materials involves physical manipulation—sorting, organizing, measuring, or otherwise handling tangible materials for processing.

task: Measure amounts of products, lengths of extruded articles, or weights of filled containers to ensure conformance to specifications.
rationale: Measuring requires physical inspection of tangible objects (weighing, measuring lengths), necessitating hands-on interaction with products and tools.

task: Mix ingredients according to specified procedures or formulas.
rationale: Mixing ingredients requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and hands-on execution of procedures.

task: Position spouts or chutes of storage bins so that containers can be filled.
rationale: Positioning spouts/chutes requires physical manipulation and adjustment of equipment components to enable filling operations.

task: Wash work areas, machines, equipment, vehicles, or products.
rationale: Washing requires physical manipulation and handling of equipment/surfaces with tools and water.

task: Unclamp and hoist full reels from braiding, winding, or other fabricating machines, using power hoists.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of reels, operation of power hoists, and bodily presence at machinery to execute unclamping and hoisting actions.

task: Break up defective products for reprocessing.
rationale: Breaking up defective products requires physical manipulation and disassembly of tangible objects for material recovery.

task: Cut or break flashing from materials or products.
rationale: Cutting or breaking flashing involves physical manipulation and use of tools on tangible materials—a hands-on construction/repair task.

task: Perform minor repairs to machines, such as replacing damaged or worn parts.
rationale: Replacing parts requires physical manipulation of machine components and operation of repair tools.

task: Attach slings, ropes, or cables to objects such as pipes, hoses, or bundles.
rationale: Attaching slings and cables requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and materials.

task: Inspect, adjust, or control radio equipment or airport lights.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and operation of equipment (radio controls, airport light systems) and physical inspection at work site.

task: Monitor gauges or meters that measure speed, amperage, battery charge, or air pressure in brake lines or in main reservoirs.
rationale: Requires physical presence at work site to visually inspect gauges/meters and monitor physical systems.

task: Observe tracks to detect obstructions.
rationale: Requires physical presence on tracks to inspect and detect obstructions through direct visual/tactile observation.

task: Receive starting signals from conductors and use controls such as throttles or air brakes to drive electric, diesel-electric, steam, or gas turbine-electric locomotives.
rationale: Operating locomotive controls (throttles, air brakes) is direct physical manipulation of machinery and vehicle operation.

task: Operate locomotives to transport freight or passengers between stations or to assemble or disassemble trains within rail yards.
rationale: Operating locomotives requires physical manipulation of controls and machinery to move trains, transporting cargo or passengers across rail networks.

task: Inspect locomotives to verify adequate fuel, sand, water, or other supplies before each run or to check for mechanical problems.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of locomotives, checking fuel levels, examining mechanical components hands-on at work site.

task: Inspect locomotives after runs to detect damaged or defective equipment.
rationale: Physical inspection of locomotives requires hands-on examination of equipment and components at the work site.

task: Operate controls to open and close transit vehicle doors.
rationale: Operating physical controls on transit vehicles requires hands-on interaction with machinery/equipment to perform door operations.

task: Drive and control rail-guided public transportation, such as subways, elevated trains, and electric-powered streetcars, trams, or trolleys, to transport passengers.
rationale: Operating rail-guided vehicles requires hands-on control of machinery and physical presence at the work site to safely transport passengers.

task: Move levers, depress foot pedals, and turn dials to operate power machinery, such as power shovels, stripping shovels, scraper loaders, or backhoes.
rationale: Operating power machinery requires physical manipulation of controls (levers, pedals, dials) and bodily operation of vehicles/equipment.

task: Set up or inspect equipment prior to operation.
rationale: Inspecting equipment requires physical presence and hands-on examination of tangible objects prior to operation.

task: Observe hand signals, grade stakes, or other markings when operating machines so that work can be performed to specifications.
rationale: Operating machines and observing physical markings/signals requires hands-on control of equipment and active physical presence at the work site.

task: Operate machinery to perform activities such as backfilling excavations, vibrating or breaking rock or concrete, or making winter roads.
rationale: Operating machinery to perform physical work like backfilling, rock breaking, or road construction requires hands-on operation of heavy equipment and tools.

task: Lubricate, adjust, or repair machinery and replace parts, such as gears, bearings, or bucket teeth.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of machinery parts, lubrication application, adjustments, and replacement of gears, bearings, or teeth—all hands-on technical work.

task: Move materials over short distances, such as around a construction site, factory, or warehouse.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and movement of tangible materials to different locations.

task: Measure and verify levels of rock or gravel, bases, or other excavated material.
rationale: Measuring and verifying material levels requires physical inspection, hands-on measurement, and being present at the work site to examine excavated materials.

task: Adjust dig face angles for varying overburden depths and set lengths.
rationale: Adjusting dig face angles involves operating excavation machinery and physically manipulating work site parameters for mining/extraction operations.

task: Drive machines to work sites.
rationale: Operating machines to transport them requires physical manipulation and control of the machinery/vehicle to reach work sites.

task: Perform manual labor to prepare or finish sites, such as shoveling materials by hand.
rationale: Shoveling materials requires physical manipulation and bodily movement using a tool to move tangible objects at a work site.

task: Observe positions of cars passing loading spouts, and swing spouts into the correct positions at the appropriate times.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of loading spouts (machinery) and physical observation of moving vehicles to coordinate timing and positioning.

task: Operate ship loading and unloading equipment, conveyors, hoists, and other specialized material handling equipment such as railroad tank car unloading equipment.
rationale: Operating physical machinery and equipment (conveyors, hoists, tank car loaders) requires direct hands-on control of mechanical systems.

task: Check conditions and weights of vessels to ensure cleanliness and compliance with loading procedures.
rationale: Requires physical inspection and hands-on assessment of vessel conditions, checking cleanliness and weights in person.

task: Operate industrial trucks, tractors, loaders, and other equipment to transport materials to and from transportation vehicles and loading docks, and to store and retrieve materials in warehouses.
rationale: Operating industrial machinery (trucks, tractors, loaders) to transport and handle materials requires physical operation of equipment and manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Connect ground cables to carry off static electricity when unloading tanker cars.
rationale: Connecting ground cables requires physical manipulation of cables and equipment on tanker cars to manage static discharge safely.

task: Seal outlet valves on tank cars, barges, and trucks.
rationale: Sealing outlet valves requires physical manipulation of tangible equipment on tank cars, barges, and trucks.

task: Test samples for specific gravity, using hydrometers, or send samples to laboratories for testing.
rationale: Physical handling of test samples and operation of hydrometers (physical instruments) to measure specific gravity.

task: Remove and replace tank car dome caps, or direct other workers in their removal and replacement.
rationale: Removing and replacing tank car dome caps involves physical manipulation of equipment parts and hands-on mechanical work on vehicles.

task: Lower gauge rods into tanks or read meters to verify contents, temperatures, and volumes of liquid loads.
rationale: Lowering gauge rods into tanks requires physical manipulation of tools and equipment. Reading meters involves physical inspection of gauges/instruments at the worksite.

task: Clean interiors of tank cars or tank trucks, using mechanical spray nozzles.
rationale: Physical operation of spray nozzles and manual cleaning of tank car/truck interiors requires hands-on mechanical work.

task: Operate conveyors and equipment to transfer grain or other materials from transportation vehicles.
rationale: Primary execution involves operating physical machinery (conveyors, equipment) to transfer materials—hands-on mechanical operation.

task: Test vessels for leaks, damage, and defects, and repair or replace defective parts as necessary.
rationale: Testing involves physical inspection and touching vessels; repairs require manual manipulation of parts and tools.

task: Unload cars containing liquids by connecting hoses to outlet plugs and pumping compressed air into cars to force liquids into storage tanks.
rationale: Unloading involves physical manipulation of hoses, connecting them to outlets, and operating compressed air equipment—all hands-on mechanical work.

task: Copy and attach load specifications to loaded tanks.
rationale: Copying and attaching documents to physical tanks requires manual handling, physical manipulation of objects, and bodily presence at the work site.

task: Start pumps and adjust valves or cables to regulate the flow of products to vessels, using knowledge of loading procedures.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of pumps, manual adjustment of valves and cables, and hands-on equipment manipulation to regulate product flow.

task: Perform general warehouse activities, such as opening containers and crates, filling warehouse orders, assisting in taking inventory, and weighing and checking materials.
rationale: Tasks involve physical manipulation of containers, materials, and equipment (opening crates, weighing, checking items) at a warehouse worksite.

task: Install and perform minor repairs to hardware, software, or peripheral equipment, following design or installation specifications.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of hardware/equipment, operation of tools, and hands-on assembly/repair work.

task: Set up equipment for employee use, performing or ensuring proper installation of cables, operating systems, or appropriate software.
rationale: Installing cables and setting up equipment involves physical manipulation of tangible objects and tools, even if software is also involved.

task: Obtain information about or inspect performance facilities, equipment, and accommodations to ensure that they meet specifications.
rationale: Inspecting facilities and equipment requires physical presence at the site to examine and verify specifications firsthand.

task: Examine or test crops or products to estimate their value, determine their grade, or locate any evidence of disease or insect damage.
rationale: Physical inspection of crops/products in field or facility; requires hands-on examination, visual/tactile assessment for damage, disease, or quality.

task: Estimate land production possibilities, surveying property and studying factors such as crop rotation history, soil fertility, or irrigation facilities.
rationale: Surveying property and studying land conditions requires physical inspection, examining soil, irrigation systems, and on-site observation of land characteristics.

task: Collect, identify, evaluate, or preserve case evidence.
rationale: Collecting, identifying, evaluating, and preserving evidence requires physical handling of tangible objects, examination, and presence at scenes.

task: Inspect manufactured or processed products to ensure compliance with contract specifications or legal requirements.
rationale: Inspecting products requires physical examination and hands-on assessment of tangible objects to verify compliance.

task: Inspect government-owned equipment or materials in the possession of private contractors to ensure compliance with contracts or regulations or to prevent misuse.
rationale: Inspecting government-owned equipment requires physical presence to examine, touch, and assess tangible objects for compliance and condition.

task: Submit samples of products to government laboratories for testing, as required.
rationale: Submitting samples requires physically handling and transporting tangible product samples to laboratories.

task: Develop or execute tests to detect diseases, genetic disorders, or other abnormalities.
rationale: Executing tests involves physical specimen handling, operating laboratory equipment, and performing hands-on diagnostic procedures.

task: Prepare pharmaceutical compounds for commercial distribution.
rationale: Preparing pharmaceutical compounds involves physical manipulation of substances, equipment operation, and hands-on chemical processing for production.

task: Design or perform experiments with equipment, such as lasers, accelerators, or mass spectrometers.
rationale: Designing and performing experiments requires hands-on operation of complex physical equipment and instruments in a laboratory setting.

task: Inspect equipment and read order sheets to prepare for delivery to users.
rationale: Primary task includes physical inspection of equipment, which requires hands-on examination and presence at work site.

task: Design or build laboratory equipment needed for special research projects.
rationale: Designing and building laboratory equipment requires hands-on physical manipulation, assembly, and construction of tangible objects.

task: Examine sample garments on and off models, modifying designs to achieve desired effects.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of garments, fitting them on models, and hands-on modification of designs to see and achieve visual effects.

task: Sketch rough and detailed drawings of apparel or accessories, and write specifications such as color schemes, construction, material types, and accessory requirements.
rationale: Sketching drawings requires physical manipulation of tools (pen, paper, pencil) and hand-eye coordination for detailed visual work.

task: Provide sample garments to agents and sales representatives, and arrange for showings of sample garments at sales meetings or fashion shows.
rationale: Providing and arranging physical sample garments requires handling tangible objects and coordinating physical logistics for showings.

task: Draw patterns for articles designed, cut patterns, and cut material according to patterns, using measuring instruments and scissors.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tools (scissors, measuring instruments) and material cutting—direct hands-on execution.

task: Test fabrics or oversee testing so that garment care labels can be created.
rationale: Testing fabrics requires physical manipulation and inspection of textile samples to assess durability, shrinkage, colorfastness, and other properties.

task: Sew together sections of material to form mockups or samples of garments or articles, using sewing equipment.
rationale: Sewing involves physical manipulation of materials and operation of sewing equipment to create garment samples.

task: Participate in athletic events or competitive sports, according to established rules and regulations.
rationale: Athletic events require physical execution—bodily movement, exertion, and manipulation of equipment integral to competition.

task: Exercise or practice under the direction of athletic trainers or professional coaches to develop skills, improve physical condition, or prepare for competitions.
rationale: Requires physical movement, bodily exertion, and hands-on athletic activity to develop skills and condition.

task: Maintain equipment used in a particular sport.
rationale: Maintaining equipment involves physical manipulation, inspection, repair, and servicing of tangible sporting equipment objects.

task: Maintain optimum physical fitness levels by training regularly, following nutrition plans, or consulting with health professionals.
rationale: Training regularly requires physical exercise and bodily movement; following nutrition involves hands-on meal preparation and consumption.

task: Observe and document activities of individuals to detect unlawful acts or to obtain evidence for cases, using binoculars and still or video cameras.
rationale: Requires physical use of binoculars, cameras, and being present at location to observe and document activities.

task: Operate boom trucks, loaders, stump chippers, brush chippers, tractors, power saws, trucks, sprayers, and other equipment and tools.
rationale: Operating physical machinery (boom trucks, loaders, chippers, tractors, power saws, sprayers) requires hands-on control of equipment and tools.

task: Climb trees, using climbing hooks and belts, or climb ladders to gain access to work areas.
rationale: Climbing trees and ladders using physical tools requires direct bodily movement and physical presence to execute the work.

task: Clean, sharpen, and lubricate tools and equipment.
rationale: Cleaning, sharpening, and lubricating tools requires hands-on physical manipulation and operation of equipment.

task: Cut away dead and excess branches from trees, or clear branches around power lines, using climbing equipment or buckets of extended truck booms, or chainsaws, hooks, handsaws, shears, and clippers.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (chainsaws, shears, clippers, hooks) and climbing equipment to cut branches from trees.

task: Trim, top, and reshape trees to achieve attractive shapes or to remove low-hanging branches.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (pruning equipment) and bodily movement to interact with trees at height.

task: Prune, cut down, fertilize, and spray trees as directed by tree surgeons.
rationale: Pruning, cutting, fertilizing, and spraying trees require physical manipulation of tools and direct hands-on work with plants and equipment.

task: Hoist tools and equipment to tree trimmers, and lower branches with ropes or block and tackle.
rationale: Physical manipulation of tools, equipment, and branches using ropes and block and tackle requires hands-on physical execution and bodily movement.

task: Operate shredding and chipping equipment, and feed limbs and brush into the machines.
rationale: Task involves physically operating machinery and manually feeding materials into equipment, requiring hands-on equipment operation and physical manipulation.

task: Load debris and refuse onto trucks and haul it away for disposal.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical manipulation (loading debris) and operating machinery (driving trucks to haul waste).

task: Inspect trees to determine if they have diseases or pest problems.
rationale: Physical inspection of trees requires being on-site, visually examining bark, leaves, and branches, often touching or closely observing for signs of disease or pests.

task: Cable, brace, tie, bolt, stake, and guy trees and branches to provide support.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of cables, braces, ties, bolts, and stakes to physically support trees and branches.

task: Collect debris and refuse from tree trimming and removal operations into piles, using shovels, rakes, or other tools.
rationale: Collecting debris requires physical manipulation of objects using tools like shovels and rakes to gather and pile materials from tree work sites.

task: Clear sites, streets, and grounds of woody and herbaceous materials, such as tree stumps and fallen trees and limbs.
rationale: Requires physical removal and manipulation of tree stumps, fallen trees, and limbs from outdoor spaces.

task: Remove broken limbs from wires, using hooked extension poles.
rationale: Physical operation of hooked extension poles to remove debris from wires requires manual manipulation of tools and physical presence at the work site.

task: Trim jagged stumps, using saws or pruning shears.
rationale: Requires physical operation of saws or pruning shears to cut and manipulate wood objects.

task: Transplant and remove trees and shrubs, and prepare trees for moving.
rationale: Transplanting, removing, and preparing trees/shrubs requires physical manipulation, digging, cutting, and handling of tangible plants and materials.

task: Spray trees to treat diseased or unhealthy trees, including mixing chemicals and calibrating spray equipment.
rationale: Requires physical operation of spray equipment, manual chemical mixing, equipment calibration, and direct physical application to trees.

task: Water, root-feed, and fertilize trees.
rationale: Watering, root-feeding, and fertilizing trees requires physical manipulation of tools, hoses, and materials, and direct hands-on interaction with plants.

task: Apply tar or other protective substances to cut surfaces or seal surfaces and to protect them from fungi and insects.
rationale: Applying tar or protective substances requires physical manipulation and manual application to surfaces using tools and bodily movement.

task: Scrape decayed matter from cavities in trees and fill holes with cement to promote healing and to prevent further deterioration.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation—scraping decayed matter and filling holes with cement are hands-on, tangible operations requiring physical tools and bodily presence at the tree.

task: Split logs or wooden blocks into bolts, pickets, posts, or stakes, using hand tools such as ax wedges, sledgehammers, and mallets.
rationale: Splitting logs involves physical manipulation using hand tools (ax, wedges, sledgehammers, mallets) to break wood into pieces.

task: Inspect work areas or operating equipment to ensure conformance to established standards in areas such as cleanliness or maintenance.
rationale: Requires physical inspection and presence at work site to observe and assess equipment/areas against standards.

task: Administer first aid to injured group participants.
rationale: Administering first aid requires hands-on physical examination and treatment of injuries (bandaging, CPR, wound care, etc.).

task: Pilot airplanes or drive land and water vehicles to transport tourists to activity or tour sites.
rationale: Operating vehicles (airplanes, cars, boats) to transport tourists requires physical operation of machinery and control at a work site.

task: Set up camps, and prepare meals for tour group members.
rationale: Setting up camps requires physical labor (erecting tents, arranging gear). Meal preparation involves cooking, handling food, and using kitchen equipment—all manual/physical work.

task: Instruct novices in climbing techniques, mountaineering, and wilderness survival, and demonstrate use of hunting, fishing, and climbing equipment.
rationale: Demonstrating use of equipment and instructing climbing/mountaineering requires hands-on physical demonstration and operation of physical tools and gear.

task: Provide boarding assistance to elderly, sick, or injured people.
rationale: Boarding assistance requires hands-on physical support—helping people move onto vehicles, planes, or vessels through guiding, stabilizing, and bodily presence.

task: Open and close doors for passengers.
rationale: Operating door mechanisms requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and repeated bodily movement at a work site.

task: Explain and demonstrate safety procedures and safety equipment use.
rationale: Demonstrating safety equipment requires physical handling and showing hands-on use of equipment to others.

task: Perform equipment safety checks prior to departure.
rationale: Equipment safety checks require physical inspection, touching, and examining tangible objects before departure.

task: Signal transportation operators to stop or to proceed.
rationale: Signaling requires physical gestures, hand signals, or flag movements at the work site to direct vehicle operators.

task: Adjust window shades or seat cushions at the request of passengers.
rationale: Physically manipulating window shades and seat cushions requires hands-on interaction with tangible objects.

task: Keep areas neat while working and return items to correct locations following demonstrations.
rationale: Keeping areas neat and physically returning items to locations requires hands-on physical manipulation and movement in the work environment.

task: Set up and arrange displays or demonstration areas to attract the attention of prospective customers.
rationale: Setting up and arranging displays requires physical manipulation of objects, moving merchandise, and arranging items in a work space.

task: Transport, assemble, and disassemble materials used in presentations.
rationale: Transporting, assembling, and disassembling materials requires physical manipulation and movement of tangible objects.

task: Practice demonstrations to ensure that they will run smoothly.
rationale: Practicing demonstrations requires physically performing and executing the demo to test procedures and identify issues.

task: Visit trade shows, stores, community organizations, or other venues to demonstrate products or services or to answer questions from potential customers.
rationale: Demonstrating products requires physical manipulation and presence at venues to interact directly with customers and show product functionality.

task: Wear costumes or sign boards and walk in public to promote merchandise, services, or events.
rationale: Requires physical presence and bodily movement in public spaces while wearing costumes or holding signs to promote products/services.

task: Stock shelves with products.
rationale: Stocking shelves requires physical manipulation of products and placement on shelves, a hands-on task.

task: Assemble and maintain portfolios, print composite cards, and travel to go-sees to obtain jobs.
rationale: Assembling portfolios and printing composite cards involve physical manipulation. Traveling to go-sees requires physical presence at work sites.

task: Work closely with photographers, fashion coordinators, directors, producers, stylists, make-up artists, other models, and clients to produce the desired looks, and to finish photo shoots on schedule.
rationale: Models physically present their bodies and appearance, requiring bodily positioning, movement, and physical presence at photo shoots to create visual content.

task: Apply makeup to face and style hair to enhance appearance, considering such factors as color, camera techniques, and facial features.
rationale: Applying makeup and styling hair requires physical manipulation of products on tangible objects (face, hair) using hands and tools.

task: Pose as directed, or strike suitable interpretive poses for promoting and selling merchandise or fashions during appearances, filming, or photo sessions.
rationale: Posing requires physical positioning and bodily movement at work site (photo/film sessions) to create visual products.

task: Dress in sample or completed garments, and select accessories.
rationale: Dressing in garments and handling accessories requires physical manipulation and bodily movement—putting on clothes and selecting physical items.

task: Deliver merchandise and collect payment.
rationale: Delivering merchandise requires physical transport and manipulation of goods. Payment collection is incidental to the primary manual delivery work.

task: Explain products or services and prices and demonstrate use of products.
rationale: Demonstrating product use requires physical handling and operation of products, a hands-on activity.

task: Circulate among potential customers or travel by foot, truck, automobile, or bicycle to deliver or sell merchandise or services.
rationale: Task requires physical travel (foot, truck, automobile, bicycle) and hands-on delivery/merchandise handling. Primary execution is physical/operational.

task: Distribute product samples or literature that details products or services.
rationale: Distributing physical samples or literature requires hands-on handling and movement to deliver materials to recipients.

task: Set up and display sample merchandise at parties or stands.
rationale: Requires physical setup and arrangement of tangible merchandise items at a location.

task: Stock carts or stands.
rationale: Stocking carts or stands requires physically placing, arranging, and organizing items on displays or storage units.

task: Observe signal lights on switchboards, and dial or press buttons to make connections.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of buttons/dials and observing signal lights, involving hands-on operation of switchboard equipment.

task: Operate paging systems or other systems of bells or buzzers to notify recipients of incoming calls.
rationale: Operating physical paging systems, bells, and buzzers requires direct operation of equipment and machinery to generate alerts.

task: Seal or open envelopes, by hand or by using machines.
rationale: Physical manipulation of envelopes by hand or operating envelope-sealing machines.

task: Affix postage to packages or letters by hand, or stamp materials, using postage meters.
rationale: Affixing postage and stamping materials requires physical manipulation of objects and operation of postage meters.

task: Verify that items are addressed correctly, marked with the proper postage, and in suitable condition for processing.
rationale: Physical inspection of mail items requires visual examination and hands-on assessment of condition and markings.

task: Place incoming or outgoing letters or packages into sacks or bins based on destination or type, and place identifying tags on sacks or bins.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of letters/packages, placing them into sacks/bins, and attaching tags—all hands-on physical work.

task: Clear jams in sortation equipment.
rationale: Clearing jams requires physical manipulation of equipment to remove obstruction; hands-on mechanical work.

task: Sort and route incoming mail, and collect outgoing mail, using carts as necessary.
rationale: Sorting, routing, and collecting physical mail requires handling tangible objects and moving them using carts—direct physical manipulation.

task: Weigh packages or letters to determine postage needed, using weighing scales and rate charts.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of scales to weigh packages and letters, plus handling tangible objects.

task: Determine manner in which mail is to be sent, and prepare it for delivery to mailing facilities.
rationale: Requires physical handling of mail materials—sorting, packaging, and preparing items for transport to mailing facilities.

task: Lift and unload containers of mail or parcels onto equipment for transportation to sortation stations.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and lifting of containers onto equipment, which is hands-on material handling work.

task: Wrap packages or bundles by hand, or by using tying machines.
rationale: Wrapping packages by hand and operating tying machines both require physical manipulation and operation of equipment.

task: Fold letters or circulars and insert them in envelopes.
rationale: Folding letters and inserting them into envelopes requires direct physical manipulation of tangible objects (paper, envelopes).

task: Load computer tapes and disks, and install software and printer paper or forms.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tangible objects (tapes, disks, paper) and installation of hardware components.

task: Remove from machines printed materials, such as labeled articles, postmarked envelopes or tape, and folded sheets.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tangible objects (printed materials) being removed from machines by hand.

task: Add ink, fill paste reservoirs, and change machine ribbons when necessary.
rationale: Tasks involve physical manipulation of tangible objects (ink, paste, ribbons) and hands-on maintenance of machines.

task: Start machines that automatically feed plates, stencils, or tapes through mechanisms, and observe machine operations to detect any malfunctions.
rationale: Task requires physically starting machines and observing their operation on-site to detect malfunctions—direct interaction with physical equipment and machinery.

task: Mail merchandise samples or promotional literature in response to requests.
rationale: Physical handling and mailing of merchandise samples and promotional materials involves manipulating tangible objects.

task: Inspect mail machine output for defects and determine how to eliminate causes of any defects.
rationale: Inspecting mail machine output requires physical examination of tangible objects to identify defects, which is hands-on quality control work.

task: Remove containers of sorted mail or parcels and transfer them to designated areas according to established procedures.
rationale: Physical manipulation and transfer of mail/parcel containers from one location to another following established procedures.

task: Stamp dates and times of receipt of incoming mail.
rationale: Stamping mail requires physical manipulation of tangible objects (mail) and operation of a stamp tool.

task: Adjust guides, rollers, loose card inserters, weighing machines, and tying arms, using rules and hand tools.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical manipulation of machinery parts and hand tools to adjust guides, rollers, and other equipment components.

task: Accept and check containers of mail or parcels from large volume mailers, couriers, and contractors.

task: Manipulate controls to set, activate, and adjust mechanisms on machinery.

task: Mix feed, additives, and medicines in prescribed portions.
rationale: Mixing feed, additives, and medicines requires physical manipulation of tangible materials and substances.

task: Tag unsafe trees with high-visibility ribbons.

task: Secure steel cables or chains to logs for dragging by tractors or for pulling by cable yarding systems.

task: Mark logs for identification.
rationale: Marking logs requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and hands-on work at the job site.

task: Work as a member of a team, rotating between chain saw operation and skidder operation.

rationale: Administering medications requires physical manipulation (injections, dispensing); monitoring includes physical patient examination and observation.

task: Prescribe or administer treatments, such as transcranial magnetic stimulation, vagus nerve stimulation, and deep brain stimulation.
rationale: Administering medical treatments like TMS, vagus nerve stimulation, and deep brain stimulation requires hands-on physical execution and direct patient interaction.

task: Perform or interpret the outcomes of procedures or diagnostic tests, such as lumbar punctures, electroencephalography, electromyography, and nerve conduction velocity tests.
rationale: Performing diagnostic procedures like lumbar punctures requires physical manipulation and hands-on execution. Interpreting outcomes is primarily cognitive, but performing/executing procedures is manual work.

task: Examine patients to obtain information about functional status of areas, such as vision, physical strength, coordination, reflexes, sensations, language skills, cognitive abilities, and mental status.
rationale: Examining patients requires hands-on physical interaction: testing reflexes, sensations, coordination, strength, vision, and direct patient contact to assess functional status.

task: Instruct interns, residents, or others in ophthalmologic procedures and techniques.
rationale: Instructing in ophthalmologic procedures requires demonstrating physical techniques, hands-on guidance, and performing procedures as part of teaching.

task: Provide or direct the provision of postoperative care.
rationale: Postoperative care involves hands-on patient interaction including wound management, vital sign monitoring, physical assessment, and direct caregiving activities.

task: Prescribe or administer topical or systemic medications to treat ophthalmic conditions and to manage pain.
rationale: Administering medications involves physical manipulation (applying drops, injections) and hands-on patient interaction.

task: Perform, order, or interpret the results of diagnostic or clinical tests.
rationale: Performing diagnostic tests involves physical manipulation of medical equipment, specimens, and patient interaction. Interpreting results requires clinical expertise but may involve physical examination steps.

task: Perform laser surgeries to alter, remove, reshape, or replace ocular tissue.
rationale: Laser surgery requires operating specialized medical equipment on a patient's eye tissue, involving hands-on manipulation of instruments and physical presence at the surgical site.

task: Perform ophthalmic surgeries such as cataract, glaucoma, refractive, corneal, vitro-retinal, eye muscle, or oculoplastic surgeries.
rationale: Performing ophthalmic surgeries requires hands-on physical manipulation of surgical instruments, the eye, and delicate anatomical structures.

task: Perform comprehensive examinations of the visual system to determine the nature or extent of ocular disorders.
rationale: Comprehensive eye examinations involve hands-on physical procedures: using instruments like slit lamps, tonometers, and ophthalmoscopes; direct visual inspection; and close physical interaction with patients to assess ocular function and disorders.

task: Diagnose or treat injuries, disorders, or diseases of the eye and eye structures including the cornea, sclera, conjunctiva, or eyelids.
rationale: Diagnosing and treating eye injuries/diseases requires hands-on physical examination and manipulation of eye structures and specialized instruments.

task: Perform autopsies to determine causes of deaths.
