task: Obtain specimens by performing procedures, such as biopsies or fine needle aspirations (FNAs) of superficial nodules.
rationale: Performing biopsies and FNAs requires hands-on physical manipulation and needle insertion into tissue to collect specimens.

task: Diagnose infections, such as Hepatitis B and Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome (AIDS), by conducting tests to detect the antibodies that patients' immune systems make to fight such infections.
rationale: Conducting diagnostic tests involves physical manipulation of lab equipment, specimens, and samples to detect antibodies—hands-on laboratory work.

task: Examine microscopic samples to identify diseases or other abnormalities.
rationale: Examining microscopic samples requires physical operation of microscope equipment and hands-on manipulation of specimen slides.

task: Diagnose diseases or study medical conditions, using techniques such as gross pathology, histology, cytology, cytopathology, clinical chemistry, immunology, flow cytometry, or molecular biology.
rationale: Requires hands-on physical manipulation of specimens, microscopy, laboratory equipment operation, and physical specimen preparation techniques.

task: Conduct physical tests, such as functional capacity evaluations, to determine injured workers' capabilities to perform the physical demands of their jobs.
rationale: Conducting physical tests requires hands-on physical interaction with injured workers, including physical assessment, manipulation, and direct observation of their movements and capabilities.

task: Examine patients to assess mobility, strength, communication, or cognition.
rationale: Requires direct physical interaction with patients to assess mobility, strength, and communication—hands-on evaluation work.

task: Diagnose or treat performance-related conditions, such as sports injuries or repetitive-motion injuries.

task: Perform electrodiagnosis, including electromyography, nerve conduction studies, or somatosensory evoked potentials of neuromuscular disorders or damage.

task: Prescribe therapy services, such as electrotherapy, ultrasonography, heat or cold therapy, hydrotherapy, debridement, short-wave or microwave diathermy, and infrared or ultraviolet radiation, to enhance rehabilitation.

task: Trim material and arrange bouquets, wreaths, terrariums, and other items, using trimmers, shapers, wire, pins, floral tape, foam, and other materials.

task: Perform interventional procedures such as image-guided biopsy, percutaneous transluminal angioplasty, transhepatic biliary drainage, or nephrostomy catheter placement.

task: Prescribe or administer antibiotics, antiseptics, or compresses to treat infection or injury.

task: Perform abdominal, pelvic, or retroperitoneal surgeries.

task: Examine patients using equipment, such as radiograph (x-ray) machines or fluoroscopes, to determine the nature and extent of disorder or injury.

task: Assist in the application of monitoring techniques, such as pulmonary artery catheterization, electroencephalographic spectral analysis, echocardiography, or evoked potentials.

task: Maintain laboratory equipment such as photomicroscopes, inverted microscopes, and standard darkroom equipment.

task: Examine cell samples to detect abnormalities in the color, shape, or size of cellular components and patterns.
rationale: Examining cell samples requires hands-on microscopy work, physical manipulation of slides, and direct visual inspection of tangible specimens.

task: Examine specimens, using microscopes, to evaluate specimen quality.

task: Prepare and analyze samples, such as Papanicolaou (PAP) smear body fluids and fine needle aspirations (FNAs), to detect abnormal conditions.

task: Perform emergency medical procedures, such as basic cardiac life support (BLS), advanced cardiac life support (ACLS), and other condition-stabilizing interventions.

task: Adjust settings on patients' assistive devices, such as temporary pacemakers.

task: Treat wounds or superficial lacerations.

task: Set up, operate, or monitor invasive equipment and devices, such as colostomy or tracheotomy equipment, mechanical ventilators, catheters, gastrointestinal tubes, and central lines.

task: Obtain specimens or samples for laboratory work.
rationale: Obtaining specimens/samples requires physical collection, handling, and manipulation of tangible materials for lab work.

task: Administer blood and blood product transfusions or intravenous infusions, monitoring patients for adverse reactions.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of medical equipment (IV lines, blood products) and direct patient contact for transfusion administration and monitoring.

task: Provide post-mortem care.
rationale: Post-mortem care involves physical handling of deceased bodies, including washing, positioning, and preparing remains for burial or autopsy.

task: Perform approved therapeutic or diagnostic procedures, based upon patients' clinical status.
rationale: Therapeutic and diagnostic procedures require hands-on physical execution (injections, catheterization, wound care, physical exams) or operation of medical equipment.

task: Administer blood and blood products, monitoring patients for signs and symptoms related to transfusion reactions.
rationale: Administering blood requires physical manipulation (setting up IV lines, connecting products) and hands-on patient monitoring for adverse reactions.

task: Examine or conduct laboratory or diagnostic tests on patients to provide information on general physical condition or mental disorder.
rationale: Conducting diagnostic tests requires physical manipulation of equipment, specimen collection, and hands-on patient examination to gather medical data.

task: Administer medications intravenously, by injection, orally, through gastric tubes, or by other methods.
rationale: Administering medications requires physical manipulation and direct bodily contact: inserting IV lines, giving injections, inserting gastric tubes, handling medications.

task: Set up and monitor medical equipment and devices such as cardiac monitors, mechanical ventilators and alarms, oxygen delivery devices, transducers, or pressure lines.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and setup of medical equipment, plus hands-on monitoring of devices at patient bedside.

task: Monitor patients for changes in status and indications of conditions such as sepsis or shock and institute appropriate interventions.

task: Collect specimens for laboratory tests.
rationale: Collecting specimens requires physical manipulation and handling of tangible objects like blood, tissue, or samples using tools and equipment.

task: Conduct pulmonary assessments to identify abnormal respiratory patterns or breathing sounds that indicate problems.
rationale: Conducting pulmonary assessments requires physical examination—listening to breath sounds, palpating the chest, and directly observing the patient's breathing pattern in person.

task: Assist physicians with procedures such as bronchoscopy, endoscopy, endotracheal intubation, or elective cardioversion.
rationale: Assisting with medical procedures requires hands-on physical support, instrument handling, and direct patient interaction during bronchoscopy, endoscopy, intubation, and cardioversion.

task: Ensure that equipment or devices are properly stored after use.
rationale: Storing equipment requires physically handling and placing tangible objects in designated locations.

task: Identify malfunctioning equipment or devices.
rationale: Identifying malfunction requires physical inspection, testing, and hands-on examination of equipment or devices.

task: Administer post-anesthesia medications or fluids to support patients' cardiovascular systems.
rationale: Administering medications and fluids requires physical manipulation—injecting, infusing, handling syringes, IV lines, and monitoring patient vitals directly.

task: Perform pre-anesthetic screenings, including physical evaluations and patient interviews, and document results.
rationale: Physical evaluations require hands-on examination of patients (palpation, vital signs assessment), combined with patient interviews and documentation.

task: Manage patients' airway or pulmonary status, using techniques such as endotracheal intubation, mechanical ventilation, pharmacological support, respiratory therapy, and extubation.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and operation of medical equipment (intubation tubes, ventilators) and hands-on clinical intervention with patients.

task: Monitor patients' responses, including skin color, pupil dilation, pulse, heart rate, blood pressure, respiration, ventilation, or urine output, using invasive and noninvasive techniques.
rationale: Physical examination and monitoring requiring hands-on observation, palpation, and operation of medical monitoring devices (invasive and noninvasive).

task: Perform or manage regional anesthetic techniques, such as local, spinal, epidural, caudal, nerve blocks and intravenous blocks.
rationale: Performing regional anesthesia techniques requires direct physical manipulation—injecting anesthetics, inserting epidural catheters, performing nerve blocks with precise needle placement.

task: Prepare prescribed solutions and administer local, intravenous, spinal, or other anesthetics, following specified methods and procedures.
rationale: Administering anesthetics requires physical manipulation of medical equipment, injections, and hands-on clinical procedures.

task: Respond to emergency situations by providing airway management, administering emergency fluids or drugs, or using basic or advanced cardiac life support techniques.
rationale: Airway management, administering drugs/fluids, and cardiac life support require hands-on physical intervention and bodily presence with patients.

task: Select, prepare, or use equipment, monitors, supplies, or drugs for the administration of anesthetics.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of equipment, monitors, supplies, and drugs; hands-on preparation and administration in medical setting.

task: Calibrate and test anesthesia equipment.
rationale: Requires physical operation and manipulation of anesthesia equipment, plus hands-on testing procedures.

task: Disassemble and clean anesthesia equipment.
rationale: Disassembly and cleaning require hands-on physical manipulation of equipment components and tools.

task: Insert arterial catheters or perform arterial punctures to obtain arterial blood samples.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and invasive procedures on patients: inserting catheters and performing arterial punctures are hands-on clinical interventions.

task: Insert peripheral or central intravenous catheters.
rationale: Inserting IV catheters requires physical manipulation of medical devices, needle insertion, and hands-on clinical procedure execution.

task: Request anesthesia equipment repairs, adjustments, or safety tests.
rationale: Requires hands-on physical manipulation, adjustment, and testing of medical equipment to ensure safety and proper function.

task: Order, perform, or interpret the results of diagnostic tests, such as complete blood counts (CBCs), electrocardiograms (EKGs), and radiographs (x-rays).

task: Diagnose or treat acute health care problems, such as illnesses, infections, or injuries.

task: Perform primary care procedures such as suturing, splinting, administering immunizations, taking cultures, and debriding wounds.
rationale: Suturing, splinting, immunizations, cultures, and wound debridement all require hands-on physical manipulation and examination of patients.

task: Perform routine or annual physical examinations.
rationale: Performing physical examinations requires hands-on physical interaction with patients including palpation, inspection, and use of examination instruments.

task: Measure salinity, acidity, light, oxygen content, and other physical conditions of water to determine their relationship to aquatic life.
rationale: Requires physical measurement tools and water sampling at work sites; direct physical interaction with aquatic environments.

task: Perform mobilizations and high-velocity adjustments to joints or soft tissues, using principles of massage, stretching, or resistance.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of joints and soft tissues through hands-on massage, stretching, and resistance techniques.

task: Administer treatments or therapies, such as homeopathy, hydrotherapy, Oriental or Ayurvedic medicine, electrotherapy, and diathermy, using physical agents including air, heat, cold, water, sound, or ultraviolet light to catalyze the body to heal itself.

task: Conduct physical examinations and physiological function tests for diagnostic purposes.
rationale: Conducting physical examinations requires hands-on bodily contact and manipulation—palpating, auscultating, and performing physiological tests directly with patients.

task: Perform venipuncture or skin pricking to collect blood samples.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation—inserting needle into vein/skin and handling blood samples directly.

task: Treat minor cuts, abrasions, or contusions.
rationale: Treating cuts and abrasions requires hands-on physical interaction with the patient's body—cleaning wounds, applying bandages or ointments, physical examination and care.

task: Perform minor surgical procedures, such as removing warts, moles, or cysts, sampling tissues for skin cancer or lipomas, and applying or removing sutures.
rationale: Involves hands-on surgical procedures: cutting, tissue sampling, suturing, and physical manipulation of medical instruments and patient tissues.

task: Conduct periodic public health maintenance activities such as immunizations and screenings for diseases and disease risk factors.
rationale: Immunizations and disease screenings require physical examination, needle injections, blood draws, and direct patient contact—all hands-on clinical work.

task: Assist ophthalmologists in diagnostic ophthalmic procedures, such as ultrasonography, fundus photography, and tonometry.
rationale: Assisting with diagnostic procedures involves operating ophthalmic equipment (ultrasound, cameras, tonometers) and positioning patients for physical examination.

task: Provide nonsurgical interventions, including corrective lenses, patches, drops, fusion exercises, or stereograms, to treat conditions such as strabismus, heterophoria, and convergence insufficiency.

task: Perform diagnostic tests or measurements, such as motor testing, visual acuity testing, lensometry, retinoscopy, and color vision testing.

task: Examine patients with problems related to ocular motility, binocular vision, amblyopia, or strabismus.

task: Evaluate, diagnose, or treat disorders of the visual system with an emphasis on binocular vision or abnormal eye movements.
rationale: Requires hands-on examination and testing of patients' eyes, vision, and eye movements—physical diagnostic work.

task: Perform vision screening of children in schools or community health centers.
rationale: Requires hands-on physical examination: assessing vision, using screening equipment, positioning children, observing eye movements.

task: Provide prenatal, intrapartum, postpartum, or newborn care to patients.
rationale: Prenatal, intrapartum, and postpartum care involve hands-on physical examination, assessment, and care of pregnant/postpartum patients and newborns—physical interaction required.

task: Monitor fetal development by listening to fetal heartbeat, taking external uterine measurements, identifying fetal position, or estimating fetal size and weight.

task: Perform physical examinations by taking vital signs, checking neurological reflexes, examining breasts, or performing pelvic examinations.
rationale: Requires physical hands-on interaction with patient—palpation, vital sign measurement, neurological testing, and direct examination.

task: Initiate emergency interventions to stabilize patients.
rationale: Emergency stabilization involves hands-on physical interventions like CPR, bandaging, airway management, and direct patient contact.

task: Manage newborn care during the first weeks of life.

task: Provide primary health care, including pregnancy and childbirth, to women.
rationale: Delivering healthcare including pregnancy care and childbirth requires hands-on physical examination, patient interaction, and direct clinical work.

task: Provide patients with direct family planning services, such as inserting intrauterine devices, dispensing oral contraceptives, and fitting cervical barriers, including cervical caps or diaphragms.
rationale: Inserting IUDs, fitting diaphragms, and cervical caps require hands-on physical manipulation and bodily contact with patients.

task: Prepare patients for and assist with examinations or treatments.
rationale: Preparing and assisting with patient examinations/treatments requires hands-on physical interaction, positioning patients, and direct support during medical procedures.

task: Maintain laboratory instruments to ensure proper working order and troubleshoot malfunctions when needed.
rationale: Maintaining instruments requires physical manipulation and hands-on troubleshooting of equipment to keep them in working order.

task: Conduct quality control tests.

task: Observe nurses and visit patients to ensure proper nursing care.

task: Examine and analyze blood stain patterns at crime scenes.

task: Prepare rooms, sterile instruments, equipment, or supplies and ensure that stock of supplies is maintained.
rationale: Requires physical setup and manipulation of tangible instruments, equipment, and supplies in medical/surgical environments.

task: Catalogue and sort books and other print and non-print materials according to procedure and return them to shelves, files, or other designated storage areas.
rationale: Requires physical handling of books and materials, organizing them, and placing them on shelves or storage areas.

task: Maintain and troubleshoot problems with library equipment, including computers, photocopiers, and audio-visual equipment.

task: Check for damaged library materials, such as books or audio-visual equipment, and provide replacements or make repairs.

task: Work as part of a team of physicians, nurses, or other healthcare professionals to manage patient care by assisting with medical procedures or related duties.

task: Set up, maintain, calibrate, clean, and test sterility of medical laboratory equipment.
rationale: Requires hands-on operation and physical manipulation of laboratory equipment, including setup, calibration, cleaning, and testing procedures.

task: Provide clinical services or health education to improve and maintain the oral health of patients or the general public.
rationale: Providing clinical oral health services involves physical examination, treatment, and hands-on procedures on patients' teeth and gums.

task: Maintain dental equipment and sharpen and sterilize dental instruments.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of dental instruments, sharpening (tool operation), and sterilization procedures—hands-on maintenance work.

task: Administer local, inhalation, intravenous, or other anesthetics.
rationale: Administering anesthetics requires physical manipulation—injecting, inserting IV lines, or applying inhalation devices to patients' bodies.

task: Prepack bulk medicines, fill bottles with prescribed medications, and type and affix labels.
rationale: Prepacking medicines, filling bottles, and affixing labels all require physical manipulation of medications, bottles, and labels.

rationale: Receiving and storing supplies, verifying quantities, checking inventory require physical handling and inspection of tangible objects.

task: Provide health care, first aid, immunizations, or assistance in convalescence or rehabilitation in locations such as schools, hospitals, or industry.

task: Take residents on community outings.

task: Perform physical examinations, make tentative diagnoses, and treat patients en route to hospitals or at disaster site triage centers.
rationale: Performing physical examinations of patients requires hands-on bodily contact and physical assessment; treatment en route involves manual medical procedures.

task: Clean and maintain lobby and common areas, such as restocking supplies and watering plants.

task: Prepare for basic food service, such as setting up continental breakfast or coffee and tea supplies.

task: Conduct specified laboratory tests.
rationale: Laboratory tests require physical manipulation of equipment, samples, and instruments to perform procedures and measurements.

task: Diagnose and repair vehicle heating and cooling systems.

task: Repair, rewire, and troubleshoot electrical systems.

task: Deliver artwork on courier trips.

task: Perform general cleaning activities in kitchen and dining areas.

task: Clean and sanitize work areas, equipment, utensils, dishes, or silverware.

task: Perform cleaning duties, such as sweeping and mopping floors, vacuuming carpet, tidying up server station, taking out trash, or checking and cleaning bathroom.

task: Inspect dining and serving areas to ensure cleanliness and proper setup.

task: Respond to emergency situations, such as emergency medical calls, security calls, or fire alarms.
rationale: Responding to emergencies requires physical presence at the scene, bodily movement, and hands-on intervention (medical aid, restraint, firefighting).

task: Restrain patients to prevent violence or injury or to assist physicians or nurses to administer treatments.
rationale: Restraining patients requires direct physical contact, manipulation of patient body, and hands-on physical presence to execute safely and effectively.

task: Serve or collect food trays.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tangible objects (food trays) and bodily movement to transport and distribute or gather items.

task: Stock or issue medical supplies, such as dressing packs or treatment trays.
rationale: Stocking and issuing medical supplies requires physical manipulation and handling of tangible objects like dressing packs and treatment trays.

task: Stock utility rooms, nonmedical storage rooms, or cleaning carts with supplies.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of supplies and stocking of physical containers/carts in actual spaces.

task: Transport bodies to the morgue.
rationale: Transporting bodies requires physical handling, moving/carrying tangible objects, and performing actual manual labor.

task: Transport patients to treatment units, testing units, operating rooms, or other areas, using wheelchairs, stretchers, or moveable beds.
rationale: Transporting patients via wheelchairs, stretchers, or beds requires physical manipulation and bodily movement to move objects and navigate work sites.

task: Transport portable medical equipment or medical supplies between rooms or departments.
rationale: Task requires physically moving equipment and supplies between locations, involving carrying and manual transport of tangible objects.

task: Transport specimens, laboratory items, or pharmacy items, ensuring proper documentation and delivery to authorized personnel.
rationale: Transporting physical items requires physical movement and handling of tangible objects to authorized locations.

task: Turn or reposition bedridden patients, alone or with assistance, to prevent bedsores.
rationale: Repositioning bedridden patients requires direct physical manipulation and bodily movement to move another person and prevent pressure injuries.

task: Provide physical support to patients to assist them to perform daily living activities, such as getting out of bed, bathing, dressing, using the toilet, standing, walking, or exercising.
rationale: Requires direct physical assistance and bodily contact—lifting, supporting, guiding patients through hands-on personal care and mobility tasks.

task: Separate collected materials for disposal, recycling, or reuse, in accordance with environmental policies.
rationale: Physically sorting and handling materials by touching, moving, and separating them into different containers or piles for disposal, recycling, or reuse.

task: Collect fluid or tissue samples, using appropriate collection procedures.
rationale: Collecting fluid or tissue samples requires physical manipulation, specialized collection procedures, and hands-on handling of biological materials.

task: Dispose of blood or other biohazard fluids or tissue, in accordance with applicable laws, standards, or policies.
rationale: Physical manipulation and handling of biohazard materials, including disposal containers and proper containment procedures.

task: Dispose of contaminated sharps, in accordance with applicable laws, standards, and policies.
rationale: Disposing of sharps requires physical handling and manipulation of contaminated objects following specific safety protocols and procedures.

task: Draw blood from arteries, using arterial collection techniques.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and hands-on technique to puncture arteries and collect blood samples using specialized equipment.

task: Draw blood from capillaries by dermal puncture, such as heel or finger stick methods.
rationale: Requires physical puncture of skin and manual collection of blood samples using hands-on technique.

task: Draw blood from veins by vacuum tube, syringe, or butterfly venipuncture methods.
rationale: Drawing blood requires physical manipulation of medical instruments and direct physical interaction with a patient's veins.

task: Match laboratory requisition forms to specimen tubes.
rationale: Matching requisition forms to specimen tubes requires physical handling and inspection of both documents and tubes at a workstation.

task: Organize or clean blood-drawing trays, ensuring that all instruments are sterile and all needles, syringes, or related items are of first-time use.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of medical instruments (needles, syringes, trays) and organizing/cleaning tangible objects to ensure sterility.

task: Administer subcutaneous or intramuscular injects, in accordance with licensing restrictions.
rationale: Administering injections requires hands-on physical manipulation—inserting needles, handling syringes, and performing physical medical procedures on patients.

task: Calibrate or maintain machines, such as those used for plasma collection.
rationale: Calibrating and maintaining machines requires hands-on physical manipulation and operation of equipment and tools.

task: Collect specimens at specific time intervals for tests, such as those assessing therapeutic drug levels.
rationale: Collecting specimens requires physical handling of medical equipment, patient contact (drawing blood/samples), and hands-on procedural execution.

task: Conduct hemoglobin tests to ensure donor iron levels are normal.
rationale: Conducting hemoglobin tests requires physical blood draw and laboratory manipulation of samples.

task: Conduct standards tests, such as blood alcohol, blood culture, oral glucose tolerance, glucose screening, blood smears, or peak and trough drug levels tests.
rationale: Conducting laboratory tests requires physical manipulation of specimens (blood draws, samples) and operation of laboratory equipment and instruments.

task: Monitor blood or plasma donors during and after procedures to ensure health, safety, and comfort.
rationale: Monitoring donors requires physical presence, observation of donors' physical condition, checking vital signs, and hands-on assistance for comfort and safety during procedures.

task: Process blood or other fluid samples for further analysis by other medical professionals.
rationale: Physical manipulation of biological samples (blood, fluids) requiring hands-on specimen handling, preparation, and processing procedures.

task: Serve refreshments to donors to ensure absorption of sugar into their systems.
rationale: Physically handling and serving food/beverages to people requires manual execution and physical presence.

task: Train other medical personnel in phlebotomy or laboratory techniques.
rationale: Training involves demonstrating hands-on phlebotomy and lab techniques physically to students, not just verbal instruction.

task: Transport specimens or fluid samples from collection sites to laboratories.
rationale: Transporting specimens/samples involves physical movement, handling, and delivery of tangible objects across locations.

task: Bolt equipment into place, using hand or power tools.
rationale: Bolting equipment requires direct physical manipulation using hand or power tools to secure tangible objects in place.

task: Check antenna positioning to ensure specified azimuths or mechanical tilts and adjust as necessary.
rationale: Requires physical inspection, adjustment, and manipulation of antenna equipment on-site to achieve precise positioning and mechanical specifications.

task: Maintain proper storage and security conditions for drugs.
rationale: Requires physical handling and organization of drug storage systems, temperature/humidity monitoring, security equipment operation, and inspection of storage conditions.

task: Climb communication towers to install, replace, or repair antennas or auxiliary equipment used to transmit and receive radio waves.
rationale: Climbing towers and physically installing/replacing/repairing equipment requires hands-on manipulation and physical presence at heights.

task: Inspect completed work to ensure all hardware is tight, antennas are level, hangers are properly fastened, proper support is in place, or adequate weather proofing has been installed.
rationale: Physical inspection requires being on-site, examining hardware, antennas, and hangers to verify tightness, levelness, and fastening—requires hands-on assessment and tangible object interaction.

task: Install all necessary transmission equipment components, including antennas or antenna mounts, surge arrestors, transmission lines, connectors, or tower-mounted amplifiers (TMAs).
rationale: Primary execution requires physical manipulation and installation of tangible equipment components (antennas, surge arrestors, transmission lines, connectors, TMAs) and operation of tools at work site.

task: Install or repair tower lighting components, including strobes, beacons, or lighting controllers.
rationale: Installing and repairing tower lighting components requires hands-on physical manipulation and operation of tools to assemble, replace, or fix electrical equipment on towers.

task: Install, connect, or test underground or aboveground grounding systems.
rationale: Requires physical installation of grounding systems, connection of equipment, and hands-on testing with tools at work sites.

task: Lift equipment into position, using cranes and rigging tools or equipment, such as gin poles.
rationale: Operating cranes, rigging tools, and physically positioning equipment requires direct manipulation of machinery and heavy objects.

task: Perform maintenance or repair work on existing tower equipment, using hand or power tools.
rationale: Repairing equipment requires physical operation of hand/power tools and manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Replace existing antennas with new antennas as directed.
rationale: Requires physical removal and installation of antennas, involving hands-on manipulation of equipment.

task: Run appropriate power, ground, or coaxial cables.
rationale: Running cables requires physical manipulation and installation of tangible objects (power, ground, coaxial cables) at work sites.

task: Climb towers to access components, using safety equipment, such as full-body harnesses.
rationale: Requires physical climbing and bodily movement at height with safety equipment to access and work on tower components.

task: Take site survey photos or photos of work performed, using digital cameras.
rationale: Requires physical presence at work site and operation of digital camera equipment to capture images.

task: Transport equipment to work sites, using utility trucks and equipment trailers.
rationale: Operating utility trucks and trailers to transport equipment requires physical operation of vehicles and machinery.

task: Adjust ink fountain flow rates.
rationale: Adjusting ink fountain flow rates requires physical manipulation of equipment controls and mechanical adjustment of printing machinery.

task: Change press plates, blankets, or cylinders, as required.
rationale: Changing press plates, blankets, or cylinders requires physical manipulation and operation of printing equipment components.

task: Clean ink fountains, plates, or printing unit cylinders when press runs are completed.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of printing equipment and cleaning materials; hands-on maintenance work after press operations.

task: Clean or oil presses or make minor repairs, using hand tools.
rationale: Cleaning, oiling, and repairing presses requires physical manipulation using hand tools.

task: Collect and inspect random samples during print runs to identify any necessary adjustments.
rationale: Task requires physical collection of samples and hands-on inspection during active print runs to examine quality and identify adjustments.

task: Feed paper through press cylinders and adjust feed and tension controls.
rationale: Physical manipulation of paper and operation of press machinery controls requires hands-on interaction with equipment.

task: Load presses with paper and make necessary adjustments, according to paper size.
rationale: Loading presses with paper and making mechanical adjustments requires physical manipulation of equipment and materials.

task: Obtain or mix inks and fill ink fountains.
rationale: Obtaining/mixing inks and filling fountains involves physical manipulation of ink materials and operation of equipment to handle liquids.

task: Secure printing plates to printing units and adjust tolerances.
rationale: Securing printing plates and adjusting tolerances requires physical manipulation of equipment and hands-on mechanical adjustment of printing units.

task: Start presses and pull proofs to check for ink coverage and density, alignment, and registration.
rationale: Starting presses and pulling proofs requires hands-on operation of printing machinery and physical inspection of printed materials for quality control.

task: Monitor environmental factors, such as humidity and temperature, that may impact equipment performance and make necessary adjustments.
rationale: Monitoring environmental factors requires physical presence at work site to measure and observe conditions, then making physical adjustments to equipment settings or controls.

task: Set up or operate auxiliary equipment, such as cutting, folding, plate-making, drilling, or laminating machines.
rationale: Operating physical machinery (cutting, folding, drilling, laminating machines) requires hands-on manipulation and operation of tangible equipment.

task: Bind new books, using hand tools such as bone folders, knives, hammers, or brass binding tools.
rationale: Binding books requires physical manipulation using hand tools (bone folders, knives, hammers, brass tools) to fold, cut, shape, and assemble materials.

task: Compress sewed or glued signatures, using hand presses or smashing machines.
rationale: Operating hand presses or smashing machines to compress signatures involves physical tool operation and manipulation of tangible materials.

task: Cut binder boards to specified dimensions, using board shears, hand cutters, or cutting machines.
rationale: Requires physical operation of cutting tools/machines and manipulation of binder boards to achieve specified dimensions.

task: Cut cover material to specified dimensions, fitting and gluing material to binder boards by hand or machine.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials (cutting, fitting, gluing) by hand or machine operation on tangible objects.

task: Design original or special bindings for limited editions or other custom binding projects.
rationale: Designing bindings involves physical manipulation of materials, hand-binding techniques, and hands-on craftsmanship in creating custom book bindings.

task: Form book bodies by folding and sewing printed sheets to form signatures and assembling signatures in numerical order.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of sheets, folding, sewing, and assembling book components together.

task: Imprint or emboss lettering, designs, or numbers on book covers, using gold, silver, or colored foil, and stamping machines.
rationale: Requires operating stamping machines and physically manipulating book covers with foil to imprint designs—hands-on execution with tools.

task: Insert book bodies in devices that form back edges of books into convex shapes and produce grooves that facilitate cover attachment.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of book bodies and operation of machinery to shape and groove book backs for binding.

task: Clean and help maintain equipment or work areas and sterilize glassware, according to prescribed methods.
rationale: Task involves physical cleaning, maintenance of equipment/work areas, and sterilizing glassware—all hands-on activities.

task: Perform highly skilled hand finishing binding operations, such as grooving or lettering.
rationale: Binding operations require physical manipulation of materials and skilled hand-tool operation for grooving and lettering work.

task: Punch holes in and fasten paper sheets, signatures, or other material, using hand or machine punches and staplers.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of papers and operation of hand/machine tools (punches, staplers) to fasten materials together.

task: Repair, restore, or rebind old, rare, or damaged books, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of books and operation of hand tools for repair, restoration, and rebinding work.

task: Set up or operate bindery machines, such as coil binders, thermal or tape binders, plastic comb binders, or specialty binders.
rationale: Operating bindery machines involves physical manipulation of equipment, feeding materials, and controlling machinery to bind documents.

task: Set up or operate glue machines by filling glue reservoirs, turning switches to activate heating elements, or adjusting glue flow or conveyor speed.
rationale: Task involves operating physical machinery, filling reservoirs, adjusting controls, and operating conveyor systems—all hands-on physical execution.

task: Set up or operate machines that perform binding operations, such as pressing, folding, or trimming.
rationale: Requires operation of physical machinery and equipment to perform binding operations (pressing, folding, trimming).

task: Stitch or glue endpapers, bindings, backings, or signatures, using sewing machines, glue machines, or glue and brushes.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of materials (stitching, gluing) and operation of machinery (sewing machines, glue machines) to assemble book components.

task: Trim edges of books to size, using cutting machines, book trimming machines, or hand cutters.
rationale: Primary execution requires operating physical cutting machines and machinery to trim book edges, involving hands-on mechanical operation.

task: Examine stitched, collated, bound, or unbound product samples for defects, such as imperfect bindings, ink spots, torn pages, loose pages, or loose or uncut threads.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of tangible product samples—handling, examining, and touching materials to identify visual defects.

task: Install or adjust bindery machine devices, such as knives, guides, rollers, rounding forms, creasing rams, or clamps, to accommodate sheets, signatures, or books of specified sizes.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and adjustment of machine devices (knives, guides, rollers, clamps) to configure equipment for specific job requirements.

task: Lubricate, clean, or make minor repairs to machine parts to keep machines in working condition.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of machine parts: lubricating, cleaning, and repairing involve hands-on work with tangible objects.

task: Prepare finished books for shipping by wrapping or packing books and stacking boxes on pallets.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation: wrapping/packing books, stacking boxes. Requires hands-on execution with tangible objects.

task: Create prints according to customer specifications and laboratory protocols.
rationale: Creating prints involves physical manipulation of printing equipment, materials, and handling tangible outputs to meet specifications.

task: Examine developed prints for defects, such as broken lines, spots, or blurs.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of tangible prints, examining them visually and likely handling them to detect defects.

task: Fill tanks of processing machines with solutions such as developer, dyes, stop-baths, fixers, bleaches, or washes.
rationale: Filling tanks requires physical manipulation of liquid containers and operation of processing equipment.

task: Immerse film, negatives, paper, or prints in developing solutions, fixing solutions, and water to complete photographic development processes.
rationale: Requires physical immersion of materials in chemical solutions; involves hands-on manipulation of tangible objects in a photographic darkroom.

task: Insert processed negatives and prints into envelopes for delivery to customers.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tangible objects (negatives, prints, envelopes) and manual assembly/insertion work.

task: Order, label, and count stock of medications, chemicals, or supplies and enter inventory data into computer.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of stock items (ordering, labeling, counting) plus computer data entry, with primary execution being hands-on inventory work.

task: Load circuit boards, racks or rolls of film, negatives, or printing paper into processing or printing machines.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical handling and loading tangible objects (circuit boards, film, paper) into machines.

task: Measure and mix chemicals to prepare solutions for processing, according to formulas.
rationale: Measuring and mixing chemicals requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and use of measurement tools and equipment.

task: Operate special equipment to perform tasks such as transferring film to videotape or producing photographic enlargements.
rationale: Task requires operating physical equipment (film transfer machines, enlargement apparatus) and hands-on manipulation of tangible media.

task: Place sensitized paper in frames of projection printers, photostats, or other reproduction machines.
rationale: Task involves physically handling and installing sensitized paper into frames and machines.

task: Produce color or black-and-white photographs, negatives, or slides, applying standard photographic reproduction techniques and procedures.
rationale: Producing photographs requires operating cameras, manipulating film/equipment, and hands-on application of photographic techniques and procedures.

task: Retouch photographic negatives or original prints to correct defects.
rationale: Physical manipulation of photographic negatives/prints using tools (brushes, chemicals, hands) to correct defects.

task: Set or adjust machine controls, according to specifications, type of operation, or material requirements.
rationale: Setting or adjusting machine controls requires physical manipulation of control interfaces, switches, dials, or knobs on machinery equipment.

task: Clean or maintain photoprocessing or darkroom equipment, using ultrasonic equipment or cleaning and rinsing solutions.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of equipment, use of ultrasonic cleaning tools, and application of chemical solutions—all hands-on operations.

task: Transfer medication from vials to the appropriate number of sterile, disposable syringes, using aseptic techniques.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of vials and syringes, aseptic technique execution, and manual dexterity to transfer medication safely.

task: Examine quality of film fades or dissolves for potential color corrections, using color analyzers.
rationale: Examining film transitions and operating color analyzer equipment requires physical handling and operation of tools at a workstation.

task: Produce timed prints with separate densities or color settings for each scene of a production.
rationale: Producing timed prints requires physical operation of printing equipment, adjusting settings, and handling physical prints for different scenes.

task: Splice broken or separated film and mount film on reels.
rationale: Splicing and mounting film involves direct physical manipulation of tangible objects and use of tools to repair and prepare film materials.

task: Thread filmstrips through densitometers or sensitometers and expose film to light to determine density of film, necessary color corrections, or light sensitivity.
rationale: Threading filmstrips and operating densitometers/sensitometers are physical tasks involving equipment manipulation and manual film handling.

task: Mix pharmaceutical preparations, according to written prescriptions.
rationale: Mixing pharmaceutical preparations requires physical manipulation of ingredients, tools, and containers to prepare medications according to specifications.

task: Deliver medications or pharmaceutical supplies to patients, nursing stations, or surgery.
rationale: Requires physical handling and transport of tangible pharmaceutical items to specific locations, with direct delivery to patients or medical staff.

task: Inspect newly installed energy-efficient equipment to ensure that it was installed properly and is performing according to specifications.
rationale: Task requires physical inspection of equipment on-site, examining installation quality and performance through hands-on examination.

task: Maintain and merchandise home healthcare products or services.
rationale: Merchandise involves physical display and stocking of products; maintenance requires hands-on service work with equipment or supplies.

task: Create landscapes that minimize water consumption such as by incorporating drought-resistant grasses or indigenous plants.
rationale: Creating landscapes requires physical planting, soil preparation, and manipulation of plants and materials on site.

task: Hand instruments and supplies to surgeons and surgeons' assistants, hold retractors and cut sutures, and perform other tasks as directed by surgeon during operation.
rationale: Handing instruments, holding retractors, cutting sutures, and assisting during surgery require direct physical manipulation and presence in the operating room.

task: Develop or implement engineering solutions to clean up industrial accidents or other contaminated sites.
rationale: Implementing cleanup solutions involves physical manipulation of equipment, materials, and contaminated sites to execute remediation procedures.

task: Scrub arms and hands and assist the surgical team to scrub and put on gloves, masks, and surgical clothing.
rationale: Physical scrubbing and hands-on assistance dressing surgical team requires direct bodily contact and physical manipulation of materials.

task: Select or install combined heat units, power units, cogeneration equipment, or trigeneration equipment that reduces energy use or pollution.
rationale: Selecting and installing equipment requires physical manipulation and operation of machinery, tools, and tangible equipment at a work site.

task: Wash and sterilize equipment, using germicides and sterilizers.
rationale: Washing and sterilizing equipment requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and application of germicides/sterilizers via manual handling.

task: Prepare, care for, and dispose of tissue specimens taken for laboratory analysis.
rationale: Involves physical handling of tissue specimens, lab equipment operation, and hands-on specimen preparation and disposal procedures.

task: Prepare dressings or bandages and apply or assist with their application following surgery.
rationale: Task requires physical handling of medical materials and direct physical application to patient wounds post-surgery.

task: Operate, assemble, adjust, or monitor sterilizers, lights, suction machines, or diagnostic equipment to ensure proper operation.
rationale: Operating and assembling physical equipment requires hands-on manipulation and monitoring of machines to ensure proper function.

task: Operate, test, or maintain robotic equipment used for green production applications, such as waste-to-energy conversion systems, minimization of material waste, or replacement of human operators in dangerous work environments.
rationale: Operating, testing, and maintaining robotic equipment requires physical interaction with machinery and hands-on technical work.

task: Decontaminate or test field equipment used to clean or test pollutants from soil, air, or water.
rationale: Decontaminating and testing equipment requires hands-on physical manipulation and operation of tools and instruments in the field.

task: Prepare and package environmental samples for shipping or testing.
rationale: Preparing and packaging samples requires physical manipulation of tangible objects (containers, samples, packaging materials) and hands-on handling.

task: Improve fuel efficiency by testing vehicles or components that use lighter materials, such as aluminum, magnesium alloy, or plastic.
rationale: Testing vehicles or components requires hands-on physical manipulation, operation, and inspection of tangible materials and equipment.

task: Test performance of vehicles that use alternative fuels, such as alcohol blends, natural gas, liquefied petroleum gas, biodiesel, nano diesel, or alternative power methods, such as solar energy or hydrogen fuel cells.
rationale: Testing vehicle performance requires hands-on operation of vehicles, physical setup of testing equipment, and direct observation at test sites.

task: Observe patients' vital signs to assess physical condition.
rationale: Observing vital signs requires physical presence and hands-on interaction with patients (checking pulse, temperature, blood pressure, respiration).

task: Maintain supply of fluids, such as plasma, saline, blood, or glucose, for use during operations.
rationale: Maintaining supply requires physical handling of fluid containers, organizing inventory, and preparing materials for operational use.

task: Assemble components of energy-efficient optical communications systems involving photonic switches, optical backplanes, or optoelectronic interfaces.
rationale: Assembling components requires physical manipulation of photonic switches, optical backplanes, and optoelectronic interfaces—tangible objects that must be positioned, connected, and integrated.

task: Study historical climate change indicators found in locations, such as ice sheets or rock formations to develop climate change models.
rationale: Requires fieldwork at ice sheets and rock formations with physical sampling, core extraction, and hands-on geological examination in remote locations.

task: Record environmental data from field samples of soil, air, water, or pests to monitor the effectiveness of integrated pest management (IPM) practices.
rationale: Task requires physical collection of field samples (soil, air, water, pests) and on-site inspection to monitor IPM effectiveness.

task: Choose and prepare sites for new trees, using controlled burning, bulldozers, or herbicides to clear weeds, brush, and logging debris.
rationale: Requires physical operation of machinery (bulldozers), controlled burning, and application of herbicides to physically clear and prepare land.

task: Collect data related to ecological or human health risks at brownfield sites.
rationale: Data collection at brownfield sites requires physical presence on-site, hands-on sampling, testing equipment operation, and tangible environmental inspection.

task: Perform tests to identify any potential hazards related to recycled products used at green building sites.
rationale: Testing involves physical inspection, sampling, and handling of recycled materials to identify hazards. Requires hands-on examination of tangible objects.

task: Test or balance newly installed HVAC systems to determine whether indoor air quality standards are met.
rationale: Requires physical operation of HVAC equipment, handling test instruments, and on-site inspection to verify system performance.

task: Perform site activities required of green certified construction practices, such as implementing waste management procedures, identifying materials for reuse, or installing erosion or sedimentation control mechanisms.
rationale: Requires physical installation of control mechanisms, hands-on waste management, material handling, and on-site physical work to implement green practices.

task: Apply modular soil- and plant-containing grids over existing roof membranes to create green roofs.
rationale: Task requires physical installation of modular grids, soil, and plants on roofs—hands-on construction work with tangible materials and objects.

task: Apply reflective roof coatings, such as special paints or single-ply roofing sheets, to existing roofs to reduce solar heat absorption.
rationale: Requires physical application of materials (paint or sheets) to roof surfaces using tools and manual labor on-site.

task: Attach solar panels to existing roofs, according to specifications and without damaging roofing materials or the structural integrity of buildings.
rationale: Installing solar panels requires physical manipulation of equipment, tools, climbing, securing hardware, and hands-on work at job sites.

task: Install attic ventilation systems, such as turbine vents, gable or ridge vents, or conventional or solar-powered exhaust fans.
rationale: Requires physical installation of ventilation equipment on attics—climbing, mounting, fastening vents, fans, and ducting to structures.

task: Install layers of vegetation-based green roofs, including protective membranes, drainage, aeration, water retention and filter layers, soil substrates, irrigation materials, and plants.
rationale: Installing green roof layers requires hands-on physical assembly of materials, landscaping, and plant placement on a roof structure.

task: Install skylights on roofs to increase natural light inside structures or to reduce energy costs.
rationale: Installing skylights requires physical manipulation of materials, operation of tools, and work at height on roofs—essential manual execution.

task: Fabricate ducts for high efficiency heating, ventilating, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems to maximize efficiency of systems.
rationale: Fabricating ducts requires physical manipulation of materials, cutting, bending, assembling, and operating tools and machinery to construct HVAC components.

task: Perform building commissioning activities by completing mechanical inspections of a building's water, lighting, or heating, ventilating, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems.
rationale: Performing mechanical inspections requires physical presence on-site, hands-on examination, and testing of water, lighting, and HVAC systems.

task: Conduct environmental hazard inspections to identify or quantify problems, such as asbestos, poor air quality, water contamination, or other environmental hazards.
rationale: Requires physical presence at work sites to inspect, sample, and measure environmental hazards using specialized equipment and tools.

task: Inspect facilities or installations to determine their environmental impact.
rationale: Requires physical presence at facilities to observe, measure, and assess environmental conditions firsthand.

task: Sample and test air to identify gasses, such as bromine, ozone, or sulfur dioxide, or particulates, such as mold, dust, or allergens.
rationale: Sampling and testing air requires collecting physical samples using equipment and instruments, then conducting hands-on analysis of those samples.

task: Apply bioremediation techniques to hazardous wastes to allow naturally occurring bacteria to break down toxic substances.
rationale: Applying bioremediation techniques requires physical manipulation of hazardous waste materials, setting up bacterial cultures, monitoring conditions, and hands-on site work.

task: Clean mold-contaminated sites by removing damaged porous materials or thoroughly cleaning all contaminated nonporous materials.
rationale: Physical removal or cleaning of contaminated materials requires direct hands-on manipulation and handling of tangible objects at work sites.

task: Identify or separate waste products or materials for recycling or reuse.
rationale: Requires physical sorting, handling, and manipulation of waste materials and objects for separation and recycling.

task: Process e-waste, such as computer components containing lead or mercury.
rationale: Processing e-waste requires physical handling, disassembly, and manipulation of hazardous computer components and materials.

task: Remove or limit contamination following emergencies involving hazardous substances.
rationale: Physical removal/limitation of hazardous contamination requires hands-on work with equipment and materials at emergency sites.

task: Sort specialized hazardous waste at landfills or disposal centers, following proper disposal procedures.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation and sorting of hazardous waste materials at a disposal site, requiring hands-on handling.

task: Apply green technologies or techniques, such as the use of coiled tubing, slim-hole drilling, horizontal drilling, hydraulic fracturing, or gas lift systems.
rationale: Applying drilling/completion technologies involves operating specialized equipment, tools, and machinery (coiled tubing units, drilling rigs, fracturing systems) in the field.

task: Scrape or wash conveyors, using belt scrapers or belt washers, to minimize dust production.
rationale: Operating physical tools (belt scrapers/washers) on equipment requires hands-on physical manipulation and tool operation.

task: Adjust or repair computer controlled exhaust emissions devices.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and repair of tangible devices, hands-on assembly/disassembly work.

task: Maintain or repair vehicles with alternative fuel systems, including biodiesel, hybrid, or compressed natural gas vehicles.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical manipulation, operation of tools, and hands-on repair work on vehicle fuel systems.

task: Measure vehicle emissions to determine whether they are within acceptable limits.
rationale: Requires physical operation of emissions testing equipment and hands-on measurement of vehicle exhaust.

task: Assemble boilers at installation sites, using tools such as levels, plumb bobs, hammers, torches, or other hand tools.
rationale: Assembling boilers requires physical manipulation of equipment and hand tools like levels, hammers, and torches at installation sites.

task: Install equipment to improve the energy or operational efficiency of residential or commercial buildings.
rationale: Installing equipment requires physical manipulation, tools, and hands-on work at building sites.

task: Perform routine maintenance on boilers, such as replacing burners or hoses, installing replacement parts, or reinforcing structural weaknesses to ensure optimal boiler efficiency.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of boiler components, tool operation, parts installation, and hands-on repair work at the equipment site.

task: Position, attach, or blow insulating materials to prevent energy losses from buildings, pipes, or other structures or objects.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of insulating materials—positioning, attaching, and applying them to structures. Direct hands-on work.

task: Assemble prototypes or integrated-technology demonstrators of new or emerging environmental technologies for aircraft.
rationale: Physical assembly of prototypes requires hands-on manipulation and construction of tangible components and systems.

task: Capture or segregate waste material, such as aluminum swarf, machine cutting fluid, or solvents, for recycling or environmentally responsible disposal.
rationale: Physically handling, collecting, and segregating tangible waste materials requires hands-on manipulation and physical presence at work site.

task: Clean aircraft structures, parts, or components, using aqueous, semi-aqueous, aliphatic hydrocarbon, or organic solvent cleaning products or techniques to reduce carbon or other harmful emissions.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of aircraft parts using cleaning products, tools, and techniques to physically clean structures and components.

task: Dispose of scrap or waste material in accordance with company policies and environmental regulations.
rationale: Disposing of scrap/waste requires physical handling and manipulation of materials, plus likely operation of disposal equipment.

task: Separate scrap waste and related materials for reuse, recycling, or disposal.
rationale: Separating scrap waste requires physical handling and manipulation of materials for sorting and classification.

task: Immobilize patient for placement on stretcher and ambulance transport, using backboard or other spinal immobilization device.

task: Maintain vehicles and medical and communication equipment, and replenish first aid equipment and supplies.

task: Perform emergency diagnostic and treatment procedures, such as stomach suction, airway management, or heart monitoring, during ambulance ride.

task: Administer drugs, orally or by injection, or perform intravenous procedures.

task: Administer first aid treatment or life support care to sick or injured persons in prehospital settings.

task: Assess nature and extent of illness or injury to establish and prioritize medical procedures.

task: Dress with equipment such as fire-resistant clothing and breathing apparatus.

task: Respond to fire alarms and other calls for assistance, such as automobile and industrial accidents.

task: Drive and operate fire fighting vehicles and equipment.

task: Participate in fire drills and demonstrations of fire fighting techniques.

task: Clean and maintain fire stations and fire fighting equipment and apparatus.

task: Inspect buildings for fire hazards and compliance with fire prevention ordinances, testing and checking smoke alarms and fire suppression equipment as necessary.

task: Take action to contain any hazardous chemicals that could catch fire, leak, or spill.

task: Administer first aid and cardiopulmonary resuscitation to injured persons or provide emergency medical care such as basic or advanced life support.

task: Operate pumps connected to high-pressure hoses.

task: Search to locate fire survivors.

task: Train new employees to control and suppress fires.

task: Package collected pieces of evidence in securely closed containers, such as bags, crates, or boxes, to protect them.

task: Conduct inspections and acceptance testing of newly installed fire protection systems.

task: Acquire, distribute and store supplies.

task: Inspect and test fire protection or fire detection systems to verify that such systems are installed in accordance with appropriate laws, codes, ordinances, regulations, and standards.

task: Conduct fire exit drills to monitor and evaluate evacuation procedures.

task: Test sites and materials to establish facts, such as burn patterns and flash points of materials, using test equipment.

task: Dust evidence or portions of fire scenes for latent fingerprints.

task: Check victims for signs of life, such as breathing and pulse.

task: Secure persons at scene, keeping witnesses from conversing or leaving the scene before investigators arrive.

task: Participate or assist in raids and arrests.

task: Block or rope off scene and check perimeter to ensure that entire scene is secured.

task: Search for and collect evidence, such as fingerprints, using investigative equipment.
