task: Fit and assemble parts to make or repair machine tools.
rationale: Fitting and assembling parts requires hands-on physical manipulation of machine tool components and use of tools.

task: Photograph layouts, using camera, to make layout prints for supervisors or clients.
rationale: Requires operating a camera (physical tool) and physical manipulation to photograph layouts; the work product is created through hands-on equipment operation.

task: Install experimental parts or assemblies, such as hydraulic systems, electrical wiring, lubricants, or batteries into machines or mechanisms.
rationale: Installing parts and assemblies into machines requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and operation of physical tools.

task: Set up or operate metalworking, brazing, heat-treating, welding, or cutting equipment.
rationale: Operating metalworking equipment (welding, cutting, brazing) is hands-on physical operation of machinery requiring direct manipulation.

task: Test experimental models under simulated operating conditions, for purposes such as development, standardization, or feasibility of design.
rationale: Testing experimental models requires hands-on operation and physical manipulation of equipment under controlled conditions to evaluate performance and design feasibility.

task: Adjust machine components and machine tension and pressure according to size or processing angle of product.
rationale: Adjusting machine components, tension, and pressure requires hands-on physical manipulation of tangible mechanical parts.

task: Tend or operate machine that packages product.
rationale: Operating a packaging machine requires hands-on operation of physical machinery and interaction with tangible objects being packaged.

task: Remove finished packaged items from machine and separate rejected items.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation: removing packaged items from machine and manually separating rejected items by hand.

task: Regulate machine flow, speed, or temperature.
rationale: Operating machine controls requires physical interaction with equipment controls and levers to adjust settings.

task: Stop or reset machines when malfunctions occur, clear machine jams, and report malfunctions to a supervisor.
rationale: Stopping machines, clearing jams require physical manipulation and presence at equipment; reporting is secondary to primary manual execution.

task: Secure finished packaged items by hand tying, sewing, gluing, stapling, or attaching fastener.
rationale: Securing items requires physical manipulation using hands, tools, and fastening methods on tangible objects.

task: Stock and sort product for packaging or filling machine operation, and replenish packaging supplies, such as wrapping paper, plastic sheet, boxes, cartons, glue, ink, or labels.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and movement of packaging materials, boxes, supplies, and stocking items at a work site.

task: Inspect and remove defective products and packaging material.
rationale: Task requires physical inspection of products, touching/examining objects, and physically removing defective items and materials.

task: Clean and remove damaged or otherwise inferior materials to prepare raw products for processing.
rationale: Physical manipulation of raw materials—cleaning, inspecting, and removing damaged items—requires hands-on work with tangible objects.

task: Sort, grade, weigh, and inspect products, verifying and adjusting product weight or measurement to meet specifications.
rationale: Sorting, grading, weighing, and inspecting products requires physical manipulation, operation of scales/measurement tools, and hands-on interaction with tangible objects.

task: Clean, oil, and make minor adjustments or repairs to machinery and equipment, such as opening valves or setting guides.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of machinery—cleaning, oiling, adjusting valves, and repairs require hands-on work with tangible equipment.

task: Monitor the production line, watching for problems such as pile-ups, jams, or glue that isn't sticking properly.
rationale: Monitoring a production line requires physical presence on-site to observe tangible operational conditions (pile-ups, jams, adhesive failures) in real time.

task: Stack finished packaged items, or wrap protective material around each item, and pack the items in cartons or containers.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation: stacking items, wrapping materials, packing into containers—all hands-on work with tangible objects.

task: Start machine by engaging controls.
rationale: Operating physical machine controls requires hands-on manipulation of tangible equipment.

task: Package the product in the form in which it will be sent out, for example, filling bags with flour from a chute or spout.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tangible objects (bags, flour) and operation of equipment (chute/spout) to fill and package products.

task: Supply materials to spindles, conveyors, hoppers, or other feeding devices and unload packaged product.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials, loading/unloading products, and operating feeding devices—primary execution is hands-on physical work.

task: Attach identification labels to finished packaged items, or cut stencils and stencil information on containers, such as lot numbers or shipping destinations.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of items (attaching labels, cutting stencils, applying stencil marks) and handling tangible objects.

task: Clean packaging containers, line and pad crates, or assemble cartons to prepare for product packing.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of containers, crates, and cartons—handling, lining, padding, and assembling tangible objects.

task: Inspect vehicles and check gas, oil, and water levels prior to departure.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of vehicles and checking fluid levels—hands-on examination of tangible objects.

task: Park vehicles at loading areas so that passengers can board.
rationale: Requires operating a vehicle and physical presence at a worksite to position it for passenger boarding.

task: Load and unload baggage in baggage compartments.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation—lifting, carrying, and placing baggage into/from compartments.

task: Drive trucks to deliver such items as food, medical supplies, or newspapers.
rationale: Driving trucks is direct operation of a vehicle, a core manual task requiring physical control and presence.

task: Collect coins from vending machines, refill machines, and remove aged merchandise.
rationale: Collecting coins and merchandise requires hands-on physical manipulation of vending machines and tangible objects.

task: Review lists of dealers, customers, or station drops and load trucks.
rationale: Loading trucks requires physical manipulation and operation of equipment to place goods onto vehicles.

task: Arrange merchandise and sales promotion displays or issue sales promotion materials to customers.
rationale: Arranging merchandise displays requires physical manipulation and movement of objects. Issuing promotional materials involves handing items to customers—tangible physical interaction.

task: Maintain trucks and food-dispensing equipment and clean inside of machines that dispense food or beverages.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of equipment, operating tools for maintenance, and hands-on cleaning of machines. Direct physical work is primary execution.

task: Take numbered tags from customers, locate vehicles, and deliver vehicles, or provide customers with instructions for locating vehicles.
rationale: Task requires physical vehicle operation (driving), locating vehicles at specific locations, and bodily movement at work site to interact with customers and vehicles.

task: Keep parking areas clean and orderly to ensure that space usage is maximized.
rationale: Keeping parking areas clean and orderly requires physical movement, sweeping, picking up debris, and physically arranging items—manual labor work.

task: Direct motorists to parking areas or parking spaces, using hand signals or flashlights as necessary.
rationale: Directing motorists requires physical presence at work site and operation of hand signals/flashlights. Physical bodily positioning and tool use are essential to execute the task.

task: Patrol parking areas to prevent vehicle damage and vehicle or property thefts.
rationale: Requires physical presence at work site walking/patrolling parking areas to observe and detect threats.

task: Park and retrieve automobiles for customers in parking lots, storage garages, or new car lots.
rationale: Requires operating vehicles (driving) and physical manipulation (parking, retrieving cars) in work sites.

task: Greet customers and open their car doors.
rationale: Opening car doors requires physical manipulation of objects; greeting is secondary to the physical action component.

task: Lift, position, and remove barricades to open or close parking areas.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and positioning of tangible objects (barricades) to control access to parking areas.

task: Inspect vehicles to detect any damage.
rationale: Inspecting vehicles for damage requires physical presence and hands-on examination of tangible objects.

task: Escort customers to their vehicles to ensure their safety.
rationale: Requires physical presence and bodily movement to walk with customers to vehicles—direct physical interaction in the environment.

task: Perform maintenance on cars in storage to protect tires, batteries, or exteriors from deterioration.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of vehicle components (tires, batteries, exterior) to perform maintenance work.

task: Move controls to drive gasoline- or electric-powered trucks, cars, or tractors and transport materials between loading, processing, and storage areas.
rationale: Primary execution is operating vehicle controls and driving, which requires hands-on physical manipulation of machinery and bodily presence to navigate and transport materials.

task: Move levers or controls that operate lifting devices, such as forklifts, lift beams with swivel-hooks, hoists, or elevating platforms, to load, unload, transport, or stack material.
rationale: Operating physical machinery controls (levers/controls) to manipulate lifting devices requires hands-on physical operation of equipment to move materials.

task: Position lifting devices under, over, or around loaded pallets, skids, or boxes and secure material or products for transport to designated areas.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical operation of lifting devices and manual handling of cargo to position and secure materials for transport.

task: Perform routine maintenance on vehicles or auxiliary equipment, such as cleaning, lubricating, recharging batteries, fueling, or replacing liquefied-gas tank.
rationale: Task requires hands-on physical manipulation: cleaning, lubricating, fueling, and replacing tanks. Direct physical interaction with equipment.

task: Weigh materials or products and record weight or other production data on tags or labels.
rationale: Weighing materials involves physical manipulation and operation of scales; recording on tags/labels requires physical placement and handling.

task: Operate or tend automatic stacking, loading, packaging, or cutting machines.
rationale: Operating and tending machines requires hands-on interaction with physical machinery to control, monitor, and manage equipment during production.

task: Inspect meals served for conformance to prescribed diets and standards of palatability and appearance.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of meals—viewing and potentially tasting to assess palatability, appearance, and dietary conformance.

task: Turn valves and open chutes to dump, spray, or release materials from dump cars or storage bins into hoppers.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of valves and chutes to control material flow from storage—direct hands-on operation of equipment.

task: Mark and label containers, container tags, or products, using marking tools.
rationale: Physical manipulation of containers and tools to apply marks and labels requires hands-on execution at the work site.

task: Measure, weigh, and count products and materials.
rationale: Physical inspection and handling of tangible objects to measure, weigh, and count them requires hands-on interaction.

task: Remove completed or defective products or materials, placing them on moving equipment, such as conveyors, or in specified areas, such as loading docks.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of products/materials, placing them on conveyors or in specified locations—direct manual handling.

task: Seal containers or materials, using glues, fasteners, nails, and hand tools.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of objects using glues, fasteners, nails, and hand tools to seal containers.

task: Load materials and products into package processing equipment.
rationale: Loading materials and products into equipment requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and operation of processing machinery.

task: Assemble, line, and pad cartons, crates, and containers, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of containers using hand tools to assemble, line, and pad them.

task: Clean containers, materials, supplies, or work areas, using cleaning solutions and hand tools.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of hand tools and cleaning solutions to clean tangible objects and work areas.

task: Transport packages to customers' vehicles.
rationale: Transporting packages requires physical manipulation and movement of tangible objects to customers' vehicles.

task: Place or pour products or materials into containers, using hand tools and equipment, or fill containers from spouts or chutes.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of materials into containers using hand tools, equipment, and direct bodily effort—core manual work.

task: Obtain, move, and sort products, materials, containers, and orders, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of objects (obtaining, moving, sorting) using hand tools in a hands-on manner.

task: Provide presentation and product demonstration support during the introduction of new products and services to field staff and customers.
rationale: Product demonstration requires physical operation and handling of tangible products to show customers/staff how they work.

task: Organize, develop, analyze, test, and prepare special meals, such as low-fat, low-cholesterol, or chemical-free meals.
rationale: Preparing special meals requires physical manipulation: cooking, food handling, and meal assembly.

task: Inspect or review projects to monitor compliance with building and safety codes or other regulations.
rationale: Inspecting projects requires physical presence at work sites to examine buildings, structures, and materials firsthand to verify code compliance.

task: Oversee the preparation and care of the remains of people who have died.
rationale: Task requires hands-on physical preparation and care of human remains, including washing, positioning, embalming, or dressing—direct bodily contact and physical manipulation.

task: Provide information on funeral service options, products, or merchandise, and maintain a casket display area.
rationale: Maintaining a casket display area requires physical presence, arrangement, and handling of tangible objects (caskets and merchandise).

task: Close caskets and lead funeral corteges to churches or burial sites.
rationale: Closing caskets involves physical manipulation of objects. Leading corteges requires physical presence and bodily movement at work sites.

task: Perform embalming duties, as necessary.
rationale: Embalming requires direct physical manipulation of a deceased body, application of chemicals, and hands-on technical procedures.

task: Direct preparations and shipment of bodies for out-of-state burial.
rationale: Preparing and shipping bodies requires physical handling, coordination, and hands-on execution of funeral/burial arrangements.

task: Plan placement of caskets at funeral sites or place or adjust lights, fixtures, or floral displays.
rationale: Physically placing, positioning, and adjusting caskets, lights, fixtures, and floral displays requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Test new food products and equipment.
rationale: Testing food products and equipment requires hands-on physical interaction, tasting, handling, operating machinery, and inspecting tangible items.

task: Receive or usher people to their seats for services.
rationale: Ushering requires physical presence, bodily movement, and direct interaction with people to guide them to seats.

task: Examine eyes, using observation, instruments, and pharmaceutical agents, to determine visual acuity and perception, focus, and coordination and to diagnose diseases and other abnormalities, such as glaucoma or color blindness.
rationale: Requires physical examination using instruments (tonometry, retinoscopy), pharmaceutical agents (drops), and direct observation of patient's eyes. Hands-on diagnostic work.

task: Prescribe, supply, fit and adjust eyeglasses, contact lenses, and other vision aids.
rationale: Task involves physical fitting and adjustment of vision aids on patients, requiring hands-on manipulation and direct physical interaction.

task: Remove foreign bodies from the eye.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and hands-on inspection of the eye to locate and remove foreign objects.

task: Provide patients undergoing eye surgeries, such as cataract and laser vision correction, with pre- and post-operative care.
rationale: Providing pre- and post-operative care involves physical patient interaction: eye examinations, wound care, applying medications, and hands-on assessment of surgical outcomes.

task: Provide vision therapy and low-vision rehabilitation.
rationale: Requires hands-on physical assessment, testing equipment operation, and direct patient interaction for visual rehabilitation.

task: Delineate aerial photographic detail, such as control points, hydrography, topography, and cultural features, using precision stereoplotting apparatus or drafting instruments.
rationale: Physical operation of stereoplotting apparatus and drafting instruments to delineate photographic detail onto maps or documents.

task: Travel over photographed areas to observe, identify, record, and verify all relevant features.
rationale: Requires physical travel to actual locations and in-person observation to identify and verify features beyond desk review of photographs.

task: Plan and conduct ground surveys designed to establish baselines, elevations, and other geodetic measurements.
rationale: Conducting ground surveys requires physical presence on-site, operating surveying instruments (theodolites, GPS equipment), and physically establishing benchmarks and measurement points.

task: Adjust surveying instruments to maintain their accuracy.
rationale: Physical manipulation and calibration of surveying instruments requires hands-on work with tangible objects.

task: Establish fixed points for use in making maps, using geodetic and engineering instruments.
rationale: Requires physical operation of geodetic instruments and site presence to establish survey points and landmarks in the field.

task: Determine longitudes and latitudes of important features and boundaries in survey areas, using theodolites, transits, levels, and satellite-based global positioning systems (GPS).
rationale: Uses physical instruments (theodolites, transits, levels, GPS equipment) and requires physical presence at survey sites to measure and determine precise coordinates.

task: Locate and mark sites selected for geophysical prospecting activities, such as efforts to locate petroleum or other mineral products.
rationale: Locating and marking sites requires physical presence on-site to survey terrain, place markers, and interact with the physical landscape for geophysical prospecting.

task: Survey bodies of water to determine navigable channels and to secure data for construction of breakwaters, piers, and other marine structures.
rationale: Surveying water bodies requires physical presence on-site, operating surveying equipment, and collecting field data through measurement and observation of actual physical conditions.

task: Direct aerial surveys of specified geographical areas.
rationale: Operating aircraft to conduct aerial surveys requires piloting physical machinery and bodily presence at work site.

task: Perform laboratory studies of steps in manufacture of new products and test proposed processes in small-scale operation, such as a pilot plant.
rationale: Requires hands-on testing of processes, operation of pilot plant equipment, and physical manipulation of materials in laboratory/small-scale manufacturing settings.

task: Inspect mining areas for unsafe structures, equipment, and working conditions.
rationale: Requires physical presence at mining sites to visually and tactilely inspect structures, equipment, and conditions firsthand.

task: Lay out materials such as puzzles, scissors and eating utensils for use in therapy, and clean and repair these tools after therapy sessions.
rationale: Laying out, cleaning, and repairing physical therapy materials (puzzles, scissors, utensils) requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Inspect oil and gas wells to determine that installations are completed.
rationale: Inspecting oil and gas wells requires physical presence on-site to examine equipment, installations, and infrastructure in person.

task: Take samples to assess the amount and quality of oil, the depth at which resources lie, and the equipment needed to properly extract them.
rationale: Task requires physical sampling of oil resources, drilling to assess depth, and hands-on geological examination of materials.

task: Test machinery and equipment to ensure that it is safe and conforms to performance specifications.
rationale: Testing machinery requires physical operation of equipment and hands-on inspection to verify safety and performance specifications.

task: Plan, prepare, or carry out individually designed programs of physical treatment to maintain, improve, or restore physical functioning, alleviate pain, or prevent physical dysfunction in patients.
rationale: Physical treatment programs involve hands-on manipulation of patients' bodies, therapeutic exercises, manual therapy, and physical presence at a treatment site.

task: Perform and document an initial exam, evaluating data to identify problems and determine a diagnosis prior to intervention.
rationale: Initial exam requires physical inspection and hands-on evaluation to assess conditions and gather clinical data directly.

task: Receive, set up, test, or decontaminate equipment.
rationale: Task involves hands-on physical work: receiving, assembling/setting up, testing, and decontaminating equipment—all require direct physical manipulation and handling.

task: Inspect facilities to monitor compliance with regulations governing substances, such as asbestos, lead, or wastewater.
rationale: Requires physical inspection and presence on-site to examine facilities for regulated substances like asbestos and lead.

task: Assist in the cleanup of hazardous material spills.
rationale: Cleaning hazardous material spills requires physical manipulation, handling of materials, use of protective equipment and specialized tools, and hands-on remediation of contaminated areas.

task: Administer manual exercises, massage, or traction to help relieve pain, increase patient strength, or decrease or prevent deformity or crippling.
rationale: Physical manipulation of patient body through exercises, massage, and traction; hands-on therapeutic intervention.

task: Examine physiological, morphological, and cultural characteristics, using microscope, to identify and classify microorganisms in human, water, and food specimens.
rationale: Requires hands-on use of microscope and physical handling/preparation of specimens to examine and identify microorganisms.

task: Observe action of microorganisms upon living tissues of plants, higher animals, and other microorganisms, and on dead organic matter.
rationale: Observing microorganisms requires physical examination under microscopes, in lab settings, handling specimens and conducting experiments with tangible biological materials.

task: Use a variety of specialized equipment, such as electron microscopes, gas and high-pressure liquid chromatographs, electrophoresis units, thermocyclers, fluorescence-activated cell sorters, and phosphorimagers.
rationale: Operating specialized laboratory equipment requires hands-on physical operation of complex machines and instruments.

task: Conduct chemical analyses of substances such as acids, alcohols, and enzymes.
rationale: Conducting chemical analyses requires hands-on laboratory work with tangible substances, equipment operation (analyzers, spectrometers), and physical sample handling.

task: Perform routine maintenance on park structures.
rationale: Maintenance involves physical manipulation of structures (fixing, replacing parts, cleaning), requiring hands-on work with tools and equipment.

task: Construct historical, scientific, and nature visitor-center displays.
rationale: Constructing displays requires physical assembly, installation, and manipulation of materials, components, and objects on-site.

task: Take photographs and motion pictures for use in lectures and publications and to develop displays.
rationale: Requires physical operation of cameras and photography equipment to capture images and video for visual documentation.

task: Survey park to determine forest conditions and distribution and abundance of fauna and flora.
rationale: Surveying park requires physical presence in field, hands-on data collection, observing/inspecting flora and fauna directly on-site.

task: Plan or conduct geological, geochemical, or geophysical field studies or surveys, sample collection, or drilling and testing programs used to collect data for research or application.
rationale: Field studies involve physical sample collection, drilling, testing, and hands-on site work requiring bodily presence and manipulation of equipment and geological materials.

task: Investigate the composition, structure, or history of the Earth's crust through the collection, examination, measurement, or classification of soils, minerals, rocks, or fossil remains.
rationale: Collection, examination, and measurement of physical specimens (soils, minerals, rocks, fossils) require hands-on field and lab work with tangible materials.

task: Measure characteristics of the Earth, such as gravity or magnetic fields, using equipment such as seismographs, gravimeters, torsion balances, or magnetometers.
rationale: Task requires operating physical equipment (seismographs, gravimeters, magnetometers) and hands-on interaction with instruments to collect measurements.

task: Test industrial diamonds or abrasives, soil, or rocks to determine their geological characteristics, using optical, x-ray, heat, acid, or precision instruments.
rationale: Requires physical operation of precision instruments, handling tangible samples, and hands-on testing procedures in laboratory setting.

task: Test and measure patient's strength, motor development and function, sensory perception, functional capacity, or respiratory or circulatory efficiency and record data.
rationale: Physical examination and hands-on testing of patient strength, motor function, and sensory perception requires direct physical interaction with the patient.

task: Isolate, identify and prepare specimens for examination.
rationale: Isolating, identifying, and preparing specimens requires physical manipulation of tangible samples and hands-on laboratory work.

task: Conduct research, or assist in the conduct of research, including the collection of information and samples, such as blood, water, soil, plants and animals.
rationale: Collecting physical samples (blood, water, soil, plants, animals) requires hands-on manipulation and physical presence to interact with the tangible objects.

task: Set up, adjust, calibrate, clean, maintain, and troubleshoot laboratory and field equipment.
rationale: Task requires hands-on physical manipulation, adjustment, calibration, and maintenance of laboratory and field equipment—core manual work.

task: Clean, maintain and prepare supplies and work areas.
rationale: Physical manipulation of objects, cleaning supplies, and workspace organization. Hands-on execution required.

task: Participate in the research, development, or manufacturing of medicinal and pharmaceutical preparations.
rationale: Involves hands-on work with pharmaceutical materials, equipment operation, chemical synthesis, testing, and manufacturing processes.

task: Conduct standardized biological, microbiological or biochemical tests and laboratory analyses to evaluate the quantity or quality of physical or chemical substances in food or other products.
rationale: Conducting standardized laboratory tests requires hands-on manipulation of equipment, samples, and instruments to perform physical and chemical analyses.

task: Measure or weigh compounds and solutions for use in testing or animal feed.
rationale: Measuring and weighing compounds involves physical manipulation of tangible materials using scales and measurement equipment.

task: Examine animals and specimens to detect the presence of disease or other problems.
rationale: Physical examination of animals/specimens requires hands-on inspection, palpation, and direct visual observation to detect disease.

task: Feed livestock or laboratory animals.
rationale: Feeding animals requires physical handling of feed, pouring, distributing, and direct physical presence with the animals.

task: Transport and accompany clients to shopping areas or to appointments, using automobile.
rationale: Requires operating an automobile (physical tool operation) and physically accompanying clients, involving bodily presence and vehicle operation.

task: Demonstrate use and care of equipment for tenant use.
rationale: Demonstrating equipment requires physical handling and showing tenants hands-on use, not just verbal instruction.

task: Administer treatment involving application of physical agents, using equipment, moist packs, ultraviolet or infrared lamps, or ultrasound machines.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of therapeutic equipment (lamps, ultrasound machines) and application of physical agents (moist packs) to patients, involving hands-on execution.

task: Evaluate, fit, or adjust prosthetic or orthotic devices or recommend modification to orthotist.
rationale: Evaluating, fitting, and adjusting prosthetic/orthotic devices requires hands-on physical manipulation and inspection of devices and patient body.

task: Install, arrange, assemble, and prepare artifacts for exhibition, ensuring the artifacts' safety, reporting their status and condition, and identifying and correcting any problems with the set up.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and assembly of artifacts, hands-on installation, arrangement in space, and physical inspection of condition and setup.

task: Coordinate exhibit installations, assisting with design, constructing displays, dioramas, display cases, and models, and ensuring the availability of necessary materials.
rationale: Task involves constructing displays, dioramas, cases, and models—physical fabrication work requiring hands-on assembly and material manipulation.

task: Determine whether objects need repair and choose the safest and most effective method of repair.
rationale: Determining repair needs and executing repairs requires physical inspection, manipulation of objects, and operation of repair tools.

task: Clean objects, such as paper, textiles, wood, metal, glass, rock, pottery, and furniture, using cleansers, solvents, soap solutions, and polishes.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of objects and application of cleansers/polishes using hands and tools.

task: Prepare artifacts for storage and shipping.
rationale: Preparing artifacts requires physical manipulation—wrapping, packing, securing objects for storage and transport.

task: Specialize in particular materials or types of object, such as documents and books, paintings, decorative arts, textiles, metals, or architectural materials.
rationale: Specializing in conservation/restoration involves hands-on physical work with tangible objects like documents, paintings, textiles, and metals requiring careful manipulation and treatment.

task: Perform on-site field work which may involve interviewing people, inspecting and identifying artifacts, note-taking, viewing sites and collections, and repainting exhibition spaces.
rationale: Primary execution includes physical inspection of artifacts, on-site presence, and manual repainting of exhibition spaces—all require hands-on physical work and bodily presence at the work site.

task: Repair, restore, and reassemble artifacts, designing and fabricating missing or broken parts, to restore them to their original appearance and prevent deterioration.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of artifacts, fabrication of parts, and hands-on restoration and reassembly work.

task: Study object documentation or conduct standard chemical and physical tests to ascertain the object's age, composition, original appearance, need for treatment or restoration, and appropriate preservation method.
rationale: Requires physical handling of objects, conducting hands-on chemical/physical tests, inspection, and assessment work.

task: Perform tests and examinations to establish storage and conservation requirements, policies, and procedures.
rationale: Performing tests and examinations requires hands-on physical inspection, handling of materials, and direct observation to establish requirements.

task: Preserve or direct preservation of objects, using plaster, resin, sealants, hardeners, and shellac.
rationale: Applying plaster, resin, sealants, hardeners, and shellac requires physical manipulation of materials and tools on tangible objects.

task: Build, repair, and install wooden steps, scaffolds, and walkways to gain access to or permit improved view of exhibited equipment.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation (building, installing), tool operation (carpentry work), and hands-on construction of tangible wooden structures.

task: Construct, maintain, or repair medical supportive devices.
rationale: Constructing, maintaining, or repairing medical devices requires physical manipulation, assembly, and hands-on work with tangible equipment.

task: Test equipment to ensure proper operation.
rationale: Testing equipment requires hands-on operation and physical interaction with machinery to verify functionality.

task: Set and monitor product standards, examining samples of raw products or directing testing during processing, to ensure finished products are of prescribed quality.
rationale: Examining product samples requires physical inspection and hands-on evaluation of tangible raw materials and products during processing.

task: Operate equipment to produce programs or broadcast live programs from remote locations.
rationale: Operating broadcasting equipment at remote locations requires hands-on physical manipulation and operation of tangible machinery and technical devices.

task: Inspect sporting equipment or examine participants to ensure compliance with event and safety regulations.
rationale: Inspecting equipment and examining participants requires physical inspection and hands-on interaction with objects and people at the work site.

task: Instruct patient in activities and techniques, such as sports, dance, music, art, or relaxation techniques, designed to meet their specific physical or psychological needs.
rationale: Instructing patients in physical activities like sports, dance, and art requires hands-on demonstration and direct physical engagement with the patient to teach proper techniques and movements.

task: Record commercials for later broadcast.
rationale: Recording commercials requires operating broadcast equipment and cameras, which involves physical tool operation and hands-on technical execution.

task: Set up and operate devices, such as mechanical ventilators, therapeutic gas administration apparatus, environmental control systems, or aerosol generators, following specified parameters of treatment.
rationale: Setting up and operating physical medical devices requires hands-on manipulation of equipment and machinery to deliver patient care.

task: Provide emergency care, such as artificial respiration, external cardiac massage, or assistance with cardiopulmonary resuscitation.
rationale: Providing emergency care involves hands-on physical intervention—performing CPR, chest compressions, artificial respiration—requiring direct bodily contact and manipulation.

task: Design layouts of audio and video equipment and perform upgrades and maintenance.
rationale: Upgrades and maintenance involve physical manipulation of equipment; layout design includes hands-on setup of physical systems.

task: Perform minor repairs and routine cleaning of audio and video equipment.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of equipment, hands-on repair work, and cleaning—tangible objects handled directly.

task: Construct and position properties, sets, lighting equipment, and other equipment.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and positioning of tangible objects and equipment at a work site.

task: Obtain, set up, and load videotapes for scheduled productions or broadcasts.
rationale: Task requires physical handling of videotapes, setting up equipment, and loading media—tangible object manipulation and equipment operation.

task: Monitor patient's physiological responses to therapy, such as vital signs, arterial blood gases, or blood chemistry changes, and consult with physician if adverse reactions occur.
rationale: Monitoring physiological responses requires physical assessment (vital signs, blood draws, blood gas testing) and hands-on patient interaction.

task: Locate and secure settings, properties, effects, and other production necessities.
rationale: Locating and securing physical settings, properties, effects, and production necessities requires hands-on physical manipulation and presence at work sites.

task: Monitor strength, clarity, and reliability of incoming and outgoing signals, and adjust equipment as necessary to maintain quality broadcasts.
rationale: Requires physical operation of broadcast equipment and hands-on adjustments to maintain signal quality.

task: Read prescription, measure arterial blood gases, and review patient information to assess patient condition.
rationale: Measuring ABGs requires physical blood draw and equipment operation; reading prescriptions and reviewing records are integrated with hands-on clinical assessment.

task: Report equipment problems, ensure that repairs are made, and make emergency repairs to equipment when necessary and possible.
rationale: Reporting problems requires analysis and communication (text-based), but making emergency repairs involves physical operation and manipulation of equipment (manual execution).

task: Record sound onto tape or film for radio or television, checking its quality and making adjustments where necessary.
rationale: Operates recording equipment and physically adjusts settings to capture and monitor sound quality for broadcasts.

task: Align antennae with receiving dishes to obtain the clearest signal for transmission of broadcasts from field locations.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and positioning of tangible equipment (antennae and receiving dishes) at field locations.

task: Organize recording sessions and prepare areas, such as radio booths and television stations, for recording.
rationale: Task involves physical setup and preparation of recording spaces—arranging equipment, organizing areas, ensuring physical readiness of booths and stations.

task: Instruct trainees in use of television production equipment, filming of events, and copying and editing graphics or sound onto videotape.
rationale: Teaching includes demonstrating physical equipment operation and filming techniques, requiring hands-on interaction with production gear.

task: Set up and operate portable field transmission equipment outside the studio.
rationale: Requires physical setup and operation of equipment in field; involves handling, assembling, and operating machinery on-site.

task: Determine the number, type, and approximate location of microphones needed for best sound recording or transmission quality, and position them appropriately.
rationale: Positioning microphones appropriately requires physical placement and manipulation of equipment at specific locations.

task: Operate television or motion picture cameras to record scenes for television broadcasts, advertising, or motion pictures.
rationale: Operating cameras requires physical manipulation of equipment and positioning at work sites to capture scenes. The primary execution involves hands-on equipment operation.

task: Compose and frame each shot, applying the technical aspects of light, lenses, film, filters, and camera settings to achieve the effects sought by directors.
rationale: Operating camera equipment, adjusting physical settings (lenses, filters), and manipulating light/physical filmmaking tools requires hands-on physical execution.

task: Operate zoom lenses, changing images according to specifications and rehearsal instructions.
rationale: Operating zoom lenses involves physical manipulation of camera equipment and tools to adjust imaging based on specifications and instructions.

task: Use cameras in any of several different camera mounts, such as stationary, track-mounted, or crane-mounted.
rationale: Physically operating camera equipment mounted on various apparatus requires hands-on manipulation and positioning of cameras and their mounts.

task: Adjust positions and controls of cameras, printers, and related equipment to change focus, exposure, and lighting.
rationale: Physical adjustment of equipment positions and controls requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects and tools.

task: Set up cameras, optical printers, and related equipment to produce photographs and special effects.
rationale: Setting up cameras and equipment involves physical manipulation and operation of tangible tools and machinery.

task: Inspect, clean, test, and maintain respiratory therapy equipment to ensure equipment is functioning safely and efficiently, ordering repairs when necessary.
rationale: Task requires hands-on physical inspection, cleaning, and testing of equipment to verify safe operation and functionality.

task: Edit films and videotapes to insert music, dialogue, and sound effects, to arrange films into sequences, and to correct errors, using editing equipment.
rationale: Requires operating physical/digital editing equipment and manipulating audio-visual materials to create final products.

task: Examine patients to obtain information about their physical condition.
rationale: Examining patients requires physical interaction—palpation, vital sign measurement, and hands-on assessment.

task: Perform therapeutic procedures, such as injections, immunizations, suturing and wound care, and infection management.
rationale: Therapeutic procedures require direct physical manipulation and hands-on execution: injections, suturing, and wound care are tactile, clinical interventions.

task: Provide physicians with assistance during surgery or complicated medical procedures.
rationale: Assisting in surgery requires hands-on physical actions: passing instruments, preparing surgical field, monitoring patient, positioning limbs.

task: Visit and observe patients on hospital rounds or house calls, updating charts, ordering therapy, and reporting back to physician.
rationale: Visiting and observing patients requires physical presence at work site and hands-on patient assessment, though documentation and reporting are included.

task: Administer prescribed doses of radiation to specific body parts, using radiation therapy equipment according to established practices and standards.
rationale: Requires physical operation of specialized radiation therapy equipment and precise physical positioning of patient at a work site.

task: Position patients for treatment with accuracy, according to prescription.
rationale: Positioning patients requires physical manipulation and hands-on bodily interaction to move patients into specific treatment positions accurately.

task: Perform pulmonary function and adjust equipment to obtain optimum results in therapy.
rationale: Performing pulmonary function tests and adjusting therapy equipment requires hands-on physical manipulation of instruments and direct patient interaction.

task: Check radiation therapy equipment to ensure proper operation.
rationale: Checking equipment requires physical inspection, testing operations, and hands-on verification of machinery function.

task: Observe and reassure patients during treatment and report unusual reactions to physician or turn equipment off if unexpected adverse reactions occur.
rationale: Requires physical presence to observe patients, bodily interaction for reassurance/caregiving, and manual operation of equipment during treatment.

task: Check for side effects, such as skin irritation, nausea, or hair loss to assess patients' reaction to treatment.
rationale: Physical examination and observation of patient's body to assess side effects requires hands-on inspection and direct physical presence.

task: Educate, prepare, and reassure patients and their families by answering questions, providing physical assistance, and reinforcing physicians' advice regarding treatment reactions or post-treatment care.
rationale: Includes physical assistance to patients, which is hands-on caregiving requiring bodily presence and touch.

task: Prepare or construct equipment, such as immobilization, treatment, or protection devices.
rationale: Preparing or constructing equipment requires physical manipulation, assembly, and hands-on work with tangible objects and materials.

task: Photograph treated area of patient and process film.
rationale: Photography requires operating camera equipment and physically positioning it at the treatment site to capture images of the patient's condition.

task: Perform bronchopulmonary drainage and assist or instruct patients in performance of breathing exercises.
rationale: Bronchopulmonary drainage involves hands-on physical manipulation and assessment of patient's lungs. Breathing exercises require physical demonstration and physical assessment.

task: Provide assistance to other healthcare personnel during dosimetry procedures and tumor localization.
rationale: Assisting with dosimetry and tumor localization involves hands-on support during medical procedures, requiring physical presence and interaction with equipment and patients.

task: Store, sterilize, or prepare the special applicators containing the radioactive substance implanted by the physician.
rationale: Physical manipulation of radioactive applicators requires hands-on handling, sterilization preparation, and storage of tangible medical devices.

task: Assist in the preparation of sealed radioactive materials, such as cobalt, radium, cesium, or isotopes, for use in radiation treatments.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation and preparation of radioactive materials for treatment, requiring hands-on handling and assembly of sealed sources.

task: Examine animals to detect and determine the nature of diseases or injuries.
rationale: Examining animals requires physical inspection—handling, palpating, observing—to detect disease/injury signs. Hands-on diagnostic work.

task: Treat sick or injured animals by prescribing medication, setting bones, dressing wounds, or performing surgery.
rationale: Involves physical manipulation: prescribing medication, setting bones, dressing wounds, performing surgery on animals.

task: Inoculate animals against various diseases, such as rabies or distemper.
rationale: Inoculating animals requires physical handling, restraining the animal, and administering injections—direct physical manipulation and hands-on execution.

task: Collect body tissue, feces, blood, urine, or other body fluids for examination and analysis.
rationale: Collecting body specimens requires physical handling, touching patients, and manipulating tangible biological samples—all manual, hands-on work.

task: Demonstrate respiratory care procedures to trainees or other healthcare personnel.
rationale: Demonstrating respiratory procedures requires hands-on physical execution with equipment/patients, not just verbal instruction.

task: Operate diagnostic equipment, such as radiographic or ultrasound equipment, and interpret the resulting images.
rationale: Operating diagnostic equipment requires physical interaction with machinery and instruments. Interpreting images involves cognitive analysis but operation of radiographic/ultrasound equipment is primarily manual.

task: Provide care to a wide range of animals or specialize in a particular species, such as horses or exotic birds.
rationale: Providing animal care requires hands-on physical interaction: handling, examining, feeding, grooming, treating animals directly.

task: Euthanize animals.
rationale: Euthanizing animals requires direct physical execution—administering injections, performing procedures, or using equipment to end life.

task: Conduct postmortem studies and analyses to determine the causes of animals' deaths.
rationale: Conducting postmortem studies requires physical examination, dissection, and manipulation of animal remains to determine cause of death.

task: Inspect and test horses, sheep, poultry, or other animals to detect the presence of communicable diseases.
rationale: Physical inspection of animals requires hands-on examination, observation, and potentially handling animals to detect signs of disease.

task: Determine the effects of drug therapies, antibiotics, or new surgical techniques by testing them on animals.
rationale: Testing drugs/therapies on animals requires hands-on physical manipulation, animal handling, administering treatments, and direct observation of physical responses.

task: Monitor patients' blood pressure and heart rate using electrocardiogram (EKG) equipment during diagnostic or therapeutic procedures to notify the physician if something appears wrong.
rationale: Requires operation of physical EKG equipment and hands-on patient monitoring during procedures.

task: Monitor patients' comfort and safety during tests, alerting physicians to abnormalities or changes in patient responses.
rationale: Requires physical presence with patients, observation and assessment of vital signs, physical responses, and hands-on monitoring during medical procedures.

task: Use a variety of testing techniques to assist doctors in cardiac or pulmonary research or to diagnose disorders.
rationale: Conducting cardiac/pulmonary tests requires hands-on physical operation of medical equipment (EKG, ultrasound, spirometry machines) and direct patient interaction.

task: Conduct electrocardiogram (EKG), phonocardiogram, echocardiogram, stress testing, or other cardiovascular tests to record patients' cardiac activity, using specialized electronic test equipment, recording devices, or laboratory instruments.
rationale: Performing cardiovascular tests requires operating specialized medical equipment, attaching devices to patients, and hands-on technical execution to record cardiac activity.

task: Prepare and position patients for testing.
rationale: Preparing and positioning patients requires physical manipulation and bodily interaction with patients to move them into proper testing positions.

task: Attach electrodes to the patients' chests, arms, and legs, connect electrodes to leads from the electrocardiogram (EKG) machine, and operate the EKG machine to obtain a reading.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation: attaching electrodes to patient's body, connecting electrodes to equipment, and operating machinery to obtain readings.

task: Adjust equipment and controls according to physicians' orders or established protocol.
rationale: Adjusting equipment and controls requires physical manipulation of devices and instruments according to medical protocols and orders.

task: Check, test, and maintain cardiology equipment, making minor repairs when necessary, to ensure proper operation.
rationale: Task requires physical inspection, testing, maintenance, and hands-on repairs of medical equipment to ensure proper operation.

task: Make emergency visits to resolve equipment problems.
rationale: Resolving equipment problems requires hands-on physical troubleshooting, repair, or manipulation of equipment and tools.

task: Operate diagnostic imaging equipment to produce contrast enhanced radiographs of heart and cardiovascular system.
rationale: Operating diagnostic imaging equipment requires physical manipulation and operation of specialized machinery to produce radiographs.

task: Inject contrast medium into patients' blood vessels.
rationale: Injecting contrast medium requires direct physical manipulation of needles/catheters into blood vessels—hands-on medical procedure.

task: Detect and map radiopharmaceuticals in patients' bodies, using a camera to produce photographic or computer images.
rationale: Operating nuclear medicine camera to physically acquire diagnostic images from patients. Requires hands-on operation of specialized imaging equipment and physical presence at the worksite.

task: Conduct tests, such as electrocardiograms (EKGs), stress testing, or lung capacity tests, to evaluate patients' cardiopulmonary functions.
rationale: Conducting medical tests (EKGs, stress tests, lung capacity tests) requires hands-on physical operation of equipment and direct patient interaction/examination.

task: Explain test procedures and safety precautions to patients and provide them with assistance during test procedures.
rationale: Requires physical presence to demonstrate procedures, guide patients, and provide hands-on assistance during medical testing.

task: Dispose of radioactive materials and store radiopharmaceuticals, following radiation safety procedures.
rationale: Requires physical handling and manipulation of radioactive materials and radiopharmaceuticals, plus hands-on adherence to safety procedures.

task: Prepare stock radiopharmaceuticals, adhering to safety standards that minimize radiation exposure to workers and patients.
rationale: Physically preparing radiopharmaceuticals requires handling radioactive materials, operating specialized equipment, and direct manipulation of tangible substances.

task: Maintain and calibrate radioisotope and laboratory equipment.
rationale: Maintaining and calibrating equipment requires hands-on physical manipulation, operation of tools, and direct inspection of instruments.

task: Measure glandular activity, blood volume, red cell survival, or radioactivity of patient, using scanners, Geiger counters, scintillometers, or other laboratory equipment.
rationale: Requires physical operation of laboratory equipment (scanners, Geiger counters, scintillometers) and hands-on measurement of patient samples.

task: Clean calcareous deposits, accretions, and stains from teeth and beneath margins of gums, using dental instruments.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of dental instruments and hands-on manipulation of teeth and gums to remove deposits and stains.

task: Add radioactive substances to biological specimens, such as blood, urine, or feces, to determine therapeutic drug or hormone levels.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of biological specimens, radioactive substances, and laboratory equipment in hands-on laboratory work.

task: Prepare a major meal, following recipes and determining group food quantities.
rationale: Preparing a meal requires physical manipulation of ingredients, use of cooking tools/equipment, and hands-on food preparation.

task: Feel and visually examine gums for sores and signs of disease.
rationale: Requires physical touch (feeling) and close visual inspection of patient's gums; hands-on clinical examination.

task: Administer anesthesia to animals, under the direction of a veterinarian, and monitor animals' responses to anesthetics so that dosages can be adjusted.
rationale: Administering anesthesia involves physical injection/application; monitoring requires hands-on animal assessment and vital sign observation.

task: Care for and monitor the condition of animals recovering from surgery.
rationale: Caring for animals involves hands-on physical work: monitoring vital signs, wound care, administering medications, and direct bodily interaction with animals.

task: Prepare and administer medications, vaccines, serums, or treatments, as prescribed by veterinarians.
rationale: Preparing and administering medications/vaccines involves physical manipulation of substances, using syringes or applicators, and hands-on contact with animals.

task: Perform laboratory tests on blood, urine, or feces, such as urinalyses or blood counts, to assist in the diagnosis and treatment of animal health problems.
rationale: Performing laboratory tests requires physical manipulation of samples, operation of lab equipment, and hands-on technical execution of procedures.

task: Administer emergency first aid, such as performing emergency resuscitation or other life saving procedures.
rationale: Performing CPR, rescue breathing, and emergency resuscitation requires direct physical manipulation and hands-on bodily contact with the patient.

task: Collect, prepare, and label samples for laboratory testing, culture, or microscopic examination.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of samples, preparation procedures, and hands-on labeling work at the laboratory bench.

task: Clean and sterilize instruments, equipment, or materials.
rationale: Physical manipulation of instruments and equipment using cleaning/sterilization methods and tools.

task: Provide veterinarians with the correct equipment or instruments, as needed.
rationale: Providing equipment involves physical handling and distribution of tangible instruments to support veterinary work.

task: Fill prescriptions, measuring medications and labeling containers.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of medications, measuring, and labeling containers—hands-on pharmaceutical work.

task: Prepare animals for surgery, performing such tasks as shaving surgical areas.
rationale: Shaving and preparing animals involves physical manipulation and hands-on care of the animal's body to ready it for surgery.

task: Take animals into treatment areas and assist with physical examinations by performing such duties as obtaining temperature, pulse, or respiration data.
rationale: Physical handling of animals, obtaining vital signs (temperature, pulse, respiration) requires hands-on physical interaction and bodily movement.

task: Observe the behavior and condition of animals and monitor their clinical symptoms.
rationale: Observing animals' behavior and condition requires physical presence at the work site and hands-on inspection of living subjects.

task: Take and develop diagnostic radiographs, using x-ray equipment.
rationale: Requires operating x-ray equipment and physical positioning of patients/objects for imaging capture.

task: Feel lymph nodes under patient's chin to detect swelling or tenderness that could indicate presence of oral cancer.
rationale: Physical examination requiring hands-on palpation to detect swelling and tenderness in lymph nodes.

task: Give enemas and perform catheterizations, ear flushes, intravenous feedings, or gavages.
rationale: These are hands-on clinical procedures involving direct physical manipulation and insertion of medical devices into patients' bodies.

task: Prepare treatment rooms for surgery.
rationale: Preparing treatment rooms requires physical setup: arranging equipment, sanitizing surfaces, organizing instruments, and arranging furniture.

task: Maintain instruments, equipment, or machinery to ensure proper working condition.
rationale: Maintaining instruments requires physical manipulation, inspection, and repair of tangible equipment and machinery.

task: Perform dental work, such as cleaning, polishing, or extracting teeth.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and operation of dental tools and instruments on patient teeth and mouth.

task: Clean kennels, animal holding areas, surgery suites, examination rooms, or animal loading or unloading facilities to control the spread of disease.
rationale: Cleaning facilities requires physical manipulation of tools, materials, and spaces to achieve sanitization and disease control.

task: Provide assistance with animal euthanasia and the disposal of remains.
rationale: Euthanasia and remains disposal involve physical handling of animals and use of equipment/tools requiring hands-on execution.

task: Dress and suture wounds and apply splints or other protective devices.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation: cleaning wounds, suturing (threading, tying), applying dressings and splints—hands-on medical care.

task: Bathe animals, clip nails or claws, and brush or cut animals' hair.
rationale: Direct physical handling and manipulation of animals; bathing, grooming, and nail clipping require hands-on contact and use of tools.

task: Apply fluorides or other cavity preventing agents to arrest dental decay.
rationale: Direct application of fluoride agents to teeth requires hands-on physical manipulation and treatment within the patient's mouth.

task: Conduct specialized procedures, such as animal branding or tattooing or hoof trimming.
rationale: Requires direct physical manipulation of animals and specialized tools (branding iron, tattoo equipment, hoof trimming tools) to perform hands-on procedures.

task: Measure clients' bridge and eye size, temple length, vertex distance, pupillary distance, and optical centers of eyes, using measuring devices.
rationale: Measuring clients requires physical interaction with measuring devices and positioning clients to take precise physical measurements of facial features and optical parameters.

task: Verify that finished lenses are ground to specifications.
rationale: Verifying finished lenses requires physical inspection—measuring, touching, and examining tangible lens objects against specifications.

task: Assist clients in selecting frames according to style and color, and ensure that frames are coordinated with facial and eye measurements and optical prescriptions.
rationale: Requires physical measurement of client's face/eyes, handling frames, and hands-on fitting to ensure proper coordination.

task: Heat, shape, or bend plastic or metal frames to adjust eyeglasses to fit clients, using pliers and hands.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of eyeglass frames using pliers and hands to heat, shape, and bend materials—core manual work.

task: Examine gums, using probes, to locate periodontal recessed gums and signs of gum disease.
rationale: Physical examination using probes to inspect gums requires hands-on tactile assessment and physical presence at patient.

task: Determine clients' current lens prescriptions, when necessary, using lensometers or lens analyzers and clients' eyeglasses.
rationale: Requires physical operation of lensometers/lens analyzers and handling clients' eyeglasses to measure prescriptions.

task: Show customers how to insert, remove, and care for their contact lenses.
rationale: Demonstrating physical insertion, removal, and handling of contact lenses requires hands-on demonstration and bodily manipulation.

task: Repair damaged frames.
rationale: Repairing frames requires hands-on physical manipulation of tangible objects, using tools, and assembling/fixing components.

task: Arrange and maintain displays of optical merchandise.
rationale: Arranging and maintaining displays requires physical manipulation of merchandise, organizing shelves, and hands-on work with tangible objects.

task: Fabricate lenses to meet prescription specifications.
rationale: Fabricating lenses requires physical manipulation of optical materials, using precision tools and machinery to shape and finish lenses to exact specifications.

task: Grind lens edges, or apply coatings to lenses.
rationale: Both grinding and coating lenses require hands-on physical manipulation of optical equipment and materials using specialized tools and machinery.

task: Assemble eyeglasses by cutting and edging lenses, and fitting the lenses into frames.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of lenses and frames, using tools to cut and edge lenses, and hands-on assembly work.

task: Expose and develop x-ray film.
rationale: Exposing and developing x-ray film requires physical manipulation of film, chemicals, and darkroom equipment to process tangible materials.

task: Conduct an initial assessment of an athlete's injury or illness to provide emergency or continued care and to determine whether they should be referred to physicians for definitive diagnosis and treatment.
rationale: Conducting an injury assessment requires physical examination of the athlete—palpation, range-of-motion testing, visual inspection—necessitating hands-on physical presence and interaction.

task: Care for athletic injuries, using physical therapy equipment, techniques, or medication.
rationale: Involves physical manipulation of therapeutic equipment and hands-on techniques to treat injuries.

task: Apply protective or injury preventive devices, such as tape, bandages, or braces, to body parts, such as ankles, fingers, or wrists.
rationale: Physical manipulation and application of tangible objects (tape, bandages, braces) directly to body parts requires hands-on physical execution.

task: Inspect playing fields to locate any items that could injure players.
rationale: Requires physical presence at work site and hands-on inspection of field conditions to identify hazards.

task: Massage body parts to relieve soreness, strains, or bruises.
rationale: Massage involves hands-on physical manipulation of body parts to achieve therapeutic outcomes.

task: Accompany injured athletes to hospitals.
rationale: Requires physical presence accompanying injured athletes and may involve physical assistance or support during transport.

task: Perform team support duties, such as running errands, maintaining equipment, or stocking supplies.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of equipment, supplies, and objects; hands-on stocking and maintenance work.

task: Lead stretching exercises for team members prior to games or practices.
rationale: Leading stretching requires demonstrating physical movements and performing hands-on guidance with team members' bodies.

task: Remove excess cement from coronal surfaces of teeth.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of dental instruments to remove excess cement from tooth surfaces; hands-on clinical procedure.

task: Provide patients with help moving in and out of beds, baths, wheelchairs, or automobiles and with dressing and grooming.
rationale: Involves hands-on physical assistance with patient movement, mobility aids, and personal care activities requiring direct bodily contact and physical presence.

task: Plan, purchase, prepare, or serve meals to patients or other family members, according to prescribed diets.
rationale: Task requires physical preparation and serving of meals, including food handling, cooking, and delivery to patients/family members.

task: Direct patients in simple prescribed exercises or in the use of braces or artificial limbs.
rationale: Directing patients in physical exercises and fitting/demonstrating braces or artificial limbs requires hands-on physical demonstration and guidance with tangible equipment.

task: Check patients' pulse, temperature, and respiration.
rationale: Physical examination requiring hands-on assessment of vital signs through touching and observing the patient's body.

task: Change dressings.
rationale: Changing dressings requires hands-on physical manipulation of medical dressings and direct contact with the patient's wound.

task: Perform a variety of duties as requested by client, such as obtaining household supplies or running errands.
rationale: Obtaining household supplies and running errands requires physical movement, shopping, and handling/carrying goods.

task: Make impressions for study casts.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of impression materials and dental/laboratory objects to create study casts.

task: Accompany clients to doctors' offices or on other trips outside the home, providing transportation, assistance, and companionship.
rationale: Task involves physical transportation of clients (driving), physical assistance with mobility, and hands-on companionship at various locations requiring bodily presence.

task: Care for children with disabilities or who have sick parents or parents with disabilities.
rationale: Caring for children involves hands-on physical assistance, bodily presence, and direct caregiving interaction with vulnerable individuals.

task: Massage patients or apply preparations or treatments, such as liniment, alcohol rubs, or heat-lamp stimulation.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation—massaging patients and applying topical treatments and devices directly to the body.

task: Aid patients in dressing and grooming themselves.
rationale: Direct physical assistance with dressing and grooming requires hands-on interaction with patients and manipulation of clothing and grooming items.

task: Implement, or assist occupational therapists with implementing, treatment plans designed to help clients function independently.
rationale: Implementing treatment plans requires hands-on caregiving, physical assistance, and direct physical interaction with clients to help them regain functional abilities.

task: Design, fabricate, or repair assistive devices or make adaptive changes to equipment or environments.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation, fabrication, assembly, and repair of tangible devices and equipment modifications using tools and materials.

task: Assemble, clean, or maintain equipment or materials for patient use.
rationale: Assembling, cleaning, and maintaining equipment requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and hands-on work with materials.

task: Transport patients to and from the occupational therapy work area.
rationale: Requires physical movement and bodily presence to escort/transport patients, involves physical interaction with individuals and navigation of work spaces.

task: Administer local anesthetic agents.
rationale: Administering anesthetics requires hands-on physical execution: injecting medication into a patient's body using medical tools.

task: Demonstrate therapy techniques, such as manual or creative arts or games.
rationale: Demonstrating therapy techniques requires hands-on physical execution and bodily engagement in the work itself.

task: Clean and organize work area and disinfect equipment after treatment.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of objects, cleaning supplies, and equipment; hands-on disinfection and organization of tangible items.

task: Instruct, motivate, safeguard, or assist patients practicing exercises or functional activities, under direction of medical staff.
rationale: Instructing and assisting patients with exercises requires hands-on physical presence, guidance, and interaction to support their functional movements and activities.

task: Secure patients into or onto therapy equipment.
rationale: Physical manipulation and positioning of patients onto equipment requires hands-on physical interaction and bodily movement.

task: Transport patients to and from treatment areas, using wheelchairs or providing standing support.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and bodily movement to operate wheelchairs and provide hands-on support for patient mobility.

task: Conduct dental health clinics for community groups to augment services of dentist.
rationale: Conducting dental health clinics involves hands-on physical examination and care of patients' teeth and oral cavities, requiring direct physical interaction.

task: Maintain equipment or furniture to keep it in good working condition, including performing the assembly or disassembly of equipment or accessories.
rationale: Physical manipulation of equipment/furniture, assembly, disassembly, and hands-on maintenance work require direct physical engagement with tangible objects.

task: Administer active or passive manual therapeutic exercises, therapeutic massage, or heat, light, sound, water, or electrical modality treatments, such as ultrasound.
rationale: Physical manipulation and hands-on treatment of patients using therapeutic exercises, massage, and medical modalities require direct physical contact and bodily presence.

task: Change linens, such as bed sheets and pillow cases.
rationale: Changing linens requires physical manipulation of bedding materials—handling, removing, and installing sheets and pillowcases.

task: Arrange treatment supplies to keep them in order.
rationale: Arranging treatment supplies requires physical manipulation and organization of tangible medical objects.

task: Assist patients to dress, undress, or put on and remove supportive devices, such as braces, splints, or slings.
rationale: Directly assisting patients with dressing and physically handling/applying supportive medical devices requires hands-on physical contact and manipulation.

task: Measure patient's range-of-joint motion, body parts, or vital signs to determine effects of treatments or for patient evaluations.
rationale: Requires physical measurement and hands-on examination of patient's body, joints, and vital signs using physical assessment techniques.

task: Train patients to use orthopedic braces, prostheses, or supportive devices.
rationale: Training requires physical demonstration and hands-on adjustment of devices on patients' bodies.

task: Fit patients for orthopedic braces, prostheses, or supportive devices, adjusting fit as needed.
rationale: Fitting braces and prostheses requires physical manipulation of devices and direct physical interaction with patients to adjust fit.

task: Participate in patient care tasks, such as assisting with passing food trays, feeding residents, or bathing residents on bed rest.
rationale: Task requires hands-on physical execution: handling food trays, feeding, and bathing residents. Bodily movement and direct physical caregiving are primary.

task: Administer traction to relieve neck or back pain, using intermittent or static traction equipment.
rationale: Administering traction requires physical operation of equipment and hands-on patient interaction to apply therapeutic treatment.

task: Organize and assemble routine or specialty surgical instrument trays or other sterilized supplies, filling special requests as needed.
rationale: Organizing and assembling surgical instrument trays requires physical manipulation of sterilized surgical instruments and supplies, sorting and arranging them into trays.

task: Clean instruments to prepare them for sterilization.
rationale: Physical handling and manipulation of instruments, using water/cleaning solutions and tools to remove debris and contaminants.

task: Operate and maintain steam autoclaves, keeping records of loads completed, items in loads, and maintenance procedures performed.
rationale: Requires operating physical equipment (steam autoclave), performing hands-on maintenance, and keeping records of completed tasks.

task: Disinfect and sterilize equipment, such as respirators, hospital beds, or oxygen or dialysis equipment, using sterilizers, aerators, or washers.
rationale: Physically operating sterilizers, aerators, and washers to handle and process tangible medical equipment requires hands-on manipulation and equipment operation.

task: Start equipment and observe gauges and equipment operation to detect malfunctions and to ensure equipment is operating to prescribed standards.
rationale: Starting equipment and observing gauges requires physical interaction with machinery and being present at the work site to monitor physical operations and detect malfunctions.

task: Examine equipment to detect leaks, worn or loose parts, or other indications of disrepair.
rationale: Physical inspection requiring hands-on examination of equipment, touching/seeing objects to detect mechanical issues.

task: Check sterile supplies to ensure that they are not outdated.
rationale: Physical inspection of tangible supplies requires being present and examining items for expiration dates.

task: Purge wastes from equipment by connecting equipment to water sources and flushing water through systems.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of equipment, connecting water sources, and operating flushing systems through hands-on work.

task: Deliver equipment to specified hospital locations or to patients' residences.
rationale: Delivering equipment requires physical transportation, handling, and placement of tangible equipment at specific locations.

task: Assist hospital staff with patient care duties, such as providing transportation or setting up traction.
rationale: Transporting patients and setting up medical traction require hands-on physical manipulation, bodily movement, and direct patient care interaction.

task: Monitor animals recovering from surgery and notify veterinarians of any unusual changes or symptoms.
rationale: Requires physical presence to observe animals, monitor vital signs, and detect symptoms through direct observation and hands-on inspection.

task: Administer anesthetics during surgery and monitor the effects on animals.
rationale: Administering anesthetics requires physical injection/application and hands-on monitoring of animal vital signs during surgery.

task: Clean, maintain, and sterilize instruments or equipment.
rationale: Cleaning, maintaining, and sterilizing instruments requires physical handling and manipulation of tangible objects and equipment.

task: Administer medication, immunizations, or blood plasma to animals as prescribed by veterinarians.
