task: Provide emergency first aid to sick or injured animals.
rationale: Requires hands-on physical interaction with animals: examining injuries, stabilizing wounds, administering treatment.

task: Clean and maintain kennels, animal holding areas, examination or operating rooms, or animal loading or unloading facilities to control the spread of disease.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical manipulation of cleaning materials and physical inspection/maintenance of facility spaces to control disease spread.

task: Hold or restrain animals during veterinary procedures.
rationale: Holding or restraining animals requires direct physical contact and bodily control of tangible objects.

task: Perform routine laboratory tests or diagnostic tests, such as taking or developing x-rays.
rationale: Performing lab tests and taking/developing x-rays requires operating diagnostic equipment and physical manipulation of specimens and materials.

task: Fill medication prescriptions.
rationale: Filling prescriptions involves physically handling medications, counting pills, labeling bottles, and packaging—tangible manual work.

task: Collect laboratory specimens, such as blood, urine, or feces, for testing.
rationale: Collecting specimens requires direct physical handling and interaction with patient body samples and materials.

task: Examine animals to detect behavioral changes or clinical symptoms that could indicate illness or injury.
rationale: Physical examination of animals requires direct hands-on observation and interaction to detect behavioral changes and clinical symptoms.

task: Assist veterinarians in examining animals to determine the nature of illnesses or injuries.
rationale: Physical examination of animals requires hands-on inspection, palpation, and direct bodily interaction to assess injuries and illnesses.

task: Prepare surgical equipment and pass instruments or materials to veterinarians during surgical procedures.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of surgical instruments and materials, and hands-on coordination during procedures.

task: Perform enemas, catheterizations, ear flushes, intravenous feedings, or gavages.
rationale: All tasks require hands-on physical manipulation of medical equipment and direct bodily contact with patients.

task: Prepare feed for animals according to specific instructions, such as diet lists or schedules.
rationale: Preparing feed requires physical manipulation of food/materials and handling of tangible objects for animals.

task: Exercise animals or provide them with companionship.
rationale: Physical interaction with animals requires bodily movement and hands-on caregiving or activity engagement.

task: Perform hygiene-related duties, such as clipping animals' claws or cleaning and polishing teeth.
rationale: Clipping claws and cleaning teeth require hands-on physical manipulation of animals and dental tools.

task: Prepare examination or treatment rooms by stocking them with appropriate supplies.
rationale: Stocking rooms requires physical handling and arrangement of tangible medical supplies and equipment in a workspace.

task: Provide assistance with euthanasia of animals or disposal of corpses.
rationale: Euthanasia and corpse disposal involve physical manipulation of animal bodies and operation of equipment or tools.

task: Dust, spray, or bathe animals to control insect pests.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of animals and application of pest control substances through dusting, spraying, or bathing—direct hands-on work.

task: Observe and care for patients throughout examinations to ensure their safety and comfort.
rationale: Requires direct physical presence and bodily interaction with patients (positioning, monitoring, hands-on comfort measures).

task: Groom, trim, or clip animals' coats.
rationale: Grooming requires hands-on physical manipulation of animals and use of grooming tools like clippers and scissors.

task: Operate ultrasound equipment to produce and record images of the motion, shape, and composition of blood, organs, tissues, or bodily masses, such as fluid accumulations.
rationale: Operating ultrasound equipment requires hands-on physical manipulation of transducers and machinery to perform examinations and produce medical images.

task: Select appropriate equipment settings and adjust patient positions to obtain the best sites and angles.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of equipment and patient positioning to achieve proper angles and sites for medical imaging or examination.

task: Prepare patient for exam by explaining procedure, transferring patient to ultrasound table, scrubbing skin and applying gel, and positioning patient properly.
rationale: Task requires physical patient transfer, skin scrubbing, gel application, and positioning—hands-on clinical caregiving and manipulation.

task: Record and store suitable images, using camera unit connected to the ultrasound equipment.
rationale: Operating ultrasound camera equipment and physically handling/positioning the device to capture and record images requires physical manipulation and operation of specialized equipment.

task: Coordinate work with physicians or other healthcare team members, including providing assistance during invasive procedures.
rationale: Assisting during invasive procedures requires hands-on physical presence, manipulation of instruments/materials, and direct participation in patient care execution.

task: Exchange paper currency for playing chips or coin money.
rationale: Physical manipulation of currency and chips/coins; handling and exchanging tangible objects at point of transaction.

task: Deal cards to house hands, and compare these with players' hands to determine winners, as in black jack.
rationale: Dealing cards requires physical manipulation of tangible playing cards and objects at a gaming table.

task: Conduct gambling games, such as dice, roulette, cards, or keno, following all applicable rules and regulations.
rationale: Conducting gambling games requires physical manipulation of tangible objects (dice, cards, roulette wheels) and hands-on operation of game equipment.

task: Stand behind a gaming table and deal the appropriate number of cards to each player.
rationale: Dealing cards requires physical manipulation of tangible objects (cards) and requires being physically present at the gaming table.

task: Inspect cards and equipment to be used in games to ensure that they are in good condition.
rationale: Requires physical inspection by touching and examining tangible objects (cards, equipment) in person to assess condition.

task: Start and control games and gaming equipment, and announce winning numbers or colors.
rationale: Starting/controlling gaming equipment requires physical operation of machines and tools; announcing results involves verbal communication but is secondary to equipment operation.

task: Open and close cash floats and game tables.
rationale: Opening and closing cash floats requires physical handling of money, coins, and cash boxes. Opening and closing game tables involves physical manipulation of equipment and materials.

task: Clean and polish footwear, using brushes, sponges, cleaning fluid, polishes, waxes, liquid or sole dressing, and daubers.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of tools (brushes, sponges) and materials (cleaning fluid, polishes, waxes) to clean and polish footwear—direct hands-on work with tangible objects.

task: Clean, check, and maintain sonographic equipment, submitting maintenance requests or performing minor repairs as necessary.
rationale: Requires physical cleaning, inspection, and hands-on minor repairs of tangible medical equipment.

task: Operate washing machines and dryers to clean soiled apparel and towels.
rationale: Operating physical machinery (washing machines and dryers) to clean items requires hands-on manipulation of equipment and tangible objects.

task: Activate emergency action plans and administer first aid, as necessary.
rationale: First aid requires physical manipulation and hands-on care of injured persons. Emergency action activation involves both cognitive and physical components, but administering first aid is the primary manual execution.

task: Store personal possessions for patrons, issue claim checks for articles stored, and return articles on receipt of checks.
rationale: Requires physical handling of tangible items (possessions), storing them, and retrieving them from storage—direct manipulation of objects.

task: Provide towels and sheets to clients in public baths, steam rooms, and restrooms.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of towels and sheets, carrying them, and distributing them to clients—hands-on work.

task: Collect soiled linen or clothing for laundering.
rationale: Collecting soiled linen requires physical manipulation and handling of tangible items for transport to laundering.

task: Operate controls that regulate temperatures or room environments.
rationale: Operating physical controls (thermostats, switches, levers) is direct manipulation of tangible mechanical/electronic devices.

task: Attend to needs of athletic teams in clubhouses.
rationale: Attending to athletic team needs involves physical tasks like cleaning, organizing equipment, providing refreshments, and hands-on facility maintenance.

task: Provide assistance to patrons by performing duties such as opening doors or carrying bags.
rationale: Opening doors and carrying bags require physical manipulation and bodily movement to assist patrons directly.

task: Stencil identifying information on equipment.
rationale: Physical operation of stenciling tools to mark equipment; requires hands-on manipulation of stencils and marking materials on tangible objects.

task: Issue gym clothes, uniforms, towels, athletic equipment, and special athletic apparel.
rationale: Physical distribution of tangible items (clothes, uniforms, towels, equipment) requires handling and delivering goods.

task: Maintain a lost-and-found collection.
rationale: Maintaining a lost-and-found requires physically handling, organizing, and storing tangible objects.

task: Set up various apparatus or athletic equipment.
rationale: Setting up apparatus/athletic equipment requires physical manipulation and assembly of tangible objects.

task: Apply cosmetics to impart lifelike appearance to the deceased.
rationale: Applying cosmetics to deceased requires hands-on physical manipulation of products and direct contact with the body.

task: Incise stomach and abdominal walls and probe internal organs, using trocar, to withdraw blood and waste matter from organs.
rationale: Incising, probing, and manipulating internal organs using surgical instruments (trocar) is hands-on physical execution requiring direct manipulation of body tissues.

task: Perform medical procedures, such as administering oxygen, inserting and removing airways, taking vital signs, or giving emergency treatment, such as first aid or cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR).
rationale: Direct physical intervention on patients: administering oxygen, inserting airways, taking vital signs, CPR—all require hands-on contact and bodily movement.

task: Close incisions, using needles and sutures.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of instruments (needles, sutures) to close surgical incisions on a patient's body.

task: Reshape or reconstruct disfigured or maimed bodies when necessary, using dermasurgery techniques and materials such as clay, cotton, plaster of Paris, and wax.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of materials (clay, wax, plaster) and hands-on techniques to reconstruct bodies. Primary execution is tangible, tactile work.

task: Make incisions in arms or thighs and drain blood from circulatory system and replace it with embalming fluid, using pump.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation: making incisions, draining blood, operating pump equipment, and working with tangible human remains.

task: Dress bodies and place them in caskets.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and handling of deceased bodies, dressing them, and positioning them in caskets—direct hands-on manual work.

task: Join lips, using needles and thread or wire.
rationale: Joining lips requires physical manipulation of needles and thread or wire, and hands-on suturing work—a clearly manual/physical task.

task: Attach trocar to pump-tube, start pump, and repeat probing to force embalming fluid into organs.
rationale: Physical manipulation of equipment (trocar, pump, tube) and bodily/instrument movement during embalming procedure on a cadaver.

task: Perform special procedures necessary for remains that are to be transported to other states or overseas, or where death was caused by infectious disease.
rationale: Performing special procedures on remains requires physical manipulation, embalming, handling of biohazardous materials, and hands-on preparation work.

task: Load and unload film cassettes used to record images from procedures.
rationale: Loading and unloading film cassettes requires direct physical manipulation of tangible objects and equipment.

task: Insert convex celluloid or cotton between eyeballs and eyelids to prevent slipping and sinking of eyelids.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of materials and precise physical contact with delicate eye anatomy during prosthetic fitting.

task: Wash and dry bodies, using germicidal soap and towels or hot air dryers.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of bodies, application of soap, and use of towels or dryers—direct hands-on physical contact and bodily movement.

task: Pack body orifices with cotton saturated with embalming fluid to prevent escape of gases or waste matter.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of cotton and direct contact with a body—hands-on embalming procedure.

task: Assist with placing caskets in hearses and organize cemetery processions.
rationale: Placing caskets in hearses requires physical manipulation of heavy objects. Organizing processions involves physical presence and coordination at work site.

task: Serve as pallbearers, attend visiting rooms, and provide other assistance to the bereaved.
rationale: Serving as pallbearers requires physical lifting and carrying of a casket. Attending and assisting the bereaved involves physical presence and hands-on support.

task: Arrange funeral home equipment and perform general maintenance.
rationale: Both arranging equipment and performing maintenance require physical manipulation of tangible objects and hands-on work at a facility.

task: Assist coroners at death scenes or at autopsies, file police reports, and testify at inquests or in court, if employed by a coroner.
rationale: Assisting coroners at death scenes involves physical presence, examination of bodies, and hands-on evidence handling. Autopsies require physical participation.

task: Press diaphragm to evacuate air from lungs.
rationale: Pressing the diaphragm requires physical bodily movement to manipulate respiratory muscles and control breathing.

task: Perform a variety of tasks during funerals to assist funeral directors and to ensure that services run smoothly and as planned.
rationale: Assisting during funerals involves physical presence, arrangement of seating, handling flowers/arrangements, and coordinating attendees—hands-on support work.

task: Offer assistance to mourners as they enter or exit limousines.
rationale: Assisting mourners entering/exiting limousines requires physical presence, bodily movement, and hands-on support or guidance at the vehicle.

task: Close caskets at appropriate point in services.
rationale: Physically closing caskets requires hands-on manipulation of a tangible object at the work site.

task: Direct or escort mourners to parlors or chapels in which wakes or funerals are being held.
rationale: Directing or escorting mourners requires physical presence and bodily movement at the work site to guide people through the funeral facility.

task: Place caskets in parlors or chapels prior to wakes or funerals.
rationale: Physically moving and positioning caskets requires manipulation of tangible objects and bodily movement in the work environment.

task: Clean and drive funeral vehicles, such as cars or hearses, in funeral processions.
rationale: Cleaning vehicles involves physical manipulation and maintenance work; driving requires operation of a motor vehicle.

task: Carry flowers to hearses or limousines for transportation to places of interment.
rationale: Physical handling and carrying of flowers to transport vehicles requires direct manipulation of tangible objects and bodily movement.

task: Clean funeral parlors or chapels.
rationale: Cleaning involves physical manipulation of tools and materials to sanitize spaces; requires hands-on work and bodily presence.

task: Arrange floral offerings or lights around caskets.
rationale: Physically arranging floral offerings and lights around caskets requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects.

task: Issue and store funeral equipment.
rationale: Issuing and storing funeral equipment involves physical manipulation and handling of tangible objects and materials at a work site.

task: Assist with cremations and the processing and packaging of cremated remains.
rationale: Involves physical handling, processing, and packaging of human remains—direct manual work with tangible materials.

task: Act as pallbearers.
rationale: Pallbearers physically carry and handle a casket, requiring manual strength and coordination.

task: Receive, store, or deliver luggage or mail.
rationale: Receiving, storing, and delivering luggage/mail requires physical manipulation and transportation of tangible objects.

task: Pick up and deliver items or run errands for guests.
rationale: Requires physical movement to pick up, carry, and deliver tangible items to different locations.

task: Escort individuals or groups on cruises, sightseeing tours, or through places of interest, such as industrial establishments, public buildings, or art galleries.
rationale: Escorting requires physical presence and bodily movement to guide groups through spaces, interact with environments, and ensure their safety and experience.

task: Provide for physical safety of groups, performing such activities as providing first aid or directing emergency evacuations.
rationale: First aid requires hands-on physical care; evacuation direction requires physical presence and bodily movement at the work site.

task: Teach skills, such as proper climbing methods, and demonstrate and advise on the use of equipment.
rationale: Teaching climbing methods and equipment use requires physical demonstration of techniques and hands-on guidance to students.

task: Assemble and check the required supplies and equipment prior to departure.
rationale: Assembling and checking supplies/equipment requires physical manipulation and hands-on inspection of tangible objects before departure.

task: Drive motor vehicles to transport visitors to establishments and tour site locations.
rationale: Driving motor vehicles is direct operation of machinery, requiring physical control and presence to navigate and transport passengers.

task: Teach proper breathing techniques used during physical exertion.
rationale: Demonstrating breathing techniques requires physical performance and embodied knowledge transfer, not just verbal instruction.

task: Teach and demonstrate use of gymnastic and training equipment, such as trampolines and weights.
rationale: Physical demonstration of equipment use requires hands-on operation and bodily movement to show technique and proper form.

task: Maintain fitness equipment.
rationale: Maintaining fitness equipment requires hands-on physical work: inspecting, adjusting, repairing, and servicing tangible machines and devices.

task: Conduct therapeutic, recreational, or athletic activities.
rationale: Conducting therapeutic, recreational, or athletic activities requires hands-on physical engagement, bodily movement, and direct interaction with participants or equipment.

task: Administer emergency first aid, wrap injuries, treat minor chronic disabilities, or refer injured persons to physicians.
rationale: Requires physical hands-on actions: administering first aid, wrapping injuries, examining patients to assess and treat conditions.

task: Teach individual and team sports to participants through instruction and demonstration, using knowledge of sports techniques and of participants' physical capabilities.
rationale: The task includes physical demonstration of sports techniques and hands-on instruction requiring bodily movement and physical execution to teach participants.

task: Maintain cage security.
rationale: Maintaining cage security requires physical inspection, checking locks, monitoring physical premises, and hands-on work at the site.

task: Perform removal and rotation of cash, coin, or chip inventories as necessary.
rationale: Physical handling and manipulation of cash, coins, or chips requires tangible object manipulation and on-site inventory management.

task: Supply currency, coins, chips, or gaming checks to other departments as needed.
rationale: Supplying currency, coins, chips, or checks requires physically handling and transferring tangible objects to other departments.

task: Record casino exchange transactions, using cash registers.
rationale: Operating a cash register requires physical manipulation of the register, handling cash, and tangible transaction processing.

task: Issue initial and replacement safe-deposit keys to customers, and admit customers to vaults.
rationale: Issuing physical keys and physically admitting customers to vaults requires physical manipulation and presence at work site.

task: Requisition and store shipping materials and supplies to maintain inventory of stock.
rationale: Task involves physically handling, storing, and maintaining inventory of tangible shipping materials and supplies.

task: Construct corners by fastening in plumb position a corner pole or building a corner pyramid of bricks, and filling in between the corners using a line from corner to corner to guide each course, or layer, of brick.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of bricks and corner poles, fastening materials, and hands-on construction work building masonry structures.

task: Measure distance from reference points and mark guidelines to lay out work, using plumb bobs and levels.
rationale: Requires physical use of tools (plumb bobs, levels) and bodily movement to measure, mark, and establish work guidelines at a job site.

task: Fasten or fuse brick or other building material to structure with wire clamps, anchor holes, torch, or cement.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials (brick, clamps, cement) and operation of tools (torch, clamps) to attach building materials to structures.

task: Break or cut bricks, tiles, or blocks to size, using trowel edge, hammer, or power saw.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and operation of tools (trowel, hammer, power saw) to cut/break materials to size.

task: Remove excess mortar with trowels and hand tools, and finish mortar joints with jointing tools, for a sealed, uniform appearance.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical manipulation of hand tools (trowels, jointing tools) to finish mortar joints on masonry surfaces.

task: Apply and smooth mortar or other mixture over work surface.
rationale: Applying and smoothing mortar requires hands-on physical manipulation using tools to spread mixture on surfaces.

task: Mix specified amounts of sand, clay, dirt, or mortar powder with water to form refractory mixtures.
rationale: Physically mixing materials with water requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects and materials.

task: Examine brickwork or structure to determine need for repair.
rationale: Physical inspection of brickwork requires being on-site, touching/examining structural elements to assess condition and repair needs.

task: Clean working surface to remove scale, dust, soot, or chips of brick and mortar, using broom, wire brush, or scraper.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (broom, wire brush, scraper) and bodily movement to clean surfaces of debris.

task: Lay and align bricks, blocks, or tiles to build or repair structures or high temperature equipment, such as cupola, kilns, ovens, or furnaces.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of bricks/blocks/tiles and hands-on placement and alignment to build/repair structures.

task: Remove burned or damaged brick or mortar, using sledgehammer, crowbar, chipping gun, or chisel.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of tools (sledgehammer, crowbar, chisel) and manual manipulation of brick/mortar materials.

task: Spray or spread refractory material over brickwork to protect against deterioration.
rationale: Spraying or spreading refractory material requires physical manipulation and application of materials onto brickwork surfaces using tools or equipment.

task: Start machine, engage clutch, and push and move levers to guide machine along forms or guidelines and to control the operation of machine attachments.
rationale: Operating machinery requires physical manipulation of controls (clutch, levers) and guiding the machine along a path.

task: Inspect, clean, maintain, and repair equipment, using mechanics' hand tools, or report malfunctions to supervisors.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation and operation of hand tools for equipment maintenance, inspection, cleaning, and repair work.

task: Operate machines to spread, smooth, level, or steel-reinforce stone, concrete, or asphalt on road beds.
rationale: Operating machines to manipulate and process materials (stone, concrete, asphalt) requires hands-on physical control of equipment and machinery at a work site.

task: Operate oil distributors, loaders, chip spreaders, dump trucks, and snow plows.
rationale: Operating physical machinery and vehicles (distributors, loaders, spreaders, dump trucks, snow plows) requires hands-on control and physical presence.

task: Set up and tear down equipment.
rationale: Setting up and tearing down equipment requires physical manipulation and operation of tangible objects and tools.

task: Operate tamping machines or manually roll surfaces to compact earth fills, foundation forms, and finished road materials, according to grade specifications.
rationale: Requires physical operation of machinery (tamping machines) and manual labor (rolling surfaces) to compact materials on-site.

task: Shovel blacktop.
rationale: Shoveling requires physical manipulation of materials and operation of a hand tool.

task: Drive machines onto truck trailers, and drive trucks to transport machines and material to and from job sites.
rationale: Involves operating vehicles and machinery (driving trucks, operating machines), requiring physical control and presence to execute transportation tasks.

task: Observe distribution of paving material to adjust machine settings or material flow, and indicate low spots for workers to add material.
rationale: Requires physical presence at work site to observe material distribution, inspect for low spots, and communicate with workers to direct material placement.

task: Light burners or start heating units of machines, and regulate screed temperatures and asphalt flow rates.
rationale: Operating physical heating equipment, lighting burners, and manually regulating machine settings requires hands-on operation of machinery and physical control of equipment.

task: Control paving machines to push dump trucks and to maintain a constant flow of asphalt or other material into hoppers or screeds.
rationale: Operating paving machines requires hands-on physical control of machinery to position equipment and manage material flow during asphalt paving work.

task: Set up forms and lay out guidelines for curbs, according to written specifications, using string, spray paint, and concrete or water mixes.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of tools (string, spray paint, concrete/water mixes), setting up forms, and laying out concrete curbs on-site.

task: Fill tanks, hoppers, or machines with paving materials.
rationale: Filling tanks and hoppers requires physical manipulation and operation of equipment to load and dispense paving materials.

task: Drive and operate curbing machines to extrude concrete or asphalt curbing.
rationale: Operating machinery (curbing machines) to physically extrude and place concrete/asphalt requires hands-on equipment operation and physical presence at work site.

task: Cut or break up pavement and drive guardrail posts, using machines equipped with interchangeable hammers.
rationale: Operating machinery (hammer-equipped machines) and physical manipulation of pavement and guardrail posts at work site.

task: Install dies, cutters, and extensions to screeds onto machines, using hand tools.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tools and equipment to install dies, cutters, and extensions onto machines.

task: Operate machines that clean or cut expansion joints in concrete or asphalt and that rout out cracks in pavement.
rationale: Operating specialized machinery to physically cut, clean, and route concrete/asphalt requires hands-on equipment operation and physical presence at the work site.

task: Place strips of material, such as cork, asphalt, or steel into joints, or place rolls of expansion-joint material on machines that automatically insert material.
rationale: Task involves physical placement and handling of tangible materials (cork, asphalt, steel strips) into joints, requiring manual manipulation and operation of machinery.

task: Trace out short circuits in wiring, using test meter.
rationale: Requires physical operation of a test meter instrument and hands-on inspection of wiring to diagnose electrical problems.

task: Measure, cut, and bend wire and conduit, using measuring instruments and hand tools.
rationale: Physical manipulation of materials (wire, conduit) using hand tools and measuring instruments requires hands-on execution.

task: Maintain tools, vehicles, and equipment and keep parts and supplies in order.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools, vehicles, equipment, parts, and supplies—hands-on maintenance and organization work.

task: Drill holes and pull or push wiring through openings, using hand and power tools.
rationale: Involves drilling holes and physically pulling/pushing wiring using hand and power tools—direct physical manipulation of tools and materials.

task: Perform semi-skilled and unskilled laboring duties related to the installation, maintenance and repair of a wide variety of electrical systems and equipment.
rationale: Installation, maintenance, and repair of electrical systems requires hands-on physical manipulation of equipment, tools, and wiring.

task: Disassemble defective electrical equipment, replace defective or worn parts, and reassemble equipment, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of equipment parts using hand tools to disassemble, replace components, and reassemble—direct manual work.

task: Transport tools, materials, equipment, and supplies to work site by hand, handtruck, or heavy, motorized truck.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical transportation using hands, handtrucks, or operating motorized trucks to move tangible objects.

task: Examine electrical units for loose connections and broken insulation and tighten connections, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation using hand tools to tighten connections and inspect electrical units for damage—hands-on mechanical work.

task: Strip insulation from wire ends, using wire stripping pliers, and attach wires to terminals for subsequent soldering.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of wire and pliers, precise hand-eye coordination for stripping and attaching wires to terminals.

task: Thread conduit ends, connect couplings, and fabricate and secure conduit support brackets, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of conduit and hand tools, assembly of couplings, and fabrication/installation of brackets.

task: Construct controllers and panels, using power drills, drill presses, taps, saws, and punches.
rationale: Task involves physical operation of machinery (power drills, drill presses, taps, saws, punches) to construct tangible objects (controllers and panels).

task: String transmission lines or cables through ducts or conduits, under the ground, through equipment, or to towers.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of cables/lines and operation of equipment to route through ducts, conduits, underground, and structures.

task: Clean work area and wash parts.
rationale: Cleaning and washing require physical manipulation and handling of objects and materials to accomplish the work.

task: Erect electrical system components and barricades, and rig scaffolds, hoists, and shoring.
rationale: Tasks require physical assembly, erection, and operation of equipment, scaffolds, and safety infrastructure at work sites.

task: Install copper-clad ground rods, using a manual post driver.
rationale: Installing ground rods involves physical operation of tools (manual post driver) and tangible manipulation of materials (driving copper-clad rods into ground).

task: Raise, lower, or position equipment, tools, and materials, using hoist, hand line, or block and tackle.
rationale: Operating hoists, hand lines, and block-and-tackle equipment to physically position materials requires hands-on manipulation of tools and objects.

task: Dig trenches or holes for installation of conduit or supports.
rationale: Digging trenches/holes requires physical manipulation of tools and materials at a work site to prepare for installation.

task: Bolt component parts together to form tower assemblies, using hand tools.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of components using hand tools to assemble tower structures.

task: Paint a variety of objects related to electrical functions.
rationale: Painting requires physical manipulation of tools and objects, application of paint to surfaces through bodily movement and hand control.

task: Operate cutting torches and welding equipment, while working with conduit and metal components to construct devices associated with electrical functions.
rationale: Requires operation of welding/cutting torches and physical manipulation of metal components and conduit to construct electrical devices.

task: Break up concrete, using airhammer, to facilitate installation, construction, or repair of equipment.
rationale: Operating an airhammer to break concrete requires physical operation of a tool to manipulate tangible materials on site.

task: Solder electrical connections, using soldering iron.
rationale: Soldering requires operating a soldering iron tool and physically manipulating electrical components to join connections.

task: Trim trees and clear undergrowth along right-of-way.
rationale: Physical manipulation and operation of tools (saws, trimmers, machinery) to cut trees and clear vegetation requires hands-on execution.

task: Cut or drill holes in walls or floors to accommodate the passage of pipes.
rationale: Cutting and drilling holes requires physical operation of tools (drill, saw) and direct manipulation of tangible structures.

task: Measure, cut, thread and assemble new pipe, placing the assembled pipe in hangers or other supports.
rationale: Involves physical manipulation of pipes—measuring, cutting, threading, assembling, and placing in hangers. Hands-on work with tools and materials.

task: Mount brackets and hangers on walls and ceilings to hold pipes, and set sleeves or inserts to provide support for pipes.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of brackets, hangers, sleeves, and installation on walls/ceilings—hands-on construction work.

task: Requisition tools and equipment, select type and size of pipe, and collect and transport materials and equipment to work site.
rationale: Task involves physical selection, collection, and transport of materials/equipment to a work site—requires hands-on manipulation and movement of tangible objects.

task: Fit or assist in fitting valves, couplings, or assemblies to tanks, pumps, or systems, using hand tools.
rationale: Fitting valves and assemblies using hand tools requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and operation of hand tools on physical equipment.

task: Assist pipe fitters in the layout, assembly, and installation of piping for air, ammonia, gas, and water systems.
rationale: Involves physical assembly and installation of piping systems; requires hands-on manipulation of pipes, tools, and physical presence at work site.

task: Excavate and grade ditches, and lay and join pipe for water and sewer service.

task: Cut pipe and lift up to fitters.
rationale: Physical manipulation (cutting pipe, lifting) and handling of tangible objects requiring hands-on execution.

task: Disassemble and remove damaged or worn pipe.
rationale: Disassembling and removing pipes requires physical manipulation of tangible objects using tools, hands-on work at the site.

task: Clean shop, work area, and machines, using solvent and rags.
rationale: Cleaning involves physical manipulation of rags, application of solvent, and hands-on contact with tangible objects and machines.

task: Install gas burners to convert furnaces from wood, coal, or oil.
rationale: Installing burners requires physical manipulation of furnace components, assembly, and hands-on equipment installation work.

task: Immerse pipe in chemical solution to remove dirt, oil, and scale.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of pipes and immersion in chemical solution, direct hands-on work with tangible objects.

task: Clean and renew steam traps.
rationale: Cleaning and renewing steam traps requires hands-on physical manipulation of equipment components and mechanical work at the site.

task: Fill pipes with sand or resin to prevent distortion, and hold pipes during bending and installation.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of pipes, filling with materials, and holding during installation—direct hands-on execution.

task: Flag motorists to warn them of obstacles or repair work ahead.

task: Set out signs and cones around work areas to divert traffic.
rationale: Physical placement of tangible objects (signs and cones) in work areas requires hands-on manipulation and bodily movement.

task: Dump, spread, and tamp asphalt, using pneumatic tampers, to repair joints and patch broken pavement.

task: Drive trucks to transport crews and equipment to work sites.
rationale: Operating vehicles (trucks) to transport is direct physical operation of machinery and requires hands-on execution.

task: Inspect, clean, and repair drainage systems, bridges, tunnels, and other structures.
rationale: Requires physical inspection, cleaning, and repair of structures and systems—hands-on work at job sites.

task: Haul and spread sand, gravel, and clay to fill washouts and repair road shoulders.
rationale: Hauling and spreading materials requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and operation of vehicles/equipment to execute.

task: Erect, install, or repair guardrails, road shoulders, berms, highway markers, warning signals, and highway lighting, using hand tools and power tools.

task: Remove litter and debris from roadways, including debris from rock and mud slides.

task: Clean and clear debris from culverts, catch basins, drop inlets, ditches, and other drain structures.

task: Diagnose and treat diseases, injuries, or malformations of teeth, gums, or related oral structures and provide preventive or corrective services.
rationale: Dentists physically examine teeth/gums, perform procedures like drilling, filling, extraction, and cleaning—requiring hands-on manipulation and clinical presence.

task: Fill pulp chamber and canal with endodontic materials.
rationale: Filling pulp chamber and canal requires physical manipulation of endodontic materials using instruments inside the tooth structure.

task: Treat exposure of pulp by pulp capping, removal of pulp from pulp chamber, or root canal, using dental instruments.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of dental instruments to perform pulp treatment procedures; direct hands-on clinical execution.

task: Eliminate irritating margins of fillings and correct occlusions, using dental instruments.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of dental instruments and direct hands-on work with patient's teeth and fillings.

task: Perform oral or periodontal surgery on the jaw or mouth.
rationale: Surgical procedure requires physical manipulation of oral tissues, operation of surgical instruments, and hands-on clinical execution.

task: Remove diseased tissue, using surgical instruments.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of surgical instruments and tissue; hands-on medical procedure.

task: Apply fluoride or sealants to teeth.
rationale: Applying fluoride or sealants requires direct physical manipulation of dental tools and materials in a patient's mouth.

task: Bleach, clean, or polish teeth to restore natural color.
rationale: Bleaching, cleaning, or polishing teeth requires direct physical manipulation of dental tools and materials in the patient's mouth.

task: Monitor patients' physical and emotional well-being and report unusual behavior or physical ailments to medical staff.
rationale: Monitoring patient well-being requires physical observation, interaction with patients, and hands-on caregiving presence in patient care settings.

task: Provide nursing, psychiatric, or personal care to patients with cognitive, intellectual, or developmental disabilities.
rationale: Direct hands-on caregiving involving physical assistance, bodily contact, and in-person support for vulnerable patients with disabilities.

task: Take and record measures of patients' physical condition, using devices such as thermometers or blood pressure gauges.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of medical devices and direct hands-on patient contact to obtain measurements.

task: Open and close cash registers, performing tasks such as counting money, separating charge slips, coupons, and vouchers, balancing cash drawers, and making deposits.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tangible objects: counting money, handling charge slips, coupons, vouchers, and managing cash drawers.

task: Restrain violent, potentially violent, or suicidal patients by verbal or physical means as required.
rationale: Physically restraining patients requires hands-on bodily contact and physical presence to execute the restraint action.

task: Aid patients in performing tasks, such as bathing or keeping beds, clothing, or living areas clean.
rationale: Assisting patients with bathing, bed-making, and cleaning requires hands-on physical care and manipulation of objects and the patient's body.

task: Administer oral medications or hypodermic injections, following physician's prescriptions and hospital procedures.
rationale: Administering medications and injections requires direct physical handling and manipulation of objects and patient contact.

task: Issue medications from dispensary and maintain records in accordance with specified procedures.
rationale: Issuing medications requires physical handling of pharmaceutical items from a dispensary. Record maintenance is secondary to the primary manual dispensing work.

task: Provide emergency first aid and summon medical assistance when necessary.
rationale: First aid involves physical examination and hands-on treatment of injuries (bandaging, CPR, stabilizing). Summoning assistance is secondary communication.

task: Make regular rounds to ensure that residents and areas are safe and secure.
rationale: Physical presence at work site to patrol and inspect areas for safety and security is bodily movement through the environment.

task: Direct and participate in on- and off-campus recreational activities for residents of institutions, boarding schools, fraternities or sororities, children's homes, or similar establishments.
rationale: Task requires physical presence and bodily participation in recreational activities (sports, games, outdoor events), not just directing.

task: Sort and distribute mail.
rationale: Requires physical handling and sorting of mail items, then delivery/distribution to locations.

task: Provide transportation or escort for expeditions, such as shopping trips or visits to doctors or dentists.
rationale: Driving or physically escorting people requires operating a vehicle and physical presence to navigate and accompany clients to destinations.

task: Inventory, pack, and remove items left behind by former residents.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of objects (inventorying, packing, removing items), necessitating hands-on work with tangible items.

task: Count money and audit money drawers.
rationale: Counting and auditing money drawers requires physical handling of cash and tangible currency objects.

task: Maintain cage security according to rules.
rationale: Maintaining cage security requires physical inspection, checking locks, monitoring enclosures, and hands-on securing work.

task: Furnish change persons with a money bank at the start of each shift.
rationale: Distributing/handing over physical money banks requires physical handling of tangible objects at the start of shift.

task: Walk, ride bicycles, drive vehicles, or use public conveyances to reach destinations to deliver messages or materials.
rationale: Involves physical operation of vehicles and movement to reach destinations—core manual execution required.

task: Load vehicles with listed goods, ensuring goods are loaded correctly and taking precautions with hazardous goods.
rationale: Loading vehicles requires physical manipulation of goods, handling heavy objects, and operating within a work environment. Primary execution is hands-on physical work.

task: Unload and sort items collected along delivery routes.
rationale: Unloading and sorting items requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and bodily movement to handle cargo.

task: Deliver messages and items, such as newspapers, documents, and packages, between establishment departments and to other establishments and private homes.
rationale: Requires physical movement to carry and deliver tangible objects (newspapers, documents, packages) between locations.

task: Perform routine maintenance on delivery vehicles, such as monitoring fluid levels and replenishing fuel.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of vehicles—checking fluid levels, refueling—hands-on vehicle maintenance at the work site.

task: Collect, seal, and stamp outgoing mail, using postage meters and envelope sealers.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of mail, operation of postage meters and envelope sealers, and handling of tangible objects.

task: Check equipment to ensure that it is operating properly.
rationale: Checking equipment requires physical inspection and hands-on examination to verify proper operation.

task: Fight forest fires or perform prescribed burning tasks under the direction of fire suppression officers or forestry technicians.
rationale: Involves physical operations: fighting fires, handling fire equipment, performing burning tasks, and on-site presence in hazardous conditions.

task: Perform fire protection or suppression duties, such as constructing fire breaks or disposing of brush.
rationale: Constructing fire breaks and disposing of brush requires physical labor, tool use, and direct manipulation of materials and terrain.

task: Ticket, arrange, and display merchandise to promote sales.
rationale: Arranging and displaying merchandise requires physical manipulation and positioning of products on shelves and fixtures.

task: Select or cut trees according to markings or sizes, types, or grades.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation—cutting trees and handling equipment like chainsaws. Involves bodily movement and tool operation on-site.

task: Identify diseased or undesirable trees and remove them, using power saws or hand saws.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of power/hand saws and manual removal of trees—direct physical manipulation of tools and objects.

task: Spray or inject vegetation with insecticides to kill insects or to protect against disease or with herbicides to reduce competing vegetation.
rationale: Requires physical operation of spraying/injection equipment and direct application of chemicals to vegetation in the field.

task: Thin or space trees, using power thinning saws.
rationale: Operating a power thinning saw requires physical manipulation of a tool to cut trees.

task: Erect signs or fences, using posthole diggers, shovels, or other hand tools.
rationale: Erecting signs or fences requires physical operation of hand tools (posthole diggers, shovels) and manual construction work.

task: Prune or shear tree tops or limbs to control growth, increase density, or improve shape.
rationale: Pruning/shearing involves physical manipulation of tools (pruners, saws) and direct hands-on work on tree limbs to shape and control growth.

task: Select tree seedlings, prepare the ground, or plant the trees in reforestation areas, using manual planting tools.
rationale: Selecting seedlings, preparing ground, and planting trees require physical manipulation of objects and manual tools in the field.

task: Provide assistance to forest survey crews by clearing site-lines, holding measuring tools, or setting stakes.
rationale: Task involves physical actions: clearing brush, holding tools, and setting stakes at survey sites.

task: Operate skidders, bulldozers, or other prime movers to pull a variety of scarification or site preparation equipment over areas to be regenerated.
rationale: Operating heavy machinery (skidders, bulldozers) to manipulate ground and pull equipment requires physical operation of vehicles and equipment at a work site.

task: Maintain campsites or recreational areas, replenishing firewood or other supplies and cleaning kitchens or restrooms.
rationale: Replenishing supplies, cleaning facilities, and campsite maintenance require physical manipulation and bodily presence to perform hands-on work at the site.

task: Check the forms that hold the concrete to see that they are properly constructed.
rationale: Physically inspecting concrete forms requires being on-site, examining tangible objects, and potentially touching/examining structural elements to verify proper construction.

task: Set the forms that hold concrete to the desired pitch and depth, and align them.
rationale: Setting forms requires physical manipulation of objects (forms), measuring, aligning, and positioning them—hands-on work with tangible materials.

task: Spread, level, and smooth concrete, using rake, shovel, hand or power trowel, hand or power screed, and float.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of concrete and operation of hand/power tools (rake, shovel, trowel, screed, float) at work site.

task: Mold expansion joints and edges, using edging tools, jointers, and straightedge.
rationale: Requires physical operation of tools (jointers, edging tools) and hands-on manipulation of materials to shape expansion joints and edges.

task: Signal truck driver to position truck to facilitate pouring concrete, and move chute to direct concrete on forms.
rationale: Physical operation of concrete truck equipment and manual manipulation of chute to direct concrete placement at job site.

task: Produce rough concrete surface, using broom.
rationale: Uses a physical tool (broom) to manipulate concrete surface through hands-on sweeping work.

task: Operate power vibrator to compact concrete.
rationale: Operating a power vibrator requires physical manipulation and operation of a powered tool to compact concrete at a work site.

task: Mix cement, sand, and water to produce concrete, grout, or slurry, using hoe, trowel, tamper, scraper, or concrete-mixing machine.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials and operation of tools/machines (hoe, trowel, tamper, scraper, concrete mixer) to produce concrete products.

task: Cut out damaged areas, drill holes for reinforcing rods, and position reinforcing rods to repair concrete, using power saw and drill.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of power tools (saw, drill) and manual positioning of reinforcing rods to repair concrete—hands-on construction work.

task: Wet concrete surface, and rub with stone to smooth surface and obtain specified finish.
rationale: Physical manipulation of concrete surface with stone tool to smooth and finish requires hands-on contact and physical movement.

task: Wet surface to prepare for bonding, fill holes and cracks with grout or slurry, and smooth, using trowel.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of materials (wetting surface, filling holes, smoothing with trowel). Hands-on work with tangible objects and tools.

task: Demonstrate use or operation of merchandise.
rationale: Demonstrating merchandise requires physical handling and operation of the product to show its functionality to customers or clients.

task: Clean chipped area, using wire brush, and feel and observe surface to determine if it is rough or uneven.
rationale: Requires physical use of wire brush tool and tactile/visual inspection by touching and observing the surface directly.

task: Apply hardening and sealing compounds to cure surface of concrete, and waterproof or restore surface.
rationale: Requires physical application of compounds to concrete surfaces using tools and hands-on work at the site.

task: Chip, scrape, and grind high spots, ridges, and rough projections to finish concrete, using pneumatic chisels, power grinders, or hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of pneumatic tools and hand tools to manipulate concrete surfaces through grinding, scraping, and chipping.

task: Spread roofing paper on surface of foundation, and spread concrete onto roofing paper with trowel to form terrazzo base.
rationale: Spreading roofing paper and concrete with a trowel requires physical manipulation and use of tools on a work site.

task: Build wooden molds, and clamp molds around area to be repaired, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials (building molds, clamping), use of hand tools, and hands-on work with tangible objects.

task: Sprinkle colored marble or stone chips, powdered steel, or coloring powder over surface to produce prescribed finish.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials (sprinkling chips/powder) over a surface to create a finish. Primary execution is hands-on physical work.

task: Cut metal division strips, and press them into terrazzo base so that top edges form desired design or pattern.
rationale: Cutting and pressing metal strips into terrazzo requires physical manipulation of materials and tools to shape and install them into the base.

task: Fabricate concrete beams, columns, and panels.
rationale: Fabricating concrete beams, columns, and panels requires physical manipulation of materials, operating machinery (concrete mixers, molds, lifting equipment), and hands-on construction work.

task: Waterproof or restore concrete surfaces, using appropriate compounds.
rationale: Applying waterproofing/restoration compounds requires direct physical manipulation and application to concrete surfaces.

task: Install anchor bolts, steel plates, door sills and other fixtures in freshly poured concrete or pattern or stamp the surface to provide a decorative finish.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of fixtures into concrete and stamping/patterning the surface—hands-on physical work.

task: Clean shelves, counters, and tables.
rationale: Cleaning requires physical manipulation and contact with surfaces and objects.

task: Apply muriatic acid to clean surface, and rinse with water.
rationale: Applying chemical solution and rinsing surfaces requires direct physical manipulation and chemical handling on-site.

task: Push roller over surface to embed chips in surface.
rationale: Pushing a roller over a surface requires physical manipulation and bodily movement to operate a tool for embedding material.

task: Polish surface, using polishing or surfacing machine.
rationale: Operating a polishing/surfacing machine requires physical operation of machinery and hands-on control of the equipment and workpiece.

task: Drive truck-mounted units to well sites.
rationale: Operating a vehicle (truck) to transport equipment to work sites is physical machinery operation.

task: Install pressure-control devices onto wellheads.
rationale: Installing devices onto wellheads requires physical manipulation of equipment and tools at a work site.

task: Thread cables through derrick pulleys, using hand tools.
rationale: Threading cables through pulleys requires physical manipulation using hand tools and hands-on work at a height or work site.

task: Operate pumps that circulate water, oil, or other fluids through wells to remove sand or other materials obstructing the free flow of oil.
rationale: Operating pumps involves physically manipulating equipment and machinery to circulate fluids through wells, a hands-on operational task.

task: Close and seal wells no longer in use.
rationale: Closing and sealing wells involves physical manipulation of equipment, materials, and structures to permanently disable wells—manual labor with tangible objects.

task: Operate controls that raise derricks or level rigs.
rationale: Operating physical controls of machinery (derricks, rigs) to manipulate equipment requires hands-on physical operation of mechanical systems.

task: Perforate well casings or sidewalls of boreholes with explosive charges.
rationale: Task requires physical placement and detonation of explosive charges in wells—direct hands-on operation of tools and materials at work site.

task: Operate specialized equipment to remove obstructions by backing off or severing pipes by chemical or explosive action.
rationale: Requires hands-on operation of specialized equipment and physical tools to perform pipe removal/obstruction clearing work.

task: Select fishing methods or tools for removing obstacles such as liners, broken casing, screens, or drill pipe.
rationale: Selecting and using fishing tools/methods requires physical manipulation of specialized equipment and tools to remove downhole obstacles in wells.

task: Examine unserviceable wells to determine actions to be taken to improve well conditions.
rationale: Examining wells requires physical on-site inspection, accessing well locations, and direct observation of conditions to assess structural and operational status.

task: Insert detection instruments into wells with obstructions.
rationale: Inserting instruments into wells requires physical manipulation of tools and objects in a tangible work environment.

task: Monitor sound wave-generating or detecting mechanisms to determine well fluid levels.
rationale: Monitoring sound detection equipment at a well requires physical presence, operation of instruments, and hands-on interaction with equipment to assess fluid levels.

task: Bag or package purchases and wrap gifts.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation—bagging, packaging, and wrapping items with hands and tools.

task: Help customers try on or fit merchandise.
rationale: Fitting merchandise requires hands-on physical interaction: adjusting garments, handling items, and assessing fit through touch and observation.

task: Clean up spilled oil by bailing it into barrels.
rationale: Physical manipulation of spilled oil and barrels; requires hands-on cleanup and material handling.

task: Unscrew or tighten pipes, casing, tubing, and pump rods, using hand and power wrenches and tongs.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (hand/power wrenches, tongs) and direct handling of tangible objects (pipes, casing, tubing, pump rods) to unscrew or tighten them.

task: Bolt together pump and engine parts.
rationale: Bolting together parts requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and operation of tools to assemble components.

task: Move pipes to and from trucks, using truck winches and motorized lifts, or by hand.
rationale: Involves physical manipulation and operation of equipment (winches, lifts) plus manual handling of pipes.

task: Dismantle and repair oil field machinery, boilers, and steam engine parts, using hand tools and power tools.
rationale: Task requires physical dismantling and repair of machinery using hand tools and power tools—primary execution is hands-on mechanical work.

task: Dig drainage ditches around wells and storage tanks.
rationale: Requires physical labor: digging, earthmoving, and hands-on manipulation of soil and tools at a work site.

task: Prepare merchandise for purchase or rental.
rationale: Preparing merchandise requires physically handling, organizing, packaging, or arranging tangible items for customer transactions.

task: Supply equipment to rig floors as requested and provide assistance to roughnecks.
rationale: Supplying equipment and assisting roughnecks requires physical manipulation of equipment and hands-on work at oil rig sites.

task: Dig holes, set forms, and mix and pour concrete into forms to make foundations for wood or steel derricks.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools, materials, and equipment: digging, mixing, and pouring concrete—all hands-on work.

task: Cut down and remove trees and brush to clear drill sites, to reduce fire hazards, and to make way for roads to sites.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation—cutting, removing trees and brush with tools and equipment at work sites.

task: Cut new or duplicate keys, using key cutting machines.
rationale: Operating key-cutting machines and physically manipulating keys requires hands-on operation of physical equipment and tools.

task: Insert new or repaired tumblers into locks to change combinations.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of lock components (tumblers) and tools to install/repair them into locks.

task: Move picklocks in cylinders to open door locks without keys.
rationale: Picking a lock requires physical manipulation of tools (picklocks) and interaction with physical objects (lock cylinders) to achieve mechanical results.

task: Disassemble mechanical or electrical locking devices, and repair or replace worn tumblers, springs, and other parts, using hand tools.
rationale: Disassembling and repairing locking devices requires physical manipulation of small parts, hand tools, and tangible object repair work.

task: Repair and adjust safes, vault doors, and vault components, using hand tools, lathes, drill presses, and welding and acetylene cutting apparatus.
rationale: Requires hands-on operation of physical tools, machinery (lathes, drill presses, welding equipment), and physical manipulation of vault components.

task: Install safes, vault doors, and deposit boxes according to blueprints, using equipment such as power drills, taps, dies, truck cranes, and dollies.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of heavy objects (safes, vault doors), operation of power tools (drills, taps, dies) and machinery (cranes, dollies), and hands-on installation work.

task: Open safe locks by drilling.
rationale: Drilling requires operating a physical tool (drill) and manual manipulation to open locks—primary execution is hands-on mechanical work.

task: Remove interior and exterior finishes on safes and vaults, and spray on new finishes.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of objects (removing finishes), operation of spray equipment, and hands-on application of new finishes.

task: Read prescriptions or specifications and examine models or impressions to determine the design of dental products to be constructed.
rationale: Examining physical models, impressions, and reading prescriptions requires hands-on physical inspection and interpretation of tangible dental objects.

task: Fabricate, alter, or repair dental devices, such as dentures, crowns, bridges, inlays, or appliances for straightening teeth.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of dental materials and devices, operation of dental lab tools and equipment, hands-on fabrication and assembly work.

task: Test appliances for conformance to specifications and accuracy of occlusion, using articulators and micrometers.
rationale: Testing appliances with articulators and micrometers requires hands-on physical manipulation, measuring, and inspection of tangible dental equipment.

task: Place tooth models on an apparatus that mimics bite and movement of patient's jaw to evaluate functionality of model.
rationale: Placing tooth models on apparatus and evaluating functionality requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and hands-on setup.

task: Melt metals or mix plaster, porcelain, or acrylic pastes and pour materials into molds or over frameworks to form dental prostheses or apparatuses.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials (melting metals, mixing pastes, pouring into molds) and hands-on work forming dental prostheses.

task: Prepare metal surfaces for bonding with porcelain to create artificial teeth, using small hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of metal surfaces with small hand tools to prepare them for bonding—direct hands-on manual work.

task: Remove excess metal or porcelain and polish surfaces of prostheses or frameworks, using polishing machines.
rationale: Task involves physical operation of polishing machines and manual manipulation of prostheses/frameworks to remove material and polish surfaces.

task: Create a model of patient's mouth by pouring plaster into a dental impression and allowing plaster to set.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of plaster and dental impression materials to create a tangible model through pouring and setting.

task: Load newly constructed teeth into porcelain furnaces to bake the porcelain onto the metal framework.
rationale: Loading objects into furnaces requires physical manipulation and operation of equipment to perform the task.

task: Build and shape wax teeth, using small hand instruments and information from observations or dentists' specifications.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of wax using hand instruments to build and shape teeth based on specifications.

task: Apply porcelain paste or wax over prosthesis frameworks or setups, using brushes and spatulas.
rationale: Applying porcelain paste or wax requires physical manipulation of tools (brushes, spatulas) and direct handling of prosthesis materials at a workbench.

task: Fill chipped or low spots in surfaces of devices, using acrylic resins.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of materials (acrylic resins) to fill surface defects on devices, involving hands-on application work.

task: Prepare wax bite blocks and impression trays for use.
rationale: Preparing wax bite blocks and impression trays requires physical manipulation and setup of dental equipment and materials for patient use.

task: Mold wax over denture setups to form the full contours of artificial gums.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of wax material and hands-on shaping to form artificial gum contours on denture setups.

task: Rebuild or replace linings, wire sections, or missing teeth to repair dentures.
rationale: Rebuilding or replacing denture linings, wires, and teeth involves physical manipulation of dental prosthetic objects and hands-on repair work.

task: Shape and solder wire and metal frames or bands for dental products, using soldering irons and hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of metal wire/frames and operation of soldering irons and hand tools—direct hands-on work with tangible objects.

task: Move levers to activate traffic signals, navigation lights, and alarms.
rationale: Moving levers requires physical manipulation of tangible objects/controls to operate machinery systems.

task: Control machinery to open and close canal locks and dams, railroad or highway drawbridges, or horizontally or vertically adjustable bridges.
rationale: Operating machinery to control physical infrastructure (locks, dams, drawbridges) requires hands-on operation of mechanical/electrical equipment and direct physical control systems.

task: Maintain and guard stations in bridges to check waterways for boat traffic.
rationale: Requires physical presence at bridge stations to monitor waterways and perform guard duties on-site.

task: Inspect canal and bridge equipment, and areas, such as roadbeds, for damage or defects, reporting problems to supervisors as necessary.
rationale: Requires physical inspection and presence at work site to observe and assess damage to equipment, structures, and roadbeds in person.

task: Clean and lubricate equipment, and make minor repairs and adjustments.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of equipment for cleaning, lubrication, repairs, and adjustments.

task: Perform maintenance duties, such as sweeping, painting, and yard work to keep facilities clean and in order.
rationale: Physical manipulation of tools and materials: sweeping, painting, and yard work require hands-on execution with physical tools and equipment.

task: Check that bridges are clear of vehicles and pedestrians prior to opening.
rationale: Requires physical presence at the bridge to visually inspect and verify clearance of vehicles and pedestrians before operating.

task: Turn valves to increase or decrease water levels in locks.
rationale: Operating physical valves requires direct manual manipulation of tangible objects to control water levels.

task: Stop automobile and pedestrian traffic on bridges, and lower automobile gates prior to moving bridges.
rationale: Physical operation of gates and control mechanisms to manage traffic flow on moving bridges requires hands-on equipment operation.

task: Raise drawbridges and observe passage of water traffic or lower drawbridges and raise automobile gates.
rationale: Operating physical machinery (raising/lowering drawbridges and gates) requires hands-on control of equipment and physical systems.

task: Attach ropes or cable lines to bitts on lock decks or wharfs to secure vessels.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of ropes/cables and attachment to bitts on docks—direct hands-on work with tangible objects.

task: Inspect trucks prior to beginning routes to ensure safe operating condition.
rationale: Physical inspection of trucks requires hands-on examination of equipment, tires, brakes, and components at the work site.

task: Refuel trucks or add other fluids, such as oil or brake fluid.
rationale: Refueling and adding fluids requires physical operation of equipment and manipulation of tangible objects like fuel nozzles and fluid containers.

task: Drive trucks, following established routes, through residential streets or alleys or through business or industrial areas.
rationale: Driving trucks is operation of physical machinery/vehicles requiring active manual control and physical presence.

task: Operate equipment that compresses collected refuse.
rationale: Operating a compressor is physical machinery operation requiring hands-on control and bodily presence.

task: Operate automated or semi-automated hoisting devices that raise refuse bins and dump contents into openings in truck bodies.
rationale: Operating hoisting devices involves hands-on control of machinery to physically manipulate refuse bins and dump their contents.

task: Dismount garbage trucks to collect garbage and remount trucks to ride to the next collection point.
rationale: Dismounting and remounting trucks, physically collecting garbage requires hands-on physical manipulation and bodily movement at worksites.

task: Tag garbage or recycling containers to inform customers of problems, such as excess garbage or inclusion of items that are not permitted.
rationale: Task requires physical placement of tags on garbage/recycling containers, which involves hands-on manipulation of objects.

task: Clean trucks or compactor bodies after routes have been completed.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and operation of cleaning equipment on trucks/compactors at work site.

task: Provide for stewardship of plant or animal resources or habitats, studying land use, monitoring animal populations, or providing shelter, resources, or medical treatment for animals.
rationale: Stewardship involves hands-on management, monitoring, and physical care of plants/animals and habitats—requires bodily presence and manipulation.

task: Inspect grounds, facilities, and equipment routinely to determine necessity of repairs or maintenance.
rationale: Requires physical presence on-site to visually examine and touch facilities, equipment, and grounds to assess their condition.

task: Clean common areas, change light bulbs, and make minor property repairs.
rationale: All three tasks require physical manipulation and hands-on work: cleaning involves physical labor, changing bulbs requires manual action, and repairs require hands-on execution.

task: Examine damaged vehicle to determine extent of structural, body, mechanical, electrical, or interior damage.
rationale: Physical inspection of vehicle damage requires hands-on examination, touching and visual assessment of actual structural, body, and mechanical components onsite.

task: Inspect facilities and equipment, such as emergency management centers and communications equipment, to determine their operational and functional capabilities in emergency situations.
rationale: Requires physical presence to inspect facilities and equipment hands-on to assess operational and functional capabilities in emergencies.

task: Inventory and distribute nuclear, biological, and chemical detection and contamination equipment, providing instruction in its maintenance and use.
rationale: Inventory and distribution require physical handling of equipment; instruction involves demonstrating device operation and maintenance—hands-on work.

task: Inspect property, examining its general condition, type of construction, age, and other characteristics, to decide if it is a good insurance risk.
rationale: Inspection requires being on-site, physically examining construction, materials, condition—hands-on assessment of tangible property characteristics.

task: Monitor functioning of equipment and make necessary modifications to ensure system operates in conformance with specifications.
rationale: Monitoring and modifying equipment requires physical inspection, hands-on adjustment, and operation of machinery to ensure conformance with specifications.

task: Build, test, and modify product prototypes, using working models or theoretical models constructed with computer simulation.
rationale: Building prototypes requires physical manipulation of materials and assembly of tangible objects. Testing involves hands-on interaction with working models.

task: Assemble and modify existing pieces of equipment to meet special needs.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical manipulation and assembly of tangible equipment pieces and components.

task: Test new products for flavor, texture, color, nutritional content, and adherence to government and industry standards.
rationale: Testing products requires hands-on sensory evaluation (tasting, touching, observing) and likely laboratory analysis using equipment.

task: Check raw ingredients for maturity or stability for processing, and finished products for safety, quality, and nutritional value.
rationale: Requires physical inspection and testing of raw materials and finished products to assess maturity, stability, safety, and quality.

task: Demonstrate products to clients.
rationale: Demonstrating products to clients requires physical interaction with tangible products to show features and functionality.

task: Inspect food processing areas to ensure compliance with government regulations and standards for sanitation, safety, quality, and waste management.
rationale: Inspecting requires physical presence at work sites to examine facilities, surfaces, equipment, and conditions firsthand for compliance.

task: Measure and assess vegetation resources for biological assessment companies, environmental impact statements, and rangeland monitoring programs.
rationale: Measuring and assessing vegetation requires physical field work, including hands-on sampling, visual inspection, and collection of plant data at work sites.

task: Maintain soil stability and vegetation for non-grazing uses, such as wildlife habitats and outdoor recreation.
rationale: Task requires physical land management, vegetation planting/maintenance, and hands-on site work to establish and maintain habitats.

task: Plan and implement revegetation of disturbed sites.
rationale: Revegetation involves physical site preparation, planting, mulching, and hands-on installation of vegetation at work sites.

task: Follow strict safety procedures when handling toxic materials to avoid contamination.
rationale: Handling toxic materials involves physical manipulation of tangible objects with required safety protocols and bodily presence at worksite.

task: Prepare and analyze organ, tissue, and cell samples to identify toxicity, bacteria, or microorganisms or to study cell structure.
rationale: Preparing samples requires physical manipulation of organs, tissues, and cells using laboratory equipment and techniques.

task: Use equipment such as atomic absorption spectrometers, electron microscopes, flow cytometers, or chromatography systems.
rationale: Operating specialized laboratory equipment requires hands-on physical interaction with machines to conduct analysis or measurement.

task: Collect, sort, distribute, or prepare mail, messages, or courier deliveries.
