task: Shovel snow from walks, driveways, or parking lots, and spread salt in those areas.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation (shoveling) and spreading salt—direct bodily movement and tool operation at work site.

task: Care for established lawns by mulching, aerating, weeding, grubbing, removing thatch, or trimming or edging around flower beds, walks, or walls.
rationale: Lawn care involves physical manipulation of tools and equipment (mulching, aerating, weeding, trimming) and hands-on work with plants and soil.

task: Use hand tools, such as shovels, rakes, pruning saws, saws, hedge or brush trimmers, or axes.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of hand tools (shovels, saws, trimmers, axes) to manipulate objects and perform landscaping/maintenance work.

task: Prune or trim trees, shrubs, or hedges, using shears, pruners, or chain saws.
rationale: Requires physical operation of tools (shears, pruners, chain saws) and manual manipulation to cut vegetation.

task: Gather and remove litter.
rationale: Gathering and removing litter requires physical manipulation—bending, picking up, carrying, and disposing of tangible objects.

task: Maintain or repair tools, equipment, or structures, such as buildings, greenhouses, fences, or benches, using hand or power tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools and equipment to repair/maintain tangible objects like buildings, fences, and structures.

task: Mix and spray or spread fertilizers, herbicides, or insecticides onto grass, shrubs, or trees, using hand or automatic sprayers or spreaders.
rationale: Requires physical operation of sprayers/spreaders and manual application of chemicals to plants and vegetation.

task: Provide proper upkeep of sidewalks, driveways, parking lots, fountains, planters, burial sites, or other grounds features.
rationale: Upkeep requires physical manipulation—cleaning, sweeping, repairing, planting—and hands-on work with grounds and features.

task: Water lawns, trees, or plants, using portable sprinkler systems, hoses, or watering cans.
rationale: Physical manipulation of watering equipment and direct interaction with plants via hoses, sprinklers, and cans.

task: Trim or pick flowers and clean flower beds.
rationale: Trimming and picking flowers, cleaning beds involve physical manipulation of plants and manual labor in outdoor environment.

task: Rake, mulch, and compost leaves.
rationale: Raking, mulching, and composting leaves requires physical manipulation of leaves and use of tools like rakes.

task: Follow planned landscaping designs to determine where to lay sod, sow grass, or plant flowers or foliage.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of landscaping materials (laying sod, sowing grass, planting) and bodily presence at the work site.

task: Plant seeds, bulbs, foliage, flowering plants, grass, ground covers, trees, or shrubs, and apply mulch for protection, using gardening tools.
rationale: Physical manipulation of plants and soil using gardening tools; hands-on work with tangible horticultural materials.

task: Decorate gardens with stones or plants.
rationale: Decorating gardens requires physical manipulation of stones and plants, placement, and hands-on landscape work at the site.

task: Maintain irrigation systems, including winterizing the systems and starting them up in spring.
rationale: Maintaining irrigation systems requires physical operation, adjustment, and repair of equipment and infrastructure components.

task: Care for natural turf fields, making sure the underlying soil has the required composition to allow proper drainage and to support the grasses.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of soil, inspection of drainage systems, and hands-on grounds maintenance work.

task: Use irrigation methods to adjust the amount of water consumption and to prevent waste.
rationale: Operating irrigation systems, adjusting water controls, and managing physical infrastructure requires hands-on manipulation of equipment and tools at a work site.

task: Haul or spread topsoil, or spread straw over seeded soil to hold soil in place.
rationale: Hauling and spreading topsoil/straw requires physical manipulation and operation of tools or vehicles to move and distribute materials.

task: Care for artificial turf fields, periodically removing the turf and replacing cushioning pads or vacuuming and disinfecting the turf after use to prevent the growth of harmful bacteria.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation: removing turf, replacing pads, vacuuming, and disinfecting. Hands-on work with tangible objects at the field site.

task: Plan or cultivate lawns or gardens.
rationale: Cultivating lawns/gardens involves physical manipulation of soil, plants, tools, and bodily movement at the work site.

task: Attach wires from planted trees to support stakes.
rationale: Attaching wires requires physical manipulation of tangible objects (wires, trees, stakes) and hands-on work at an outdoor site.

task: Install rock gardens, ponds, decks, drainage systems, irrigation systems, retaining walls, fences, planters, or playground equipment.
rationale: Installing outdoor structures and systems requires physical manipulation of materials, tools, and equipment at a work site.

task: Build forms and mix and pour cement to form garden borders.
rationale: Building forms, mixing cement, and pouring it requires physical manipulation of materials and tools—hands-on construction work.

task: Distribute costumes or related equipment and keep records of item status.
rationale: Distributing costumes requires physical handling and manipulation of tangible items; record-keeping is secondary to primary execution.

task: Arrange costumes in order of use to facilitate quick-change procedures for performances.
rationale: Arranging costumes requires physical manipulation of tangible clothing items and organizing them spatially for stage operations.

task: Return borrowed or rented items when productions are complete and return other items to storage.
rationale: Requires physical handling and transportation of tangible items back to storage or rental locations.

task: Clean and press costumes before and after performances and perform any minor repairs.
rationale: Cleaning, pressing, and repairing costumes requires physical manipulation of garments and operation of pressing equipment.

task: Assign lockers to employees and maintain locker rooms, dressing rooms, wig rooms, or costume storage or laundry areas.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and maintenance of lockers, organizing spaces, and managing physical storage areas in person.

task: Design or construct costumes or send them to tailors for construction, major repairs, or alterations.
rationale: Designing involves sketching/pattern-making; constructing requires sewing, cutting, and manipulating fabric and materials physically. Both are hands-on work.

task: Inventory stock to determine types or conditions of available costuming.
rationale: Inventorying stock requires physical inspection of costuming items to assess types and conditions on-site.

task: Monitor, maintain, or secure inventories of costumes, wigs, or makeup, providing keys or access to assigned directors, costume designers, or wardrobe mistresses/masters.
rationale: Involves physical monitoring and maintaining tangible inventory (costumes, wigs, makeup), plus distributing keys and granting physical access to secure areas.

task: Examine costume fit on cast members and sketch or write notes for alterations.
rationale: Task requires physical inspection of costumes on cast members, hands-on examination, and direct observation at the work site.

task: Care for non-clothing items, such as flags, table skirts, or draperies.
rationale: Caring for fabric items requires physical handling—cleaning, mending, arranging, or maintaining tangible objects like flags and draperies.

task: Direct and assist passengers in emergency procedures, such as evacuating a plane following an emergency landing.
rationale: Directing passengers in emergency evacuation requires physical presence, demonstrating procedures, and hands-on assistance moving people during active emergency.

task: Announce and demonstrate safety and emergency procedures, such as the use of oxygen masks, seat belts, and life jackets.
rationale: Demonstrating safety equipment requires physical manipulation and hands-on showing of oxygen masks, seat belts, and life jackets to passengers.

task: Walk aisles of planes to verify that passengers have complied with federal regulations prior to takeoffs and landings.
rationale: Task requires physical presence in plane aisles to inspect and verify passenger compliance through direct observation and physical interaction.

task: Verify that first aid kits and other emergency equipment, including fire extinguishers and oxygen bottles, are in working order.
rationale: Physically inspecting and testing emergency equipment (first aid kits, fire extinguishers, oxygen bottles) requires hands-on examination and operation of tangible objects.

task: Administer first aid to passengers in distress.
rationale: Administering first aid requires physical hands-on action (bandaging, CPR, wound care, vital checks).

task: Check to ensure that food, beverages, blankets, reading material, emergency equipment, and other supplies are aboard and are in adequate supply.
rationale: Physical inspection and checking of tangible supplies and equipment aboard a vessel or transport.

task: Assist passengers entering or disembarking the aircraft.
rationale: Physically assisting passengers requires bodily movement, hands-on support, and presence at the aircraft location to guide and help people board/exit.

task: Inspect and clean cabins, checking for any problems and making sure that cabins are in order.
rationale: Task requires physical presence to inspect cabins visually and touching/handling to clean, which is hands-on work with physical objects.

task: Conduct periodic trips through the cabin to ensure passenger comfort and to distribute reading material, headphones, pillows, playing cards, and blankets.
rationale: Physical movement through cabin distributing tangible objects requires bodily presence and manual handling of items.

task: Take inventory of headsets, alcoholic beverages, and money collected.
rationale: Taking inventory requires physically counting and handling tangible items (headsets, beverages, money).

task: Operate audio and video systems.
rationale: Operating audio and video systems requires physical interaction with equipment controls, buttons, switches, and devices to manage their function.

task: Assist passengers in placing carry-on luggage in overhead, garment, or under-seat storage.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of luggage items and bodily movement to place them in storage compartments.

task: Heat and serve prepared foods.
rationale: Heating and serving food involves physical manipulation of food items, use of cooking equipment, and hands-on food preparation activities.

task: Examine merchandise to ensure correct pricing and display, and that it functions as advertised.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of merchandise to verify pricing, display, and functionality by examining items directly.

task: Examine products purchased for resale or received for storage to determine product condition.
rationale: Physical inspection of products requires hands-on examination, touching, and visual assessment of items in person to determine condition.

task: Diagnose problems with installed equipment.
rationale: Diagnosing equipment requires hands-on inspection, testing, and physical interaction with machinery to identify problems.

task: Coordinate or perform activities associated with shipping, receiving, distribution, or transportation.
rationale: Tasks involve physical handling of shipments, operating vehicles, loading/unloading cargo, and managing distribution at warehouses.

task: Conduct annual, interim, and special housing reviews and home visits to ensure conformance to regulations.
rationale: Requires physical home visits and in-person inspection of housing to verify compliance with regulations.

task: Provide boarding or disembarking assistance to passengers needing special assistance.
rationale: Requires physical assistance—helping passengers board/disembark involves hands-on support, guiding, stabilizing, and potentially lifting passengers.

task: Check baggage and cargo and direct passengers to designated locations for loading.
rationale: Task requires physical inspection of baggage/cargo and bodily presence to direct passengers, combining physical examination with in-person work.

task: Check survey responses for errors, such as the use of pens instead of pencils, and set aside response forms that cannot be used.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of survey forms to examine writing implements and handling/setting aside unusable forms.

task: Inspect equipment for safety prior to use, and perform necessary basic maintenance tasks.
rationale: Physical inspection of equipment and hands-on maintenance tasks require direct physical interaction with tangible objects.

task: Drive straight or articulated tractors equipped with accessories such as bulldozer blades, grapples, logging arches, cable winches, and crane booms to skid, load, unload, or stack logs, pull stumps, or clear brush.
rationale: Operating heavy machinery (tractors with accessories) to manipulate logs and stumps in the field requires hands-on physical operation of equipment and machinery.

task: Drive crawler or wheeled tractors to drag or transport logs from felling sites to log landing areas for processing and loading.
rationale: Operating heavy machinery (crawler/wheeled tractors) to transport logs requires hands-on operation of equipment and physical presence at work sites.

task: Drive tractors for building or repairing logging and skid roads.
rationale: Operating heavy machinery (tractors) to physically build and repair logging roads requires direct operation of equipment and physical work at job sites.

task: Grade logs according to characteristics such as knot size and straightness, and according to established industry or company standards.
rationale: Grading logs requires physical inspection and assessment of tangible objects, handling and examining logs in person to evaluate their characteristics.

task: Control hydraulic tractors equipped with tree clamps and booms to lift, swing, and bunch sheared trees.
rationale: Requires operating physical machinery (hydraulic tractors) and manipulating tangible objects (trees) through equipment controls.

task: Drive and maneuver tractors and tree harvesters to shear the tops off of trees, cut and limb the trees, and cut the logs into desired lengths.
rationale: Primary execution involves operating heavy machinery (tractors, harvesters) and physically manipulating trees and logs in forestry work.

task: Paint identification marks of specified colors on logs to identify grades or species, using spray cans, or call out grades to log markers.
rationale: Task involves physical operation of spray cans to apply paint marks on logs, or calling out grades to workers performing the marking.

task: Measure felled logs or loads of pulpwood to calculate volume, weight, dimensions, and marketable value, using measuring devices and conversion tables.
rationale: Requires physical measurement of logs/pulpwood using measuring devices at the work site, involving hands-on inspection and tangible object manipulation.

task: Measure log lengths and mark boles for bucking into logs, according to specifications.
rationale: Task requires physical measurement of logs and marking them for cutting, involving hands-on inspection and marking of tangible objects.

task: Identify logs of substandard or special grade so that they can be returned to shippers, regraded, recut, or transferred for other processing.
rationale: Task requires physical inspection and handling of logs at a work site to identify substandard quality.

task: Jab logs with metal ends of scale sticks, and inspect logs to ascertain characteristics or defects such as water damage, splits, knots, broken ends, rotten areas, twists, and curves.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of logs, operation of metal scale sticks, and hands-on inspection touching/examining objects for defects.

task: Drive to sawmills, wharfs, or skids to inspect logs or pulpwood.
rationale: Requires driving a vehicle to physical work sites and physically inspecting logs/pulpwood on location.

task: Weigh log trucks before and after unloading, and record load weights and supplier identities.
rationale: Weighing trucks requires physical operation of weighing equipment and bodily presence at the work site to interact with vehicles.

task: Saw felled trees into lengths.
rationale: Sawing involves physical operation of a saw tool and manipulation of felled trees into cut lengths.

task: Cut metal division strips and press them into the terrazzo base for joints or changes of color to form designs or patterns or to help prevent cracks.
rationale: Cutting metal strips and pressing them into terrazzo base requires hands-on physical manipulation and operation of tools to achieve the work product.

task: Blend marble chip mixtures, place into panels, and push a roller over the surface to embed the chips.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of materials (blending, placing chips) and operating a tool (roller) to embed—requires hands-on execution.

task: Measure designated amounts of ingredients for terrazzo or grout, according to standard formulas and specifications, using graduated containers and scales, and load ingredients into portable mixer.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of ingredients, operation of scales and measuring containers, and manual loading into a mixer.

task: Mold expansion joints and edges, using edging tools, jointers, or straightedges.
rationale: Uses physical tools (edging tools, jointers, straightedges) to manipulate materials, requiring hands-on manual execution.

task: Spread, level, or smooth concrete or terrazzo mixtures to form bases or finished surfaces, using rakes, shovels, hand or power trowels, hand or power screeds, or floats.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of concrete/terrazzo using tools (trowels, screeds, floats, rakes, shovels) and bodily movement to spread, level, and smooth materials.

task: Grind curved surfaces or areas inaccessible to surfacing machine, such as stairways or cabinet tops, with portable hand grinder.
rationale: Task requires operating a portable hand grinder tool to physically manipulate and process surfaces through grinding action.

task: Grind surfaces with a power grinder, or polish surfaces with polishing or surfacing machines.
rationale: Operating power grinding and polishing machines involves direct physical operation of tools to manipulate surfaces.

task: Position and secure moisture membrane and wire mesh in preparation for pouring base materials for terrazzo installation.
rationale: Positioning and securing physical materials (moisture membrane and wire mesh) requires hands-on manipulation and physical work at the installation site.

task: Modify mixing, grouting, grinding, or cleaning procedures, according to type of installation or material used.
rationale: Task involves modifying procedures for physical operations like mixing, grouting, grinding, and cleaning—all hands-on material handling and tool operation activities.

task: Wash polished terrazzo surface, using cleaner and water, and apply sealer and curing agent according to manufacturer's specifications, using brush or sprayer.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation—washing surfaces with cleaner/water, applying sealer and curing agent using brush or sprayer.

task: Mix cement, sand, and water to produce concrete, grout, or slurry, using hoe, trowel, tamper, scraper, or concrete-mixing machine.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials and operation of tools/machinery (hoe, trowel, tamper, scraper, concrete mixer) to produce concrete products.

task: Sprinkle colored marble or stone chips, powdered steel, or coloring powder over surface to produce prescribed finish.
rationale: Sprinkling materials over a surface requires physical manipulation and hands-on application to achieve a prescribed finish.

task: Wet surface to prepare for bonding, fill holes and cracks with grout or slurry, and smooth with a trowel.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (trowel), materials (grout/slurry), and hands-on surface preparation work.

task: Repair concrete by cutting out damaged areas, drilling holes for reinforcing rods, and positioning reinforcing rods, using power saw and drill.
rationale: Involves physical manipulation and operation of power tools (saw, drill) to repair concrete, requiring hands-on work with equipment and materials.

task: Clean installation site, mixing and storage areas, tools, machines, and equipment, and store materials and equipment.
rationale: Cleaning, mixing, storing, and organizing physical materials, tools, equipment requires direct manual labor and physical manipulation of tangible objects at the work site.

task: Fill slight grinding depressions with matching grout material and hand-trowel for a smooth, uniform surface.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials (grout application) and hand tool operation (troweling) to finish a surface.

task: Chip, scrape, or grind high spots, ridges, or rough projections to finish concrete, using pneumatic chisel, hand chisel, or other hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of hand tools and pneumatic chisels to physically manipulate concrete surfaces.

task: Clean chipped area, using wire brush, and feel and observe surface to determine if it is rough or uneven.
rationale: Task involves physical tool operation (wire brush) and tactile/visual surface inspection requiring hands-on presence.

task: Move terrazzo installation materials, tools, machines, or work devices to work areas, manually or using wheelbarrow.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation and transport of materials and tools to work areas, either by hand or with equipment like wheelbarrows.

task: Build wooden molds, clamping molds around areas to be repaired, or setting up frames to the proper depth and alignment.
rationale: Building and setting up wooden molds involves hands-on physical manipulation of materials, tools, and equipment to construct and position structures for repairs.

task: Signal truck driver to position truck to facilitate pouring concrete and move chute to direct concrete on forms.
rationale: Signaling truck driver and physically repositioning concrete chute are hands-on physical tasks requiring bodily movement and manipulation of equipment.

task: Produce rough concrete surface, using broom.
rationale: Physical manipulation of a tool (broom) to texture concrete surface through repetitive brushing.

task: Precast terrazzo blocks in wooden forms.
rationale: Precasting terrazzo involves physical manipulation of materials, pouring, setting, and removing from forms—hands-on manufacturing work.

task: Spread roofing paper on surface of foundation and spread concrete onto roofing paper with trowel to form terrazzo base.
rationale: Spreading roofing paper and concrete with a trowel involves physical manipulation of materials and operation of a hand tool.

task: Remove frames when the foundation is dry.
rationale: Removing frames requires physical manipulation and hands-on work with tangible objects and tools at a work site.

task: Wet concrete surface and rub with stone to smooth surface and obtain specified finish.
rationale: Wetting and rubbing concrete with stone requires physical manipulation and hands-on work to achieve surface finish.

task: Take actions to avoid potential hazards or obstructions, such as utility lines, other equipment, other workers, or falling objects.
rationale: Requires physical presence at work site to actively monitor, move, and navigate around tangible hazards and obstructions.

task: Adjust handwheels and depress pedals to control attachments, such as blades, buckets, scrapers, or swing booms.
rationale: Operating physical controls (handwheels, pedals) on machinery to manipulate tangible equipment (blades, buckets, scrapers, booms).

task: Start engines, move throttles, switches, or levers, or depress pedals to operate machines, such as bulldozers, trench excavators, road graders, or backhoes.
rationale: Operating heavy machinery requires physical manipulation of controls (throttles, switches, levers, pedals) and direct equipment operation.

task: Align machines, cutterheads, or depth gauge makers with reference stakes and guidelines or ground or position equipment, following hand signals of other workers.
rationale: Requires physical positioning and alignment of machines and equipment, following hand signals—involves hands-on manipulation and physical operation at a work site.

task: Load and move dirt, rocks, equipment, or other materials, using trucks, crawler tractors, power cranes, shovels, graders, or related equipment.
rationale: Physically manipulating materials and operating heavy equipment (trucks, cranes, shovels, graders) requires hands-on manual execution.

task: Drive and maneuver equipment equipped with blades in successive passes over working areas to remove topsoil, vegetation, or rocks or to distribute and level earth or terrain.
rationale: Operating heavy machinery (blade-equipped equipment) to manipulate physical terrain requires hands-on equipment operation and physical presence at work site.

task: Coordinate machine actions with other activities, positioning or moving loads in response to hand or audio signals from crew members.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical operation of machinery and manual positioning/moving of loads using hand/audio signals from crew.

task: Operate tractors or bulldozers to perform such tasks as clearing land, mixing sludge, trimming backfills, or building roadways or parking lots.
rationale: Operating heavy machinery (tractors, bulldozers) to physically manipulate land and materials is primary manual/physical work.

task: Repair and maintain equipment, making emergency adjustments or assisting with major repairs as necessary.
rationale: Repairing and maintaining equipment requires physical manipulation of tools and machinery to diagnose, fix, and adjust mechanical/electrical systems.

task: Check fuel supplies at sites to ensure adequate availability.
rationale: Requires physical inspection at work sites to observe and verify fuel tank levels and quantities.

task: Connect hydraulic hoses, belts, mechanical linkages, or power takeoff shafts to tractors.
rationale: Connecting hydraulic hoses, belts, and mechanical linkages requires physical manipulation and assembly of tangible tractor components.

task: Operate loaders to pull out stumps, rip asphalt or concrete, rough-grade properties, bury refuse, or perform general cleanup.
rationale: Operating loaders involves physical operation of heavy machinery to manipulate the physical environment (stumps, asphalt, concrete, earth).

task: Select and fasten bulldozer blades or other attachments to tractors, using hitches.
rationale: Fastening blades and attachments involves physical manipulation of equipment parts using hitches and tools.

task: Test atmosphere for adequate oxygen or explosive conditions when working in confined spaces.
rationale: Requires physical use of testing equipment and physical presence in confined spaces to conduct atmospheric measurements.

task: Operate compactors, scrapers, or rollers to level, compact, or cover refuse at disposal grounds.
rationale: Operating heavy machinery (compactors, scrapers, rollers) requires hands-on physical control of equipment at a disposal site.

task: Signal operators to guide movement of tractor-drawn machines.
rationale: Signaling operators involves physical gestures/hand signals at a work site to direct movement of heavy machinery, requiring bodily presence and communication through physical action.

task: Operate road watering, oiling, or rolling equipment, or street sealing equipment, such as chip spreaders.
rationale: Operating physical equipment (chip spreaders, rollers, watering machines) requires hands-on control of machinery and vehicles in the field.

task: Push other equipment when extra traction or assistance is required.
rationale: Pushing equipment requires physical force and bodily movement to manipulate objects in the physical world.

task: Drive tractor-trailer trucks to move equipment from site to site.
rationale: Operating a tractor-trailer is physical machinery operation requiring hands-on control and movement of the vehicle.

task: Turn valves to control air or water output of compressors or pumps.
rationale: Operating physical valves to regulate compressor/pump output requires manual physical manipulation of equipment controls.

task: Operate equipment to demolish or remove debris or to remove snow from streets, roads, or parking lots.
rationale: Operating heavy equipment (bulldozers, loaders, snow plows) to demolish, remove debris, or clear snow requires hands-on physical operation of machinery.

task: Apply coats of plaster or stucco to walls, ceilings, or partitions of buildings, using trowels, brushes, or spray guns.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical manipulation of tools (trowels, brushes, spray guns) and application of materials to building surfaces.

task: Mix mortar and plaster to desired consistency or direct workers who perform mixing.
rationale: Mixing mortar and plaster involves physical manipulation of materials to achieve desired consistency. Though directing workers is text-based, the primary execution involves hands-on physical work.

task: Create decorative textures in finish coat, using brushes or trowels, sand, pebbles, or stones.
rationale: Creating decorative textures requires physical manipulation of tools (brushes, trowels) and materials (sand, pebbles, stones) on surfaces.

task: Apply insulation to building exteriors by installing prefabricated insulation systems over existing walls or by covering the outer wall with insulation board, reinforcing mesh, and a base coat.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical manipulation of insulation materials, tools, and fasteners; installation of boards and mesh on building exteriors is hands-on construction work.

task: Cure freshly plastered surfaces.
rationale: Curing plastered surfaces involves physical monitoring and maintenance (moisture application, protecting surfaces) at the work site.

task: Clean and prepare surfaces for applications of plaster, cement, stucco, or similar materials, such as by drywall taping.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and preparation of surfaces using tools and materials for finishing work.

task: Rough the undercoat surface with a scratcher so the finish coat will adhere.
rationale: Physical manipulation using a scratcher tool on an undercoat surface to prepare it for painting.

task: Apply weatherproof, decorative coverings to exterior surfaces of buildings, such as by troweling or spraying on coats of stucco.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of stucco using trowels or spraying equipment on exterior surfaces; hands-on application work.

task: Install guide wires on exterior surfaces of buildings to indicate thickness of plaster or stucco and nail wire mesh, lath, or similar materials to the outside surface to hold stucco in place.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials (guide wires, mesh, lath), use of tools (nailing), and hands-on work on building exteriors.

task: Spray acoustic materials or texture finish over walls or ceilings.
rationale: Spraying materials over walls/ceilings requires operating spray equipment and physical manipulation of surfaces.

task: Mold or install ornamental plaster pieces, panels, or trim.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and installation of materials (plaster pieces, panels, trim) using hand tools and techniques.

task: Inspect derricks, or order their inspection, prior to being raised or lowered.
rationale: Physical inspection of derricks requires hands-on examination of equipment before operation; ordering inspection involves directing work but primary task is hands-on inspection.

task: Inspect derricks for flaws, and clean and oil derricks to maintain proper working conditions.
rationale: Requires physical inspection and hands-on maintenance (cleaning, oiling) of derrick equipment.

task: Control the viscosity and weight of the drilling fluid.
rationale: Controlling drilling fluid properties requires hands-on physical manipulation of materials and operation of drilling equipment systems.

task: Repair pumps, mud tanks, and related equipment.
rationale: Repairing pumps and equipment requires hands-on physical manipulation, tool operation, and direct work with tangible machinery.

task: Set and bolt crown blocks to posts at tops of derricks.
rationale: Setting and bolting crown blocks requires physical manipulation of equipment and tools to attach heavy machinery components to derrick posts.

task: Listen to mud pumps and check regularly for vibration and other problems to ensure that rig pumps and drilling mud systems are working properly.
rationale: Listening to and physically inspecting equipment for vibration requires hands-on presence at the work site to monitor physical systems.

task: Start pumps that circulate mud through drill pipes and boreholes to cool drill bits and flush out drill cuttings.
rationale: Operating physical pumps and machinery to circulate drilling fluid through equipment is direct operation of mechanical systems.

task: Position and align derrick elements, using harnesses and platform climbing devices.
rationale: Positioning and aligning derrick elements requires physical manipulation, climbing using devices, and hands-on work at height with safety equipment.

task: Guide lengths of pipe into and out of elevators.
rationale: Guiding pipe lengths requires physical manipulation and positioning of tangible objects into/out of elevators, involving hands-on physical execution.

task: Clamp holding fixtures on ends of hoisting cables.
rationale: Clamping fixtures onto cables requires hands-on physical manipulation and assembly of tangible objects.

task: Weigh clay, and mix with water and chemicals to make drilling mud, using portable mixers.
rationale: Physically weighing clay, manually mixing materials with portable equipment, and handling chemicals require hands-on physical manipulation and tool operation.

task: String cables through pulleys and blocks.
rationale: Physically threading cables through pulleys and blocks requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects and tools.

task: Steady pipes during connection to or disconnection from drill or casing strings.
rationale: Steadying pipes requires physical manipulation and hands-on work with heavy equipment during drilling operations.

task: Examine blast areas to determine amounts and kinds of explosive charges needed and to ensure that safety laws are observed.
rationale: Examining blast areas requires physical presence on-site to inspect the location and determine explosive requirements based on visual assessment and hands-on evaluation.

task: Tie specified lengths of delaying fuses into patterns in order to time sequences of explosions.
rationale: Tying fuses requires physical manipulation of tangible objects (fuses, knots) to create explosion timing sequences.

task: Place safety cones around blast areas to alert other workers of danger zones, and signal workers as necessary to ensure that they clear blast sites prior to explosions.
rationale: Physical placement of safety cones at blast sites and bodily movement/signaling to direct workers require hands-on physical presence in the work environment.

task: Place explosive charges in holes or other spots; then detonate explosives to demolish structures or to loosen, remove, or displace earth, rock, or other materials.
rationale: Placing explosives and detonating them requires direct physical manipulation of materials and operation of explosive devices at a work site.

task: Insert, pack, and pour explosives, such as dynamite, ammonium nitrate, black powder, or slurries into blast holes; then shovel drill cuttings, admit water into boreholes, and tamp material to compact charges.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of explosives and materials, operating tools, shoveling, and direct hands-on work with tangible objects and equipment at blast sites.

task: Measure depths of drilled blast holes, using weighted tape measures.
rationale: Physical use of weighted tape measure to measure blast holes requires hands-on manipulation of measuring tools and physical presence at the work site.

task: Connect electrical wire to primers, and cover charges or fill blast holes with clay, drill chips, sand, or other material.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of wires, explosives, and materials, plus hands-on preparation of blast holes—inherently manual work.

task: Lay primacord between rows of charged blast holes, and tie cord into main lines to form blast patterns.
rationale: Physically laying cord between blast holes and tying connections requires hands-on manipulation of tangible materials at a work site.

task: Assemble and position equipment, explosives, and blasting caps in holes at specified depths, or load perforating guns or torpedoes with explosives.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of explosives, blasting caps, and equipment at specified locations and depths—hands-on assembly and positioning work.

task: Verify detonation of charges by observing control panels, or by listening for the sounds of blasts.
rationale: Requires physical presence at work site to observe control panels and listen for blast sounds; direct sensory engagement with physical environment.

task: Move and store inventories of explosives, loaded perforating guns, and other materials, according to established safety procedures.
rationale: Task involves physically moving, handling, and storing tangible objects (explosives, perforating guns, materials) at work locations.

task: Light fuses, drop detonating devices into wells or boreholes, or activate firing devices with plungers, dials, or buttons, in order to set off single or multiple blasts.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of detonating devices, fuses, and firing mechanisms to execute blasts at work sites.

task: Drive trucks to transport explosives and blasting equipment to blasting sites.
rationale: Driving trucks is operation of a physical vehicle, a core manual task. Transportation itself is the primary execution.

task: Cut specified lengths of primacord and attach primers to cord ends.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation: cutting primacord to length and attaching primers—hands-on assembly work.

task: Repair and service blasting, shooting, and automotive equipment, and electrical wiring and instruments, using hand tools.
rationale: Repairing equipment with hand tools requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and operation of physical tools.

task: Set up and operate short-wave radio or field telephone equipment to transmit and receive blast information.
rationale: Setting up and operating radio/telephone equipment requires physical manipulation of tangible machinery and hands-on operational work.

task: Insert waterproof sealers, bullets, and/or powder charges into guns, and screw gun ports back into place.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of gun components (inserting sealers, bullets, powder charges) and using tools (screwing ports), requiring hands-on manual work.

task: Clean, gauge, and lubricate gun ports.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and maintenance of firearm components using tools and lubricants.

task: Lower perforating guns into wells, using hoists; then use measuring devices and instrument panels to position guns in correct positions for taking samples.
rationale: Primary execution involves physically lowering equipment using hoists and operating measuring devices to position guns—manual operation of tools and equipment.

task: Set up and operate ground support and test equipment to perform functional flight tests of electrical and electronic systems.
rationale: Operating physical test equipment and machinery requires hands-on setup, configuration, and direct operation of tangible instruments and systems.

task: Test and troubleshoot instruments, components, and assemblies, using circuit testers, oscilloscopes, or voltmeters.
rationale: Requires physical operation of test instruments (circuit testers, oscilloscopes, voltmeters) and hands-on manipulation of components and assemblies.

task: Install electrical and electronic components, assemblies, and systems in aircraft, using hand tools, power tools, or soldering irons.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical manipulation of components using hand tools, power tools, and soldering irons; involves assembly and installation work.

task: Adjust, repair, or replace malfunctioning components or assemblies, using hand tools or soldering irons.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of components and operation of hand tools and soldering irons.

task: Connect components to assemblies such as radio systems, instruments, magnetos, inverters, and in-flight refueling systems, using hand tools and soldering irons.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical manipulation of components, hand tools, and soldering equipment to assemble aircraft systems.

task: Assemble components such as switches, electrical controls, and junction boxes, using hand tools or soldering irons.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical manipulation of electrical components and operation of hand tools and soldering irons.

task: Fabricate parts and test aids as required.
rationale: Fabricating parts requires physical manipulation and operation of tools/machinery; testing aids involves hands-on physical work.

task: Lay out installation of aircraft assemblies and systems, following documentation such as blueprints, manuals, and wiring diagrams.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of aircraft assemblies and systems, installing components per documentation and blueprints.

task: Assemble prototypes or models of circuits, instruments, and systems for use in testing.
rationale: Assembling prototypes requires physical manipulation of components, soldering, wiring, and hands-on construction of circuits and instruments.

task: Inspect completed work to certify that maintenance meets standards and that aircraft are ready for operation.
rationale: Physical inspection of aircraft to examine completed maintenance work requires being present at the work site, touching/examining components, and visually assessing physical systems.

task: Conduct routine and special inspections as required by regulations.
rationale: Inspections require physical presence, observation, and hands-on examination of equipment, facilities, or conditions to assess compliance.

task: Examine and inspect aircraft components, including landing gear, hydraulic systems, and deicers to locate cracks, breaks, leaks, or other problems.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of tangible aircraft components, including touching and visually examining landing gear, hydraulic systems, and deicers for defects.

task: Inspect airframes for wear or other defects.
rationale: Physical inspection requires being present at work site, visually examining and likely touching airframe surfaces to detect wear and defects.

task: Maintain, repair, and rebuild aircraft structures, functional components, and parts, such as wings and fuselage, rigging, hydraulic units, oxygen systems, fuel systems, electrical systems, gaskets, or seals.
rationale: Maintaining, repairing, and rebuilding aircraft structures and components requires hands-on physical manipulation, assembly work, and operation of tools.

task: Measure the tension of control cables.
rationale: Measuring cable tension requires physical handling of cables and use of measurement tools/instruments.

task: Replace or repair worn, defective, or damaged components, using hand tools, gauges, and testing equipment.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical manipulation of components using hand tools, gauges, and testing equipment for replacement and repair work.

task: Measure parts for wear, using precision instruments.
rationale: Physical inspection using precision instruments (calipers, gauges) to measure parts requires hands-on manipulation and direct examination.

task: Assemble and install electrical, plumbing, mechanical, hydraulic, and structural components and accessories, using hand or power tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of components and operation of hand/power tools to assemble and install systems.

task: Test operation of engines and other systems, using test equipment, such as ignition analyzers, compression checkers, distributor timers, or ammeters.
rationale: Operating physical test equipment to examine engines and systems requires hands-on manipulation of tools and machinery to conduct inspections.

task: Obtain fuel and oil samples and check them for contamination.
rationale: Obtaining samples requires physical collection and handling of materials; checking involves hands-on inspection or lab testing of tangible substances.

task: Reassemble engines following repair or inspection and reinstall engines in aircraft.
rationale: Reassembling and reinstalling aircraft engines requires hands-on physical manipulation of complex mechanical components and machinery.

task: Modify aircraft structures, space vehicles, systems, or components, following drawings, schematics, charts, engineering orders, and technical publications.
rationale: Modifying aircraft/space vehicle structures requires physical manipulation and assembly work following technical specifications.

task: Install and align repaired or replacement parts for subsequent riveting or welding, using clamps and wrenches.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of parts, operation of clamps and wrenches, and hands-on alignment work—direct manual execution.

task: Locate and mark dimensions and reference lines on defective or replacement parts, using templates, scribes, compasses, and steel rules.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (scribe, compass, steel rule) and tangible parts to mark dimensions and reference lines.

task: Clean, strip, prime, and sand structural surfaces and materials to prepare them for bonding.
rationale: Involves physical manipulation and preparation of surfaces through cleaning, stripping, sanding, and priming using tools and materials.

task: Service and maintain aircraft and related apparatus by performing activities such as flushing crankcases, cleaning screens, and or moving parts.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of aircraft parts, flushing systems, cleaning components, and moving parts—all hands-on mechanical work.

task: Examine engines through specially designed openings while working from ladders or scaffolds, or use hoists or lifts to remove the entire engine from an aircraft.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of engines from ladders/scaffolds and operation of hoists/lifts to remove engines—direct physical and equipment operation.

task: Remove or install aircraft engines, using hoists or forklift trucks.
rationale: Requires physical operation of hoists/forklifts and hands-on removal or installation of aircraft engines—direct physical manipulation.

task: Fabricate defective sections or parts, using metal fabricating machines, saws, brakes, shears, and grinders.
rationale: Task requires operating metal fabricating machines, saws, brakes, shears, and grinders—physical tools that demand hands-on manipulation and operation.

task: Remove or cut out defective parts or drill holes to gain access to internal defects or damage, using drills and punches.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of tools (drills, punches) and manual manipulation of objects to remove/cut parts.

task: Clean, refuel, and change oil in line service aircraft.
rationale: Cleaning, refueling, and changing oil requires hands-on physical manipulation of aircraft and tools to perform maintenance tasks.

task: Trim and shape replacement body sections to specified sizes and fits and secure sections in place, using adhesives, hand tools, and power tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation (trimming, shaping, securing), hand tools, power tools, and adhesives—all manual operations.

task: Clean engines, sediment bulk and screens, and carburetors, adjusting carburetor float levels.
rationale: Cleaning engines, sediment, screens, and carburetors; adjusting float levels requires hands-on physical manipulation and operation of tools.

task: Prepare and paint aircraft surfaces.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of surfaces and operation of painting tools and equipment on aircraft.

task: Spread plastic film over areas to be repaired to prevent damage to surrounding areas.
rationale: Physical manipulation of plastic film to cover work areas requires hands-on placement and arrangement of tangible materials.

task: Check for corrosion, distortion, and invisible cracks in the fuselage, wings, and tail, using x-ray and magnetic inspection equipment.
rationale: Physical operation of specialized inspection equipment (X-ray, magnetic devices) on aircraft components requires hands-on equipment use and physical presence at work site.

task: Disassemble engines and inspect parts, such as turbine blades or cylinders, for corrosion, wear, warping, cracks, and leaks, using precision measuring instruments, x-rays, and magnetic inspection equipment.
rationale: Primary execution involves physical disassembly of engine parts and hands-on inspection using specialized equipment.

task: Cure bonded structures, using portable or stationary curing equipment.
rationale: Curing bonded structures requires operating curing equipment and physically managing materials/structures during the curing process.

task: Accompany aircraft on flights to make in-flight adjustments and corrections.
rationale: Task involves physical presence on aircraft and hands-on mechanical adjustments/corrections during flight operations.

task: Remove, inspect, repair, and install in-flight refueling stores and external fuel tanks.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and assembly of aircraft fuel systems—removing, inspecting, repairing, and installing fuel tanks involves hands-on work with aircraft equipment.

task: Repair or replace defective or worn parts such as bearings, pistons, and gears, using hand tools, torque wrenches, power tools, and welding equipment.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of parts and operation of hand tools, power tools, and welding equipment to repair machinery.

task: Test units for operability before and after repairs.
rationale: Testing units requires physical operation and inspection of equipment to verify functionality before/after repairs.

task: Remove locomotives, car mechanical units, or other components, using pneumatic hoists and jacks, pinch bars, hand tools, and cutting torches.
rationale: Task requires physical operation of pneumatic hoists, jacks, hand tools, and cutting torches to remove heavy components—primarily manual/physical work.

task: Inspect components such as bearings, seals, gaskets, wheels, and coupler assemblies to determine if repairs are needed.
rationale: Physical inspection of tangible components requires hands-on examination, touching, and visual assessment of specific parts to detect wear or damage.

task: Inspect the interior and exterior of rail cars coming into rail yards to identify defects and to determine the extent of wear and damage.
rationale: Physical inspection of rail cars requires being present at the yard, visually examining and potentially touching/testing components to assess defects and damage.

task: Adjust repaired or replaced units as needed to ensure proper operation.
rationale: Adjusting repaired/replaced units requires hands-on physical manipulation and testing of equipment to verify proper operation.

task: Perform scheduled maintenance, and clean units and components.
rationale: Maintenance and cleaning require physical manipulation of equipment and components, along with hands-on work at the job site.

task: Repair, fabricate, and install steel or wood fittings, using blueprints, shop sketches, and instruction manuals.
rationale: Primary execution involves fabricating, installing, and physically manipulating steel/wood fittings—requires hands-on construction work and tool operation.

task: Repair and maintain electrical and electronic controls for propulsion and braking systems.
rationale: Repairing and maintaining electrical/electronic controls requires hands-on physical manipulation of equipment, tools, and components to diagnose and fix systems.

task: Disassemble units such as water pumps, control valves, and compressors so that repairs can be made.
rationale: Disassembling physical equipment requires hands-on manipulation of tangible mechanical components using tools.

task: Measure diameters of axle wheel seats, using micrometers, and mark dimensions on axles so that wheels can be bored to specified dimensions.
rationale: Task involves physical tool operation (micrometers), hands-on measurement of tangible objects (axles), and marking dimensions—all requiring manual execution and physical presence at work site.

task: Align car sides for installation of car ends and crossties, using width gauges, turnbuckles, and wrenches.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of car sides using width gauges, turnbuckles, and wrenches—hands-on tool operation and assembly work.

task: Replace defective wiring and insulation, and tighten electrical connections, using hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation using hand tools to replace wiring and insulation, tighten connections—direct hands-on electrical repair work.

task: Test electrical systems of cars by operating systems and using testing equipment such as ammeters.
rationale: Task involves operating testing equipment (ammeters) and hands-on testing of car electrical systems, requiring physical manipulation and operation of tools.

task: Install and repair interior flooring, fixtures, walls, plumbing, steps, and platforms.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation and operation of tools to install/repair flooring, fixtures, plumbing, and other interior structures.
